member.name: M:SDL3.Mixer.SetTrackPlaybackPosition(System.IntPtr,System.Int64)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL MIX_SetTrackPlaybackPosition(MIX_Track *track, Sint64 frames);
member.summary:
Seek a playing track to a new position in its input.
(Not to be confused with , which is positioning of the track in 3D space, not the playback position of its audio data.)
On a playing track, the next time the mixer runs, it will start mixing from the new position.
Position is defined in _sample frames_ of decoded audio, not units of time, so that sample-perfect mixing can be achieved. To instead operate in units of time, use to get the approximate sample frames for a given tick.
This function requires an input that can seek (so it can not be used if the input was set with ), and a audio file format that allows seeking. SDL_mixer's decoders for some file formats do not offer seeking, or can only seek to times, not exact sample frames, in which case the final position may be off by some amount of sample frames. Please check your audio data and file bug reports if appropriate.
It's legal to call this function on a track that is stopped, but a future call to will reset the start position anyhow. Paused tracks will resume at the new input position.
member.param: the track to change.
the sample frame position to seek to.
member.returns: true on success, false on error; call for details.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_mixer 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.Mixer.GetTrackFadeFrames(System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC Sint64 SDLCALL MIX_GetTrackFadeFrames(MIX_Track *track);
member.summary:
Query whether a given track is fading.
This specifically checks if the track is _not stopped_ (paused or playing), and it is fading in or out, and returns the number of frames remaining in the fade.
If fading out, the returned value will be negative. When fading in, the returned value will be positive. If not fading, this function returns zero.
On various errors ( was not called, the track is NULL), this returns 0, but there is no mechanism to distinguish errors from tracks that aren't fading.
member.param: the track to query.
member.returns: less than 0 if the track is fading out, greater than 0 if fading in, zero otherwise.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_mixer 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.Mixer.GetTrackPlaybackPosition(System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC Sint64 SDLCALL MIX_GetTrackPlaybackPosition(MIX_Track *track);
member.summary:
Get the current input position of a playing track.
(Not to be confused with , which is positioning of the track in 3D space, not the playback position of its audio data.)
Position is defined in _sample frames_ of decoded audio, not units of time, so that sample-perfect mixing can be achieved. To instead operate in units of time, use to convert the return value to milliseconds.
Stopped and paused tracks will report the position when they halted. Playing tracks will report the current position, which will change over time.
member.param: the track to change.
member.returns: the track's current sample frame position, or -1 on error; call for details.

member.name: M:SDL3.Mixer.GetTrackLoops(System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC int SDLCALL MIX_GetTrackLoops(MIX_Track *track);
member.summary:
Query how many loops remain for a given track.
This returns the number of loops still pending; if a track will eventually complete and loop to play again one more time, this will return 1. If a track _was_ looping but is on its final iteration of the loop (will stop when this iteration completes), this will return zero.
On various errors ( was not called, the track is null), this returns false, but there is no mechanism to distinguish errors from non-looping tracks.
A track that is looping infinitely will return -1. This value does not report an error in this case.
A track that is stopped (not playing and not paused) will have zero loops remaining.
On various errors was not called, the track is null), this returns zero, but there is no mechanism to distinguish errors from non-looping tracks.
member.param: the track to query.
member.returns: the number of pending loops, zero if not looping, and -1 if looping infinitely.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_mixer 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.Mixer.SetTrackLoops(System.IntPtr,System.Int32)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL MIX_SetTrackLoops(MIX_Track *track, int num_loops);
member.summary:
Change the number of times a currently-playing track will loop.
This replaces any previously-set remaining loops. A value of 1 will loop to the start of playback one time. Zero will not loop at all. A value of -1 requests infinite loops. If the input is not seekable and numLoops isn't zero, this function will report success but the track will stop at the point it should loop.
The new loop count replaces any previous state, even if the track has already looped.
This has no effect on a track that is stopped, or rather, starting a stopped track later will set a new loop count, replacing this value. Stopped tracks can specify a loop count while starting via . This function is intended to alter that count in the middle of playback.
member.param: the track to configure.
new number of times to loop. Zero to disable looping, -1 to loop infinitely.
member.returns: true on success, false on error; call for details.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_mixer 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.Mixer.GetTrackAudio(System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC MIX_Audio * SDLCALL MIX_GetTrackAudio(MIX_Track *track);
member.summary:
Query the MIX_Audio assigned to a track.
This returns the MIX_Audio object currently assigned to null through a call to . If there is none assigned, or the track has an input that isn't a MIX_Audio (such as an SDL_AudioStream or SDL_IOStream), this will return null.
On various errors ( was not called, the track is null), this returns null, but there is no mechanism to distinguish errors from tracks without a valid input.
member.param: the track to query.
member.returns: a MIX_Audio if available, if not.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_mixer 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.Mixer.GetTrackAudioStream(System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_AudioStream * SDLCALL MIX_GetTrackAudioStream(MIX_Track *track);
member.summary:
Query the SDL_AudioStream assigned to a track.
This returns the SDL_AudioStream object currently assigned to track through a call to . If there is none assigned, or the track has an input that isn't an SDL_AudioStream (such as a MIX_Audio or SDL_IOStream), this will return null.
On various errors ( was not called, the track is NULL), this returns null, but there is no mechanism to distinguish errors from tracks without a valid input.
member.param: the track to query.
member.returns: an SDL_AudioStream if available, if not.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_mixer 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.Mixer.GetTrackRemaining(System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC Sint64 SDLCALL MIX_GetTrackRemaining(MIX_Track *track);
member.summary:
Return the number of sample frames remaining to be mixed in a track.
If the track is playing or paused, and its total duration is known, this will report how much audio is left to mix. If the track is playing, future calls to this function will report different values.
Remaining audio is defined in _sample frames_ of decoded audio, not units of time, so that sample-perfect mixing can be achieved. To instead operate in units of time, use to convert the return value to milliseconds.
This function does not take into account fade-outs or looping, just the current mixing position vs the duration of the track.
If the duration of the track isn't known, or track is null, this function returns -1. A stopped track reports 0.
member.param: the track to query.
member.returns: the total sample frames still to be mixed, or -1 if unknown.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_mixer 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.Mixer.TrackMSToFrames(System.IntPtr,System.Int64)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC Sint64 SDLCALL MIX_TrackMSToFrames(MIX_Track *track, Sint64 ms);
member.summary:
Convert milliseconds to sample frames for a track's current format.
This calculates time based on the track's current input format, which can change when its input does, and also if that input changes formats mid-stream (for example, if decoding a file that is two MP3s concatenated together).
On various errors ( was not called, the track is null), this returns -1. If the track has no input, this returns -1. If `ms` is < 0, this returns -1.
member.param: the track to query.
the milliseconds to convert to track-specific sample frames.
member.returns: Converted number of sample frames, or -1 for errors/no input; call for details.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_mixer 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.Mixer.TrackFramesToMS(System.IntPtr,System.Int64)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC Sint64 SDLCALL MIX_TrackFramesToMS(MIX_Track *track, Sint64 frames);
member.summary:
Convert sample frames for a track's current format to milliseconds.
This calculates time based on the track's current input format, which can change when its input does, and also if that input changes formats mid-stream (for example, if decoding a file that is two MP3s concatenated together).
Sample frames are more precise than milliseconds, so out of necessity, this function will approximate by rounding down to the closest full millisecond.
On various errors ( was not called, the track is null), this returns -1. If the track has no input, this returns -1. If frames is < 0, this returns -1.
member.param: the track to query.
the track-specific sample frames to convert to milliseconds.
member.returns: Converted number of milliseconds, or -1 for errors/no input; call for details.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_mixer 3.0.0.

member.summary:
Convert milliseconds to sample frames for a MIX_Audio's format.
This calculates time based on the audio's initial format, even if the format would change mid-stream.
If ms is < 0, this returns -1.
member.param: the audio to query.
the milliseconds to convert to audio-specific sample frames.
member.returns: Converted number of sample frames, or -1 for errors/no input; call for details.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_mixer 3.0.0.

member.summary:
Convert sample frames for a MIX_Audio's format to milliseconds.
This calculates time based on the audio's initial format, even if the format would change mid-stream.
Sample frames are more precise than milliseconds, so out of necessity, this function will approximate by rounding down to the closest full millisecond.
If frames is < 0, this returns -1.
member.param: the audio to query.
the audio-specific sample frames to convert to milliseconds.
member.returns: Converted number of milliseconds, or -1 for errors/no input; call for details.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_mixer 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.Mixer.PlayTrack(System.IntPtr,System.UInt32)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL MIX_PlayTrack(MIX_Track *track, SDL_PropertiesID options);
member.summary:
Start (or restart) mixing a track for playback.
The track will use whatever input was last assigned to it when playing; an input must be assigned to this track or this function will fail. Inputs are assigned with calls to , , or .
If the track is already playing, or paused, this will restart the track with the newly-specified parameters.
As there are several parameters, and more may be added in the future, they are specified with an SDL_PropertiesID. The parameters have reasonable defaults, and specifying a 0 for options will choose defaults for everything.
SDL_PropertiesID are discussed in [SDL's documentation](https://wiki.libsdl.org/SDL3/CategoryProperties) .
These are the supported properties:
: The number of times to loop the track when it reaches the end. A value of 1 will loop to the start one time. Zero will not loop at all. A value of -1 requests infinite loops. If the input is not seekable and this value isn't zero, this function will report success but the track will stop at the point it should loop. Default 0.
: Mix at most to this sample frame position in the track. This will be treated as if the input reach EOF at this point in the audio file. If -1, mix all available audio without a limit. Default -1.
: The same as using the property, but the value is specified in milliseconds instead of sample frames. If both properties are specified, the sample frames value is favored. Default -1.
: Start mixing from this sample frame position in the track's input. A value <= 0 will begin from the start of the track's input. If the input is not seekable and this value is > 0, this function will report failure. Default 0.
: The same as using the property, but the value is specified in milliseconds instead of sample frames. If both properties are specified, the sample frames value is favored. Default 0.
``: If the track is looping, this is the sample frame position that the track will loop back to; this lets one play an intro at the start of a track on the first iteration, but have a loop point somewhere in the middle thereafter. A value <= 0 will begin the loop from the start of the track's input. Default 0.
: The same as using the property, but the value is specified in milliseconds instead of sample frames. If both properties are specified, the sample frames value is favored. Default 0.
: The number of sample frames over which to fade in the newly-started track. The track will begin mixing silence and reach full volume smoothly over this many sample frames. If the track loops before the fade-in is complete, it will continue to fade correctly from the loop point. A value <= 0 will disable fade-in, so the track starts mixing at full volume. Default 0.
: The same as using the property, but the value is specified in milliseconds instead of sample frames. If both properties are specified, the sample frames value is favored. Default 0.
: If fading in, start fading from this volume level. 0.0f is silence and 1.0f is full volume, every in between is a linear change in gain. The specified value will be clamped between 0.0f and 1.0f. Default 0.0f.
: At the end of mixing this track, after all loops are complete, append this many sample frames of silence as if it were part of the audio file. This allows for apps to implement effects in callbacks, like reverb, that need to generate samples past the end of the stream's audio, or perhaps introduce a delay before starting a new sound on the track without having to manage it directly. A value <= 0 generates no silence before stopping the track. Default 0.
``: The same as using the property, but the value is specified in milliseconds instead of sample frames. If both properties are specified, the sample frames value is favored. Default 0.
If this function fails, mixing of this track will not start (or restart, if it was already started).
member.param: the track to start (or restart) mixing.
a set of properties that control playback. May be zero.
member.returns: true on success, false on error; call for details.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_mixer 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.Mixer.PlayTag(System.IntPtr,System.String,System.UInt32)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL MIX_PlayTag(MIX_Mixer *mixer, const char *tag, SDL_PropertiesID options);
member.summary:
Start (or restart) mixing all tracks with a specific tag for playback.
This function follows all the same rules as ; please refer to its documentation for the details. Unlike that function, operates on multiple tracks at once that have the specified tag applied, via .
If all of your tagged tracks have different sample rates, it would make sense to use the *MillisecondsNumber properties in your options, instead of *FramesNumber, and let SDL_mixer figure out how to apply it to each track.
This function returns true if all tagged tracks are started (or restarted). If any track fails, this function returns false, but all tracks that could start will still be started even when this function reports failure.
From the point of view of the mixing process, all tracks that successfully (re)start will do so at the exact same moment.
member.param: the mixer on which to look for tagged tracks.
the tag to use when searching for tracks.
the set of options that will be applied to each track.
member.returns: true on success, false on error; call for details.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_mixer 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.Mixer.PlayAudio(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL MIX_PlayAudio(MIX_Mixer *mixer, MIX_Audio *audio);
member.summary:
Play a MIX_Audio from start to finish without any management.
This is what we term a "fire-and-forget" sound. Internally, SDL_mixer will manage a temporary track to mix the specified MIX_Audio, cleaning it up when complete. No options can be provided for how to do the mixing, like offers, and since the track is not available to the caller, no adjustments can be made to mixing over time.
This is not the function to build an entire game of any complexity around, but it can be convenient to play simple, one-off sounds that can't be stopped early. An example would be a voice saying "GAME OVER" during an unpausable endgame sequence.
There are no limits to the number of fire-and-forget sounds that can mix at once (short of running out of memory), and SDL_mixer keeps an internal pool of temporary tracks it creates as needed and reuses when available.
member.param: the mixer on which to play this audio.
the audio input to play.
member.returns: true if the track has begun mixing, false on error; call for details.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_mixer 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.Mixer.StopTrack(System.IntPtr,System.Int64)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL MIX_StopTrack(MIX_Track *track, Sint64 fade_out_frames);
member.summary:
Halt a currently-playing track, possibly fading out over time.
If fadeOutFrames is > 0, the track does not stop mixing immediately, but rather fades to silence over that many sample frames before stopping. Sample frames are specific to the input assigned to the track, to allow for sample-perfect mixing. can be used to convert milliseconds to an appropriate value here.
If the track ends normally while the fade-out is still in progress, the audio stops there; the fade is not adjusted to be shorter if it will last longer than the audio remaining.
Once a track has completed any fadeout and come to a stop, it will call its , if any. It is legal to assign the track a new input and/or restart it during this callback.
It is legal to halt a track that's already stopped. It does nothing, and returns true.
member.param: the track to halt.
the number of sample frames to spend fading out to silence before halting. 0 to stop immediately.
member.returns: true if the track has stopped, false on error; call for details.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_mixer 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.Mixer.StopAllTracks(System.IntPtr,System.Int64)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL MIX_StopAllTracks(MIX_Mixer *mixer, Sint64 fade_out_ms);
member.summary:
Halt all currently-playing tracks, possibly fading out over time.
If fadeOutMs is > 0, the tracks do not stop mixing immediately, but rather fades to silence over that many milliseconds before stopping. Note that this is different than , which wants sample frames; this function takes milliseconds because different tracks might have different sample rates.
If a track ends normally while the fade-out is still in progress, the audio stops there; the fade is not adjusted to be shorter if it will last longer than the audio remaining.
Once a track has completed any fadeout and come to a stop, it will call its , if any. It is legal to assign the track a new input and/or restart it during this callback. This function does not prevent new play requests from being made.
member.param: the mixer on which to stop all tracks.
the number of milliseconds to spend fading out to silence before halting. 0 to stop immediately.
member.returns: true on success, false on error; call for details.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_mixer 3.0.0.

member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL MIX_StopTag(MIX_Mixer *mixer, const char *tag, Sint64 fade_out_ms);
member.summary:
Halt all tracks with a specific tag, possibly fading out over time.
If fadeOutMs is > 0, the tracks do not stop mixing immediately, but rather fades to silence over that many milliseconds before stopping. Note that this is different than , which wants sample frames; this function takes milliseconds because different tracks might have different sample rates.
If a track ends normally while the fade-out is still in progress, the audio stops there; the fade is not adjusted to be shorter if it will last longer than the audio remaining.
Once a track has completed any fadeout and come to a stop, it will call its , if any. It is legal to assign the track a new input and/or restart it during this callback. This function does not prevent new play requests from being made.
member.param: the mixer on which to stop tracks.
the tag to use when searching for tracks.
the number of milliseconds to spend fading out to silence before halting. 0 to stop immediately.
member.returns: true on success, false on error; call for details.

member.since: This function is available since SDL_mixer 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.Mixer.PauseTrack(System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL MIX_PauseTrack(MIX_Track *track);
member.summary:
Pause a currently-playing track.
A paused track is not considered "stopped," so its MIX_TrackStoppedCallback will not fire if paused, but it won't change state by default, generate audio, or generally make progress, until it is resumed.
It is legal to pause a track that's in any state (playing, already paused, or stopped). Unless the track is currently playing, pausing does nothing, and returns true. A false return is only used to signal errors here (such as not being called or track being null).
member.param: the track to pause.
member.returns: true if the track has paused, false on error; call for details.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_mixer 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.Mixer.PauseAllTracks(System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL MIX_PauseAllTracks(MIX_Mixer *mixer);
member.summary:
Pause all currently-playing tracks.
A paused track is not considered "stopped," so its will not fire if paused, but it won't change state by default, generate audio, or generally make progress, until it is resumed.
This function makes all tracks on the specified mixer that are currently playing move to a paused state. They can later be resumed.
member.param: the mixer on which to pause all tracks.
member.returns: true on success, false on error; call for details.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_mixer 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.Mixer.PauseTag(System.IntPtr,System.String)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL MIX_PauseTag(MIX_Mixer *mixer, const char *tag);
member.summary:
Pause all tracks with a specific tag.
A paused track is not considered "stopped," so its will not fire if paused, but it won't change state by default, generate audio, or generally make progress, until it is resumed.
This function makes all currently-playing tracks on the specified mixer, with a specific tag, move to a paused state. They can later be resumed.
Tracks that match the specified tag that aren't currently playing are ignored.
member.param: the mixer on which to pause tracks.
the tag to use when searching for tracks.
member.returns: true on success, false on error; call for details.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_mixer 3.0.0.

member.summary:
Resume a currently-paused track.
A paused track is not considered "stopped," so its will not fire if paused, but it won't change state by default, generate audio, or generally make progress, until it is resumed.
It is legal to resume a track that's in any state (playing, paused, or stopped). Unless the track is currently paused, resuming does nothing, and returns true. A false return is only used to signal errors here (such as not being called or track being null).

member.returns: true if the track has resumed, false on error; call for details.

member.name: M:SDL3.Mixer.TrackPlaying(System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL MIX_TrackPlaying(MIX_Track *track);
member.summary:
Query if a track is currently playing.
If this returns true, the track is currently contributing to the mixer's output (it's "playing"). It is not stopped nor paused.
On various errors (MIX_Init() was not called, the track is NULL), this returns false, but there is no mechanism to distinguish errors from non-playing tracks.
member.param: the track to query.
member.returns: true if playing, false otherwise.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_mixer 3.0.0.

member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL MIX_TrackPaused(MIX_Track *track);
member.summary:
Query if a track is currently paused.
If this returns true, the track is not currently contributing to the mixer's output but will when resumed (it's "paused"). It is not playing nor stopped.
On various errors ( was not called, the track is null), this returns false, but there is no mechanism to distinguish errors from non-playing tracks.
member.param: the track to query.
member.returns: true if paused, false otherwise.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_mixer 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.Mixer.SetMixerGain(System.IntPtr,System.Single)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL MIX_SetMixerGain(MIX_Mixer *mixer, float gain);
member.summary:
Set a mixer's master gain control.
Each mixer has a master gain, to adjust the volume of the entire mix. Each sample passing through the pipeline is modulated by this gain value. A gain of zero will generate silence, 1.0f will not change the mixed volume, and larger than 1.0f will increase the volume. Negative values are illegal. There is no maximum gain specified, but this can quickly get extremely loud, so please be careful with this setting.
A mixer's master gain defaults to 1.0f.
This value can be changed at any time to adjust the future mix.
member.param: the mixer to adjust.
the new gain value.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_mixer 3.0.0.

member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC float SDLCALL MIX_GetMixerGain(MIX_Mixer *mixer);
member.summary:
Get a mixer's master gain control.
This returns the last value set through , or 1.0f if no value has ever been explicitly set.
member.param: the mixer to query.
member.returns: the mixer's current master gain.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_mixer 3.0.0.

Set a track's gain control.
Each track has its own gain, to adjust its overall volume. Each sample from this track is modulated by this gain value. A gain of zero will generate silence, 1.0f will not change the mixed volume, and larger than 1.0f will increase the volume. Negative values are illegal. There is no maximum gain specified, but this can quickly get extremely loud, so please be careful with this setting.
A track's gain defaults to 1.0f.
This value can be changed at any time to adjust the future mix.
member.param: the track to adjust.
the new gain value.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.

member.param: the track to query.

member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.

Set the gain control of all tracks with a specific tag.
Each track has its own gain, to adjust its overall volume. Each sample from this track is modulated by this gain value. A gain of zero will generate silence, 1.0f will not change the mixed volume, and larger than 1.0f will increase the volume. Negative values are illegal. There is no maximum gain specified, but this can quickly get extremely loud, so please be careful with this setting.
A track's gain defaults to 1.0f.
This will change the gain control on tracks on the specified mixer that have the specified tag.
From the point of view of the mixing process, all tracks that successfully change gain values will do so at the exact same moment.
This value can be changed at any time to adjust the future mix.
member.param: the mixer on which to look for tagged tracks.

the new gain value.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.

member.name: M:SDL3.Mixer.SetMixerFrequencyRatio(System.IntPtr,System.Single)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL MIX_SetMixerFrequencyRatio(MIX_Mixer *mixer, float ratio);
member.summary:
Set a mixer's master frequency ratio.
Each mixer has a master frequency ratio, that affects the entire mix. This can cause the final output to change speed and pitch. A value greater than 1.0f will play the audio faster, and at a higher pitch. A value less than 1.0f will play the audio slower, and at a lower pitch. 1.0f is normal speed.
Each track _also_ has a frequency ratio; it will be applied when mixing that track's audio regardless of the master setting. The master setting affects the final output after all mixing has been completed.
A mixer's master frequency ratio defaults to 1.0f.
This value can be changed at any time to adjust the future mix.
member.param: the mixer to adjust.
the frequency ratio. Must be between 0.01f and 100.0f.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_mixer 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.Mixer.GetMixerFrequencyRatio(System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC float SDLCALL MIX_GetMixerFrequencyRatio(MIX_Mixer *mixer);
member.summary:
Get a mixer's master frequency ratio.
This returns the last value set through , or 1.0f if no value has ever been explicitly set.
member.param: the mixer to query.
member.returns: the mixer's current master frequency ratio.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_mixer 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.Mixer.SetTrackFrequencyRatio(System.IntPtr,System.Single)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL MIX_SetTrackFrequencyRatio(MIX_Track *track, float ratio);
member.summary:
Change the frequency ratio of a track.
The frequency ratio is used to adjust the rate at which audio data is consumed. Changing this effectively modifies the speed and pitch of the track's audio. A value greater than 1.0f will play the audio faster, and at a higher pitch. A value less than 1.0f will play the audio slower, and at a lower pitch. 1.0f is normal speed.
The default value is 1.0f.
This value can be changed at any time to adjust the future mix.
member.param: the track on which to change the frequency ratio.
the frequency ratio. Must be between 0.01f and 100.0f.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_mixer 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.Mixer.GetTrackFrequencyRatio(System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC float SDLCALL MIX_GetTrackFrequencyRatio(MIX_Track *track);
member.summary:
Query the frequency ratio of a track.
The frequency ratio is used to adjust the rate at which audio data is consumed. Changing this effectively modifies the speed and pitch of the track's audio. A value greater than 1.0f will play the audio faster, and at a higher pitch. A value less than 1.0f will play the audio slower, and at a lower pitch. 1.0f is normal speed.
The default value is 1.0f.
On various errors ( was not called, the track is null), this returns 0.0f. Since this is not a valid value to set, this can be seen as an error state.
member.param: the track on which to query the frequency ratio.
member.returns: the current frequency ratio, or 0.0f on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_mixer 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.Mixer.SetTrackOutputChannelMap(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr,System.Int32)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL MIX_SetTrackOutputChannelMap(MIX_Track *track, const int *chmap, int count);
member.summary:
Set the current output channel map of a track.
Channel maps are optional; most things do not need them, instead passing data in the order that SDL expects.
The output channel map reorders track data after transformations and before it is mixed into a mixer group. This can be useful for reversing stereo channels, for example.
Each item in the array represents an input channel, and its value is the channel that it should be remapped to. To reverse a stereo signal's left and right values, you'd have an array of { 1, 0 }. It is legal to remap multiple channels to the same thing, so { 1, 1 } would duplicate the right channel to both channels of a stereo signal. An element in the channel map set to -1 instead of a valid channel will mute that channel, setting it to a silence value.
You cannot change the number of channels through a channel map, just reorder/mute them.
Tracks default to no remapping applied. Passing a null channel map is legal, and turns off remapping.
SDL_mixer will copy the channel map; the caller does not have to save this array after this call.
member.param: the track to change.
the new channel map, null to reset to default.
The number of channels in the map.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_mixer 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.Mixer.SetTrackStereo(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL MIX_SetTrackStereo(MIX_Track *track, const MIX_StereoGains *gains);
member.summary:
Force a track to stereo output, with optionally left/right panning.
This will cause the output of the track to convert to stereo, and then mix it only onto the Front Left and Front Right speakers, regardless of the speaker configuration. The left and right channels are modulated by gains, which can be used to produce panning effects. This function may be called to adjust the gains at any time.
If gains is not null, this track will be switched into forced-stereo mode. If gains is null, this will disable spatialization (both the forced-stereo mode of this function and full 3D spatialization of ).
Negative gains are clamped to zero; there is no clamp for maximum, so one could set the value > 1.0f to make a channel louder.
The track's 3D position, reported by , will be reset to (0, 0, 0).
member.param: the track to adjust.
the per-channel gains, or null to disable spatialization.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_mixer 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.Mixer.SetTrack3DPosition(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL MIX_SetTrack3DPosition(MIX_Track *track, const MIX_Point3D *position);
member.summary:
Set a track's position in 3D space.
(Please note that SDL_mixer is not intended to be a extremely powerful 3D API. It lacks 3D features that other APIs like OpenAL offer: there's no doppler effect, distance models, rolloff, etc. This is meant to be Good Enough for games that can use some positional sounds and can even take advantage of surround-sound configurations.)
If position is not null, this track will be switched into 3D positional mode. If position is null, this will disable positional mixing (both the full 3D spatialization of this function and forced-stereo mode of ).
In 3D positional mode, SDL_mixer will mix this track as if it were positioned in 3D space, including distance attenuation (quieter as it gets further from the listener) and spatialization (positioned on the correct speakers to suggest direction, either with stereo outputs or full surround sound).
For a mono speaker output, spatialization is effectively disabled but distance attenuation will still work, which is all you can really do with a single speaker.
The coordinate system operates like OpenGL or OpenAL: a "right-handed" coordinate system. See for the details.
The listener is always at coordinate (0,0,0) and can't be changed.
The track's input will be converted to mono (1 channel) so it can be rendered across the correct speakers.
member.param: the track for which to set 3D position.
the new 3D position for the track. May be null.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_mixer 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.Mixer.GetTrack3DPosition(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL MIX_GetTrack3DPosition(MIX_Track *track, MIX_Point3D *position);
member.summary:
Get a track's current position in 3D space.
If 3D positioning isn't enabled for this track, through a call to , this will return (0,0,0).
member.param: the track to query.
on successful return, will contain the track's position.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_mixer 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.Mixer.CreateGroup(System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC MIX_Group * SDLCALL MIX_CreateGroup(MIX_Mixer *mixer);

summary.para: Create a mixing group.

Tracks are assigned to a mixing group (or if unassigned, they live in a mixer's internal default group). All tracks in a group are mixed together and the app can access this mixed data before it is mixed with all other groups to produce the final output.
This can be a useful feature, but is completely optional; apps can ignore mixing groups entirely and still have a full experience with SDL_mixer.
After creating a group, assign tracks to it with . Use to access the group's mixed data.
A mixing group can be destroyed with when no longer needed. Destroying the mixer will also destroy all its still-existing mixing groups.

member.param: the mixer on which to create a mixing group.
member.returns: a newly-created mixing group, or null on error; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_mixer 3.0.0.

member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC void SDLCALL MIX_DestroyGroup(MIX_Group *group);
member.summary:
Destroy a mixing group.
Any tracks currently assigned to this group will be reassigned to the mixer's internal default group.
member.param: the mixing group to destroy.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_mixer 3.0.0.

Get the properties associated with a group.
Currently SDL_mixer assigns no properties of its own to a group, but this can be a convenient place to store app-specific data.
A SDL_PropertiesID is created the first time this function is called for a given group.
member.param: the group to query.
member.returns: a valid property ID on success or 0 on failure; call for more information.

member.since: This function is available since SDL_mixer 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.Mixer.SetTrackGroup(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL MIX_SetTrackGroup(MIX_Track *track, MIX_Group *group);
member.summary:
Assign a track to a mixing group.
All tracks in a group are mixed together, and that output is made available to the app before it is mixed into the final output.
Tracks can only be in one group at a time, and the track and group must have been created on the same MIX_Mixer.
Setting a track to a null group will remove it from any app-created groups, and reassign it to the mixer's internal default group.
member.param: the track to set mixing group assignment.
the new mixing group to assign to. May be null
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_mixer 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.Mixer.SetTrackStoppedCallback(System.IntPtr,SDL3.Mixer.TrackStoppedCallback,System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL MIX_SetTrackStoppedCallback(MIX_Track *track, MIX_TrackStoppedCallback cb, void *userdata);
member.summary:
Set a callback that fires when a MIX_Track is stopped.
When a track completes playback, either because it ran out of data to mix (and all loops were completed as well), or it was explicitly stopped by the app, it will fire the callback specified here.
Each track has its own unique callback.
Passing a null callback here is legal; it disables this track's callback.
Pausing a track will not fire the callback, nor will the callback fire on a playing track that is being destroyed.
It is legal to adjust the track, including changing its input and restarting it. If this is done because it ran out of data in the middle of mixing, the mixer will start mixing the new track state in its current run without any gap in the audio.
member.param: the track to assign this callback to.
the function to call when the track stops. May be null.
an opaque pointer provided to the callback for its own personal use.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_mixer 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.Mixer.SetTrackCookedCallback(System.IntPtr,SDL3.Mixer.TrackStoppedCallback,System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL MIX_SetTrackCookedCallback(MIX_Track *track, MIX_TrackMixCallback cb, void *userdata);

summary.para: Set a callback that fires when the mixer has transformed a track's audio.

As a track needs to mix more data, it pulls from its input (a MIX_Audio, an SDL_AudioStream, etc). This input might be a compressed file format, like MP3, so a little more data is uncompressed from it.
Once the track has PCM data to start operating on, and its raw callback has completed, it will begin to transform the audio: gain, fading, frequency ratio, 3D positioning, etc.

A callback can be fired after all these transformations, but before the transformed data is mixed into other tracks. This lets an app view the data at the last moment that it is still a part of this track. It can also change the data in any way it pleases during this callback, and the mixer will continue as if this data came directly from the input.
Each track has its own unique cooked callback.
Passing a null callback here is legal; it disables this track's callback.

member.param: the track to assign this callback to.
the function to call when the track mixes. May be null.
an opaque pointer provided to the callback for its own personal use.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_mixer 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.Mixer.SetGroupPostMixCallback(System.IntPtr,SDL3.Mixer.GroupMixCallback,System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL MIX_SetGroupPostMixCallback(MIX_Group *group, MIX_GroupMixCallback cb, void *userdata);
member.summary:
Set a callback that fires when a mixer group has completed mixing.
After all playing tracks in a mixer group have pulled in more data from their inputs, transformed it, and mixed together into a single buffer, a callback can be fired. This lets an app view the data at the last moment that it is still a part of this group. It can also change the data in any way it pleases during this callback, and the mixer will continue as if this data came directly from the group's mix buffer.
Each group has its own unique callback. Tracks that aren't in an explicit MIX_Group are mixed in an internal grouping that is not available to the app.
Passing a null callback here is legal; it disables this group's callback.
member.param: the mixing group to assign this callback to.
the function to call when the group mixes. May be null.
an opaque pointer provided to the callback for its own personal use.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_mixer 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.Mixer.SetPostMixCallback(System.IntPtr,SDL3.Mixer.PostMixCallback,System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL MIX_SetPostMixCallback(MIX_Mixer *mixer, MIX_PostMixCallback cb, void *userdata);

summary.para: Set a callback that fires when all mixing has completed.

After all mixer groups have processed, their buffers are mixed together into a single buffer for the final output, at which point a callback can be fired. This lets an app view the data at the last moment before mixing completes. It can also change the data in any way it pleases during this callback, and the mixer will continue as if this data is the final output.
Each mixer has its own unique callback.
Passing a null callback here is legal; it disables this mixer's callback.

member.param: the mixer to assign this callback to.
the function to call when the mixer mixes. May be null.
an opaque pointer provided to the callback for its own personal use.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_mixer 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.Mixer.Generate(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr,System.Int32)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL MIX_Generate(MIX_Mixer *mixer, void *buffer, int buflen);

summary.para: Generate mixer output when not driving an audio device.

SDL_mixer allows the creation of MIX_Mixer objects that are not connected to an audio device, by calling instead of . Such mixers will not generate output until explicitly requested through this function.

The caller may request as much audio as desired, so long as buflen is a multiple of the sample frame size specified when creating the mixer (for example, if requesting stereo Sint16 audio, buflen must be a multiple of 4: 2 bytes-per-channel times 2 channels).
The mixer will mix as quickly as possible; since it works in sample frames instead of time, it can potentially generate enormous amounts of audio in a small amount of time.
On success, this always fills buffer with buflen bytes of audio; if all playing tracks finish mixing, it will fill the remaining buffer with silence.

Each call to this function will pick up where it left off, playing tracks will continue to mix from the point the previous call completed, etc. The mixer state can be changed between each call in any way desired: tracks can be added, played, stopped, changed, removed, etc. Effectively this function does the same thing SDL_mixer does internally when the audio device needs more audio to play.
This function can not be used with mixers from ; those generate audio as needed internally.

member.param: the mixer for which to generate more audio.
a pointer to a buffer to store audio in.
the number of bytes to store in buffer.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_mixer 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.Mixer.CreateAudioDecoder(System.String,System.UInt32)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC MIX_AudioDecoder * SDLCALL MIX_CreateAudioDecoder(const char *path, SDL_PropertiesID props);
member.summary:
Create a MIX_AudioDecoder from a path on the filesystem.
Most apps won't need this, as SDL_mixer's usual interfaces will decode audio as needed. However, if one wants to decode an audio file into a memory buffer without playing it, this interface offers that.
This function allows properties to be specified. This is intended to supply file-specific settings, such as where to find SoundFonts for a MIDI file, etc. In most cases, the caller should pass a zero to specify no extra properties.
SDL_PropertiesID are discussed in [SDL's documentation](https://wiki.libsdl.org/SDL3/CategoryProperties) .
When done with the audio decoder, it can be destroyed with .
This function requires SDL_mixer to have been initialized with a successful call to , but does not need an actual MIX_Mixer to have been created.
member.param: the path on the filesystem from which to load data.
decoder-specific properties. May be zero.
member.returns: an audio decoder, ready to decode.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_mixer 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.Mixer.CreateAudioDecoderIO(System.IntPtr,System.Boolean,System.UInt32)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC MIX_AudioDecoder * SDLCALL MIX_CreateAudioDecoder_IO(SDL_IOStream *io, bool closeio, SDL_PropertiesID props);
member.summary:
Create a MIX_AudioDecoder from an SDL_IOStream.
Most apps won't need this, as SDL_mixer's usual interfaces will decode audio as needed. However, if one wants to decode an audio file into a memory buffer without playing it, this interface offers that.
This function allows properties to be specified. This is intended to supply file-specific settings, such as where to find SoundFonts for a MIDI file, etc. In most cases, the caller should pass a zero to specify no extra properties.
If closeio is true, then io will be closed when this decoder is done with it. If this function fails and closeio is true, then io will be closed before this function returns.
When done with the audio decoder, it can be destroyed with .
This function requires SDL_mixer to have been initialized with a successful call to , but does not need an actual MIX_Mixer to have been created.
member.param: the i/o stream from which to load data.
if true, close the i/o stream when done with it.
decoder-specific properties. May be zero.
member.returns: an audio decoder, ready to decode.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_mixer 3.0.0.

member.summary:
Query the initial audio format of a MIX_AudioDecoder.
Note that some audio files can change format in the middle; some explicitly support this, but a more common example is two MP3 files concatenated together. In many cases, SDL_mixer will correctly handle these sort of files, but this function will only report the initial format a file uses.

member.summary:
Decode more audio from a MIX_AudioDecoder.
Data is decoded on demand in whatever format is requested. The format is permitted to change between calls.
This function will return the number of bytes decoded, which may be less than requested if there was an error or end-of-file. A return value of zero means the entire file was decoded, -1 means an unrecoverable error happened.

member.name: T:SDL3.Mixer.StereoGains
member.summary:
A set of per-channel gains for tracks using .
When forcing a track to stereo, the app can specify a per-channel gain, to further adjust the left or right outputs.
When mixing audio that has been forced to stereo, each channel is modulated by these values. A value of 1.0f produces no change, 0.0f produces silence.
A simple panning effect would be to set left to the desired value and right to 1.0f - left.
member.since: This struct is available since SDL_mixer 3.0.0.

member.name: F:SDL3.Mixer.StereoGains.Left
member.summary: left channel gain

member.name: F:SDL3.Mixer.StereoGains.Right
member.summary: right channel gain

member.name: T:SDL3.SDL.DialogFileCallback
member.code: typedef void (SDLCALL *SDL_DialogFileCallback)(void *userdata, const char * const *filelist, int filter);

summary.para: Callback used by file dialog functions.
The specific usage is described in each function.
If filelist is:

list.item: null, an error occurred. Details can be obtained with .
A pointer to null, the user either didn't choose any file or canceled the dialog.
A pointer to non-null, the user chose one or more files. The argument is a null-terminated array of pointers to UTF-8 encoded strings, each containing a path.

summary.para: The filelist argument should not be freed; it will automatically be freed when the callback returns.
The filter argument is the index of the filter that was selected, or -1 if no filter was selected or if the platform or method doesn't support fetching the selected filter.
In Android, the filelist are content:// URIs. They should be opened using with appropriate modes. This applies both to open and save file dialog.

member.param: an app-provided pointer, for the callback's use.
the file(s) chosen by the user.
index of the selected filter.
member.since: This datatype is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: T:SDL3.SDL.DialogFileFilter

summary.para: An entry for filters for file dialogs.

member.param: is a user-readable label for the filter (for example, "Office document").

is a semicolon-separated list of file extensions (for example, "doc;docx"). File extensions may only contain alphanumeric characters, hyphens, underscores and periods. Alternatively, the whole string can be a single asterisk ("*"), which serves as an "All files" filter.
member.since: This struct is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.ShowOpenFileDialog(SDL3.SDL.DialogFileCallback,System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr,SDL3.SDL.DialogFileFilter[],System.Int32,System.String,System.Boolean)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC void SDLCALL SDL_ShowOpenFileDialog(SDL_DialogFileCallback callback, void *userdata, SDL_Window *window, const SDL_DialogFileFilter *filters, int nfilters, const char *default_location, bool allow_many);

summary.para: Displays a dialog that lets the user select a file on their filesystem.
This function should only be invoked from the main thread.
This is an asynchronous function; it will return immediately, and the result will be passed to the callback.
The callback will be invoked with a null-terminated list of files the user chose. The list will be empty if the user canceled the dialog, and it will be null if an error occurred.
Note that the callback may be called from a different thread than the one the function was invoked on.
Depending on the platform, the user may be allowed to input paths that don't yet exist.
On Linux, dialogs may require XDG Portals, which requires DBus, which requires an event-handling loop. Apps that do not use SDL to handle events should add a call to in their main loop.

member.param: a function pointer to be invoked when the user selects a file and accepts, or cancels the dialog, or an error occurs.
an optional pointer to pass extra data to the callback when it will be invoked.
the window that the dialog should be modal for, may be null. Not all platforms support this option.

a list of filters, may be null>. See the [`SDL_DialogFileFilter`](SDL_DialogFileFilter#code-examples) documentation for examples]. Not all platforms support this option, and platforms that do support it may allow the user to ignore the filters. If non-NULL, it must remain valid at least until the callback is invoked.
the number of filters. Ignored if filters is null.
the default folder or file to start the dialog at, may be null. Not all platforms support this option.
if non-zero, the user will be allowed to select multiple entries. Not all platforms support this option.
member.threadsafety: This function should be called only from the main thread. The callback may be invoked from the same thread or from a different one, depending on the OS's constraints.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

the default folder or file to start the dialog at, may be null. Not all platforms support this option.

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.Props.GlobalVideoWaylandWLDisplayPointer

summary.para: The pointer to the global wl_display object used by the Wayland video backend.

Can be set before the video subsystem is initialized to import an external wl_display object from an application or toolkit for use in SDL, or read after initialization to export the wl_display used by the Wayland video backend. Setting this property after the video subsystem has been initialized has no effect, and reading it when the video subsystem is uninitialized will either return the user provided value, if one was set prior to initialization, or null. See docs/README-wayland.md for more information.

member.since: This macro is available since SDL 3.2.0.

member.name: T:SDL3.SDL.GUID

summary.para: An is a 128-bit identifier for an input device that identifies that device across runs of SDL programs on the same platform.
If the device is detached and then re-attached to a different port, or if the base system is rebooted, the device should still report the same GUID.

GUIDs are as precise as possible but are not guaranteed to distinguish physically distinct but equivalent devices. For example, two game controllers from the same vendor with the same product ID and revision may have the same GUID.
GUIDs may be platform-dependent (i.e., the same device may report different GUIDs on different operating systems).

member.since: This struct is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.LoadFunction(System.IntPtr,System.String)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_FunctionPointer SDLCALL SDL_LoadFunction(SDL_SharedObject *handle, const char *name);

summary.para: Look up the address of the named function in a shared object.
This function pointer is no longer valid after calling .
This function can only look up C function names. Other languages may have name mangling and intrinsic language support that varies from compiler to compiler.
Make sure you declare your function pointers with the same calling convention as the actual library function. Your code will crash mysteriously if you do not do this.
If the requested function doesn't exist, null is returned.

member.param: a valid shared object handle returned by .
the name of the function to look up.
member.returns: a pointer to the function or null on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

Note that any pointers from this object looked up through will no longer be valid.

member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: T:SDL3.SDL.Locale

summary.para: A struct to provide locale data.

Locale data is split into a spoken language, like English, and an optional country, like Canada. The language will be in ISO-639 format (so English would be "en"), and the country, if not NULL, will be an ISO-3166 country code (so Canada would be "CA").

member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.Locale.Language
member.summary: A language name, like "en" for English.

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.Locale.Country
member.summary: A country, like "US" for America. Can be null.

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.GetPreferredLocales(System.Int32@)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_Locale ** SDLCALL SDL_GetPreferredLocales(int *count);

summary.para: Report the user's preferred locale.

Returned language strings are in the format xx, where 'xx' is an ISO-639 language specifier (such as "en" for English, "de" for German, etc). Country strings are in the format YY, where "YY" is an ISO-3166 country code (such as "US" for the United States, "CA" for Canada, etc). Country might be null if there's no specific guidance on them (so you might get { "en", "US" } for American English, but { "en", null} means "English language, generically"). Language strings are never null, except to terminate the array.
Please note that not all of these strings are 2 characters; some are three or more.

The returned list of locales are in the order of the user's preference. For example, a German citizen that is fluent in US English and knows enough Japanese to navigate around Tokyo might have a list like: { "de", "en_US", "jp", null}. Someone from England might prefer British English (where "color" is spelled "colour", etc), but will settle for anything like it: { "en_GB", "en", null}.
This function returns null on error, including when the platform does not supply this information at all.

This might be a "slow" call that has to query the operating system. It's best to ask for this once and save the results. However, this list can change, usually because the user has changed a system preference outside of your program; SDL will send an event in this case, if possible, and you can call this function again to get an updated copy of preferred locales.

member.param: a pointer filled in with the number of locales returned, may be null.
member.returns: a null terminated array of locale pointers, or null on failure; call for more information. This is a single allocation that should be freed with when it is no longer needed.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.ShowMessageBox(SDL3.SDL.MessageBoxData@,System.Int32@)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_ShowMessageBox(const SDL_MessageBoxData *messageboxdata, int *buttonid);

summary.para: Create a modal message box.
If your needs aren't complex, it might be easier to use .

This function should be called on the thread that created the parent window, or on the main thread if the messagebox has no parent. It will block execution of that thread until the user clicks a button or closes the messagebox.
This function may be called at any time, even before . This makes it useful for reporting errors like a failure to create a renderer or OpenGL context.
On X11, SDL rolls its own dialog box with X11 primitives instead of a formal toolkit like GTK+ or Qt.

Note that if would fail because there isn't any available video target, this function is likely to fail for the same reasons. If this is a concern, check the return value from this function and fall back to writing to stderr if you can.

member.param: the structure with title, text and other options.
the pointer to which user id of hit button should be copied.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.OpenURL(System.String)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_OpenURL(const char *url);

summary.para: Open a URL/URI in the browser or other appropriate external application.

Open a URL in a separate, system-provided application. How this works will vary wildly depending on the platform. This will likely launch what makes sense to handle a specific URL's protocol (a web browser for http://, etc), but it might also be able to launch file managers for directories and other things.

What happens when you open a URL varies wildly as well: your game window may lose focus (and may or may not lose focus if your game was fullscreen or grabbing input at the time). On mobile devices, your app will likely move to the background or your process might be paused. Any given platform may or may not handle a given URL.

If this is unimplemented (or simply unavailable) for a platform, this will fail with an error. A successful result does not mean the URL loaded, just that we launched _something_ to handle it (or at least believe we did).
All this to say: this function can be useful, but you should definitely test it on every platform you target.

member.param: a valid URL/URI to open. Use file:///full/path/to/file for local files, if supported.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.GetPowerInfo(System.Int32@,System.Int32@)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_PowerState SDLCALL SDL_GetPowerInfo(int *seconds, int *percent);

summary.para: Get the current power supply details.

You should never take a battery status as absolute truth. Batteries (especially failing batteries) are delicate hardware, and the values reported here are best estimates based on what that hardware reports. It's not uncommon for older batteries to lose stored power much faster than it reports, or completely drain when reporting it has 20 percent left, etc.
Battery status can change at any time; if you are concerned with power state, you should call this function frequently, and perhaps ignore changes until they seem to be stable for a few seconds.
It's possible a platform can only report battery percentage or time left but not both.
On some platforms, retrieving power supply details might be expensive. If you want to display continuous status you could call this function every minute or so.

member.param: a pointer filled in with the seconds of battery life left, or null to ignore. This will be filled in with -1 if we can't determine a value or there is no battery.
a pointer filled in with the percentage of battery life left, between 0 and 100, or null to ignore. This will be filled in with -1 when we can't determine a value or there is no batter
member.returns: the current battery state or on failure; call for more information.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.CreateProcess(System.String[],System.Boolean)

summary.para: Create a new process.
The path to the executable is supplied in args[0]. args[1..N] are additional arguments passed on the command line of the new process, and the argument list should be terminated with a null, e.g.:
Setting pipe_stdio to true is equivalent to setting and to , and will allow the use of or and .
See for more details.

member.param: the path and arguments for the new process.
true to create pipes to the process's standard input and from the process's standard output, false for the process to have no input and inherit the application's standard output.
member.returns: the newly created and running process, or null if the process couldn't be created.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.CreateProcessWithProperties(System.UInt32)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_Process *SDLCALL SDL_CreateProcessWithProperties(SDL_PropertiesID props);

summary.para: Create a new process with the specified properties.
These are the supported properties:

list.item: : an array of strings containing the program to run, any arguments, and a null pointer, e.g. const char *args[] = { "myprogram", "argument", null}. This is a required property.
: an SDL_Environment pointer. If this property is set, it will be the entire environment for the process, otherwise the current environment is used.
: a UTF-8 encoded string representing the working directory for the process, defaults to the current working directory.
: an SDL_ProcessIO value describing where standard input for the process comes from, defaults to .
: an SDL_IOStream pointer used for standard input when is set to .
: an SDL_ProcessIO value describing where standard output for the process goes to, defaults to .
: an SDL_IOStream pointer used for standard output when is set to ..
: an SDL_ProcessIO value describing where standard error for the process goes to, defaults to .
: an SDL_IOStream pointer used for standard error when is set to .
: true if the error output of the process should be redirected into the standard output of the process. This property has no effect if is set.
: true if the process should run in the background. In this case the default input and output is and the exitcode of the process is not available, and will always be 0.

: a string containing the program to run and any parameters. This string is passed directly to CreateProcess on Windows, and does nothing on other platforms. This property is only important if you want to start programs that does non-standard command-line processing, and in most cases using is sufficient.

summary.para: On POSIX platforms, wait() and waitpid(-1, ...) should not be called, and SIGCHLD should not be ignored or handled because those would prevent SDL from properly tracking the lifetime of the underlying process. You should use instead.

member.param: the properties to use.
member.returns: the newly created and running process, or null if the process couldn't be created.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

summary.para: Get the properties associated with a process.
The following read-only properties are provided by SDL:

list.item: : the process ID of the process.
: an SDL_IOStream that can be used to write input to the process, if it was created with set to .
: a non-blocking SDL_IOStream that can be used to read output from the process, if it was created with set to .
: a non-blocking SDL_IOStream that can be used to read error output from the process, if it was created with set to .
: true if the process is running in the background.

summary.para: Read all the output from a process.
If a process was created with I/O enabled, you can use this function to read the output. This function blocks until the process is complete, capturing all output, and providing the process exit code.
If a process was created with I/O enabled, you can use this function to read the output. This function blocks until the process is complete, capturing all output, and providing the process exit code.
The data should be freed with .

member.param: The process to read.
a pointer filled in with the number of bytes read, may be null.
a pointer filled in with the process exit code if the process has exited, may be null.
member.returns: the data or null on failure; call for more information.

member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_IOStream *SDLCALL SDL_GetProcessInput(SDL_Process *process);

summary.para: Get the SDL_IOStream associated with process standard input.
The process must have been created with and pipe_stdio set to true, or with and set to .

Writing to this stream can return less data than expected if the process hasn't read its input. It may be blocked waiting for its output to be read, if so you may need to call and read the output in parallel with writing input.

member.param: The process to get the input stream for.
member.returns: the input stream or null on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

true to terminate the process immediately, false to try to stop the process gracefully. In general you should try to stop the process gracefully first as terminating a process may leave it with half-written data or in some other unstable state.

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.WaitProcess(System.IntPtr,System.Boolean,System.Int32@)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_WaitProcess(SDL_Process *process, bool block, int *exitcode);

summary.para: Wait for a process to finish.
This can be called multiple times to get the status of a process.

The exit code will be the exit code of the process if it terminates normally, a negative signal if it terminated due to a signal, or -255 otherwise. It will not be changed if the process is still running.

If you create a process with standard output piped to the application (`pipe_stdio` being true) then you should read all of the process output before calling . If you don't do this the process might be blocked indefinitely waiting for output to be read and will never return true;

member.param: The process to wait for.
If true, block until the process finishes; otherwise, report on the process' status.
a pointer filled in with the process exit code if the process has exited, may be null.
member.returns: true if the process exited, false otherwise.
member.threadsafety: This function is not thread safe.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: T:SDL3.SDL.ProcessIO

summary.para: Description of where standard I/O should be directed when creating a process.
If a standard I/O stream is set to , it will go to the same place as the application's I/O stream. This is the default for standard output and standard error.
If a standard I/O stream is set to , it is connected to NUL: on Windows and /dev/null on POSIX systems. This is the default for standard input.

If a standard I/O stream is set to , it is connected to a new SDL_IOStream that is available to the application. Standard input will be available as and allows , standard output will be available as and allows and , and standard error will be available as in the properties for the created process.

If a standard I/O stream is set to , it is connected to an existing SDL_IOStream provided by the application. Standard input is provided using , standard output is provided using , and standard error is provided using in the creation properties. These existing streams should be closed by the application once the new process is created.
In order to use an SDL_IOStream with , it must have or set. This is true for streams representing files and process I/O.

member.since: This enum is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.summary: The I/O stream is ignored.

member.summary: The I/O stream is connected to a new SDL_IOStream that the application can read or write

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.ProcessIO.Redirect

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.Malloc(System.UIntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_MALLOC void * SDLCALL SDL_malloc(size_t size);

summary.para: Allocate uninitialized memory.
The allocated memory returned by this function must be freed with .
If size is 0, it will be set to 1.
If you want to allocate memory aligned to a specific alignment, consider using .

If the allocation is successful, the returned pointer is guaranteed to be aligned to either the *fundamental alignment* (`alignof(max_align_t)` in C11 and later) or `2 * sizeof(void *)`, whichever is smaller. Use SDL_aligned_alloc() if you need to allocate memory aligned to an alignment greater than this guarantee.

member.param: the size to allocate.
member.returns: a pointer to the allocated memory, or null if allocation failed.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_MALLOC SDL_ALLOC_SIZE2(1, 2) void * SDLCALL SDL_calloc(size_t nmemb, size_t size);

summary.para: Allocate a zero-initialized array.
The memory returned by this function must be freed with .
If either of nmemb or size is 0, they will both be set to 1.

If the allocation is successful, the returned pointer is guaranteed to be aligned to either the *fundamental alignment* (`alignof(max_align_t)` in C11 and later) or `2 * sizeof(void *)`, whichever is smaller.

member.param: the number of elements in the array.
the size of each element of the array.
member.returns: a pointer to the allocated array, or null if allocation failed.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.Realloc(System.IntPtr,System.UIntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_ALLOC_SIZE(2) void * SDLCALL SDL_realloc(void *mem, size_t size);

summary.para: Change the size of allocated memory.
The memory returned by this function must be freed with .
If size is 0, it will be set to 1. Note that this is unlike some other C runtime realloc implementations, which may treat realloc(mem, 0) the same way as free(mem).
If mem is null, the behavior of this function is equivalent to . Otherwise, the function can have one of three possible outcomes:

list.item: If it returns the same pointer as mem, it means that mem was resized in place without freeing.
If it returns a different non-NULL pointer, it means that mem was freed and cannot be dereferenced anymore.
If it returns null (indicating failure), then mem will remain valid and must still be freed with .

summary.para: If the allocation is successfully resized, the returned pointer is guaranteed to be aligned to either the *fundamental alignment* (`alignof(max_align_t)` in C11 and later) or `2 * sizeof(void *)`, whichever is smaller.

member.param: a pointer to allocated memory to reallocate, or null.
the new size of the memory.
member.returns: a pointer to the newly allocated memory, or null if allocation failed.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.Free(System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC void SDLCALL SDL_free(void *mem);

summary.para: Free allocated memory.
The pointer is no longer valid after this call and cannot be dereferenced anymore.
If mem is null, this function does nothing.

member.param: a pointer to allocated memory, or null.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0
