member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.FlushIO(System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_FlushIO(SDL_IOStream *context);

summary.para: Flush any buffered data in the stream.
This function makes sure that any buffered data is written to the stream. Normally this isn't necessary but if the stream is a pipe or socket it guarantees that any pending data is sent.

member.param: SDL_IOStream structure to flush.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: Do not use the same SDL_IOStream from two threads at once.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.LoadFileIO(System.IntPtr,System.UIntPtr@,System.Boolean)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC void * SDLCALL SDL_LoadFile_IO(SDL_IOStream *src, size_t *datasize, bool closeio);

summary.para: Load all the data from an SDL data stream.
The data is allocated with a zero byte at the end (null terminated) for convenience. This extra byte is not included in the value reported via datasize.
The data should be freed with .

member.param: the SDL_IOStream to read all available data from.
a pointer filled in with the number of bytes read, may be null.
if true, calls on src before returning, even in the case of an error.
member.returns: the data or null on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: Do not use the same SDL_IOStream from two threads at once.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.LoadFile(System.String,System.UIntPtr@)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC void * SDLCALL SDL_LoadFile(const char *file, size_t *datasize);

summary.para: Load all the data from a file path.
The data is allocated with a zero byte at the end (null terminated) for convenience. This extra byte is not included in the value reported via datasize.
The data should be freed with .

member.param: the path to read all available data from.
if not null, will store the number of bytes read.
member.returns: the data or null on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.ReadU8(System.IntPtr,System.Byte@)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_ReadU8(SDL_IOStream *src, Uint8 *value);

summary.para: Use this function to read a byte from an SDL_IOStream.

This function will return false when the data stream is completely read, and will return . If false is returned and the stream is not at EOF, will return a different error value and will offer a human-readable message.

member.param: the SDL_IOStream to read from.
a pointer filled in with the data read.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure or EOF; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: Do not use the same SDL_IOStream from two threads at once.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.OpenTitleStorage(System.String,System.UInt32)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_Storage * SDLCALL SDL_OpenTitleStorage(const char *override, SDL_PropertiesID props);
member.summary:
Opens up a read-only container for the application's filesystem.
By default, uses the generic storage implementation. When the path override is not provided, the generic implementation will use the output of as the base path.
member.param: a path to override the backend's default title root.
a property list that may contain backend-specific information.
member.returns: a title storage container on success or null on failure; call for more information.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.OpenUserStorage(System.String,System.String,System.UInt32)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_Storage * SDLCALL SDL_OpenUserStorage(const char *org, const char *app, SDL_PropertiesID props);

summary.para: Opens up a container for a user's unique read/write filesystem.

While title storage can generally be kept open throughout runtime, user storage should only be opened when the client is ready to read/write files. This allows the backend to properly batch file operations and flush them when the container has been closed; ensuring safe and optimal save I/O.

member.param: the name of your organization.
the name of your application.
a property list that may contain backend-specific information.
member.returns: a user storage container on success or null on failure; call for more information.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.OpenFileStorage(System.String)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_Storage * SDLCALL SDL_OpenFileStorage(const char *path);

summary.para: Opens up a container for local filesystem storage.
This is provided for development and tools. Portable applications should use for access to game data and for access to user data.

member.param: the base path prepended to all storage paths, or null for no base path.
member.returns: a filesystem storage container on success or null on failure; call for more information.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.OpenStorage(SDL3.SDL.StorageInterface@,System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_Storage * SDLCALL SDL_OpenStorage(const SDL_StorageInterface *iface, void *userdata);

summary.para: Opens up a container using a client-provided storage interface.

Applications do not need to use this function unless they are providing their own SDL_Storage implementation. If you just need an SDL_Storage, you should use the built-in implementations in SDL, like or .
This function makes a copy of iface and the caller does not need to keep it around after this call.

member.param: the interface that implements this storage, initialized using .
the pointer that will be passed to the interface functions.
member.returns: a storage container on success or null on failure; call for more information.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.CloseStorage(System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_CloseStorage(SDL_Storage *storage);

summary.para: Closes and frees a storage container.

member.param: a storage container to close.
member.returns: true if the container was freed with no errors, false otherwise; call for more information. Even if the function returns an error, the container data will be freed; the error is only for informational purposes.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.StorageReady(System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_StorageReady(SDL_Storage *storage);

summary.para: Checks if the storage container is ready to use.

This function should be called in regular intervals until it returns true - however, it is not recommended to spinwait on this call, as the backend may depend on a synchronous message loop. You might instead poll this game's main loop while processing events and drawing a loading screen.

member.param: a storage container to query.
member.returns: true if the container is ready, false otherwise.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.EnumerateStorageDirectory(System.IntPtr,System.String,SDL3.SDL.EnumerateDirectoryCallback,System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_EnumerateStorageDirectory(SDL_Storage *storage, const char *path, SDL_EnumerateDirectoryCallback callback, void *userdata);

summary.para: Enumerate a directory in a storage container through a callback function.
This function provides every directory entry through an app-provided callback, called once for each directory entry, until all results have been provided or the callback returns either or .

This will return false if there was a system problem in general, or if a callback returns . A successful return means a callback returned to halt enumeration, or all directory entries were enumerated.
If path is null, this is treated as a request to enumerate the root of the storage container's tree. An empty string also works for this.

member.param: a storage container.
the path of the directory to enumerate, or null for the root
a function that is called for each entry in the directory.
a pointer that is passed to callback.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.GlobStorageDirectory(System.IntPtr,System.String,System.String,SDL3.SDL.GlobFlags,System.Int32@)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC char ** SDLCALL SDL_GlobStorageDirectory(SDL_Storage *storage, const char *path, const char *pattern, SDL_GlobFlags flags, int *count);

summary.para: Enumerate a directory tree, filtered by pattern, and return a list.

Files are filtered out if they don't match the string in pattern, which may contain wildcard characters '*' (match everything) and '?' (match one character). If pattern is null, no filtering is done and all results are returned. Subdirectories are permitted, and are specified with a path separator of '/'. Wildcard characters '*' and '?' never match a path separator.
flags may be set to to make the pattern matching case-insensitive.
The returned array is always NULL-terminated, for your iterating convenience, but if count is non-NULL, on return it will contain the number of items in the array, not counting the null terminator.
If path is null, this is treated as a request to enumerate the root of the storage container's tree. An empty string also works for this.

member.param: a storage container.
the path of the directory to enumerate, or null for the root.
the pattern that files in the directory must match. Can be null.
SDL_GLOB_* bitflags that affect this search.
on return, will be set to the number of items in the returned array. Can be null.
member.returns: an array of strings on success or null on failure; call for more information. The caller should pass the returned pointer to when done with it. This is a single allocation that should be freed with when it is no longer needed.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread, assuming the storage object is thread-safe.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

Apps that want to supply a custom implementation of SDL_Storage will fill in all the functions in this struct, and then pass it to to create a custom SDL_Storage object.
It is not usually necessary to do this; SDL provides standard implementations for many things you might expect to do with an SDL_Storage.
This structure should be initialized using

member.name: T:SDL3.SDL.HapticCondition

summary.para: A structure containing a template for a Condition effect.
The struct handles the following effects:

list.item: : Effect based on axes position.
: Effect based on axes velocity.
: Effect based on axes acceleration.
: Effect based on axes movement.

summary.para: Direction is handled by condition internals instead of a direction member. The condition effect specific members have three parameters. The first refers to the X axis, the second refers to the Y axis and the third refers to the Z axis. The right terms refer to the positive side of the axis and the left terms refer to the negative side of the axis. Please refer to the diagram for which side is positive and which is negative.

member.name: T:SDL3.SDL.HapticCustom

summary.para: A structure containing a template for the effect.
This struct is exclusively for the effect.

A custom force feedback effect is much like a periodic effect, where the application can define its exact shape. You will have to allocate the data yourself. Data should consist of channels * samples Uint16 samples.
If channels is one, the effect is rotated using the defined direction. Otherwise it uses the samples in data for the different axes.

member.name: T:SDL3.SDL.HapticDirection

summary.para: Structure that represents a haptic direction.
This is the direction where the force comes from, instead of the direction in which the force is exerted.
Directions can be specified by:

list.item: : Specified by polar coordinates.
: Specified by cartesian coordinates.
: Specified by spherical coordinates.

summary.para: Cardinal directions of the haptic device are relative to the positioning of the device. North is considered to be away from the user.
The following diagram represents the cardinal directions:
If type is , direction is encoded by hundredths of a degree starting north and turning clockwise. only uses the first dir parameter. The cardinal directions would be:

list.item: North: 0 (0 degrees)
East: 9000 (90 degrees)
South: 18000 (180 degrees)
West: 27000 (270 degrees)

summary.para: If type is , direction is encoded by three positions (X axis, Y axis and Z axis (with 3 axes)). uses the first three dir` parameters. The cardinal directions would be:

list.item: North: 0,-1, 0
East: 1, 0, 0
South: 0, 1, 0

summary.para: The Z axis represents the height of the effect if supported, otherwise it's unused. In cartesian encoding (1, 2) would be the same as (2, 4), you can use any multiple you want, only the direction matters.
If type is , direction is encoded by two rotations. The first two dir parameters are used. The `dir` parameters are as follows (all values are in hundredths of degrees):

list.item: Degrees from (1, 0) rotated towards (0, 1).
Degrees towards (0, 0, 1) (device needs at least 3 axes).

summary.para: Example of force coming from the south with all encodings (force coming from the south means the user will have to pull the stick to counteract):

member.since: This struct is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.HapticDirection.Type
member.summary: The type of encoding.

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.HapticDirection.Dir
member.summary: The encoded direction.

member.name: T:SDL3.SDL.HapticEffect

summary.para: The generic template for any haptic effect.
All values max at 32767 (0x7FFF). Signed values also can be negative. Time values unless specified otherwise are in milliseconds.
You can also pass to length instead of a 0-32767 value. Neither delay, interval, attack_length nor fade_length support . Fade will also not be used since effect never ends.
Additionally, the effect does not support a duration of .
Button triggers may not be supported on all devices, it is advised to not use them if possible. Buttons start at index 1 instead of index 0 like the joystick.
If both attack_length and fade_level are 0, the envelope is not used, otherwise both values are used.
Common parts:
Here we have an example of a constant effect evolution in time:
Note either the attack_level or the fade_level may be above the actual effect level.

member.since: This struct is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: T:SDL3.SDL.HapticLeftRight

summary.para: A structure containing a template for a Left/Right effect.
This struct is exclusively for the effect.

The Left/Right effect is used to explicitly control the large and small motors, commonly found in modern game controllers. The small (right) motor is high frequency, and the large (left) motor is low frequency.

member.since: This struct is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.HapticLeftRight.Length
member.summary: Duration of the effect in milliseconds.

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.HapticLeftRight.LargeMagnitude
member.summary: Control of the large controller motor.

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.HapticLeftRight.SmallMagnitude
member.summary: Control of the small controller motor.

member.name: T:SDL3.SDL.HapticPeriodic

summary.para: A structure containing a template for a Periodic effect.
The struct handles the following effects:
A periodic effect consists in a wave-shaped effect that repeats itself over time. The type determines the shape of the wave and the parameters determine the dimensions of the wave.
Phase is given by hundredth of a degree meaning that giving the phase a value of 9000 will displace it 25% of its period. Here are sample values:

list.item: 0: No phase displacement.
9000: Displaced 25% of its period.
18000: Displaced 50% of its period.
27000: Displaced 75% of its period.
36000: Displaced 100% of its period, same as 0, but 0 is preferred.

summary.para: Examples:

member.name: T:SDL3.SDL.HapticRamp

summary.para: A structure containing a template for a Ramp effect.
This struct is exclusively for the effect.

The ramp effect starts at start strength and ends at end strength. It augments in linear fashion. If you use attack and fade with a ramp the effects get added to the ramp effect making the effect become quadratic instead of linear.

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.GetHaptics(System.Int32@)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_HapticID * SDLCALL SDL_GetHaptics(int *count);
member.summary: Get a list of currently connected haptic devices.
member.param: a pointer filled in with the number of haptic devices returned, may be null.
member.returns: a 0 terminated array of haptic device instance IDs or null on failure; call for more information. This should be freed with when it is no longer needed.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.GetHapticNameForID(System.Int32)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC const char * SDLCALL SDL_GetHapticNameForID(SDL_HapticID instance_id);

summary.para: Get the implementation dependent name of a haptic device.
This can be called before any haptic devices are opened.

member.param: the haptic device instance ID.
member.returns: the name of the selected haptic device. If no name can be found, this function returns null; call for more information.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.OpenHaptic(System.Int32)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_Haptic * SDLCALL SDL_OpenHaptic(SDL_HapticID instance_id);

summary.para: Open a haptic device for use.
The index passed as an argument refers to the N'th haptic device on this system.
When opening a haptic device, its gain will be set to maximum and autocenter will be disabled. To modify these values use and .

member.param: the haptic device instance ID.
member.returns: the device identifier or null on failure; call for more information.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.param: the instance ID to get the SDL_Haptic for.
member.returns: an SDL_Haptic on success or null on failure or if it hasn't been opened yet; call for more information.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.OpenHapticFromJoystick(System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_Haptic * SDLCALL SDL_OpenHapticFromJoystick(SDL_Joystick *joystick);

summary.para: Open a haptic device for use from a joystick device.
You must still close the haptic device separately. It will not be closed with the joystick.

When opened from a joystick you should first close the haptic device before closing the joystick device. If not, on some implementations the haptic device will also get unallocated and you'll be unable to use force feedback on that device.

member.param: the SDL_Joystick to create a haptic device from.
member.returns: a valid haptic device identifier on success or null on failure; call for more information.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.summary: Close a haptic device previously opened with .
member.param: the SDL_Haptic device to close.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.GetMaxHapticEffects(System.IntPtr)

summary.para: Get the number of effects a haptic device can store.
On some platforms this isn't fully supported, and therefore is an approximation. Always check to see if your created effect was actually created and do not rely solely on .

member.returns: the number of effects the haptic device can store or a negative error code on failure; call for more information.

member.summary: Create a new haptic effect on a specified device.

an structure containing the properties of the effect to create.

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.UpdateHapticEffect(System.IntPtr,System.Int32,SDL3.SDL.HapticEffect@)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_UpdateHapticEffect(SDL_Haptic *haptic, int effect, const SDL_HapticEffect *data);

summary.para: Update the properties of an effect.

Can be used dynamically, although behavior when dynamically changing direction may be strange. Specifically the effect may re-upload itself and start playing from the start. You also cannot change the type either when running .

member.param: the SDL_Haptic device that has the effect.
the identifier of the effect to update.
member.returns: an structure containing the new effect properties to use.
member.since: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.RunHapticEffect(System.IntPtr,System.Int32,System.UInt32)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_RunHapticEffect(SDL_Haptic *haptic, int effect, Uint32 iterations);

summary.para: Run the haptic effect on its associated haptic device.

To repeat the effect over and over indefinitely, set iterations to HAPTIC_INFINITY. (Repeats the envelope - attack and fade.) To make one instance of the effect last indefinitely (so the effect does not fade), set the effect's length in its structure/union to HAPTIC_INFINITY instead.

member.param: the SDL_Haptic device to run the effect on.
the ID of the haptic effect to run.
the number of iterations to run the effect; use HAPTIC_INFINITY to repeat forever.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

the ID of the haptic effect to stop.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.SetHapticGain(System.IntPtr,System.Int32)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_SetHapticGain(SDL_Haptic *haptic, int gain);

summary.para: Set the global gain of the specified haptic device.
Device must support the feature.
The user may specify the maximum gain by setting the environment variable HAPTIC_GAIN_MAX which should be between 0 and 100. All calls to will scale linearly using SDL_HAPTIC_GAIN_MAX as the maximum.

member.param: the SDL_Haptic device to set the gain on.
value to set the gain to, should be between 0 and 100 (0 - 100).
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.SetHapticAutocenter(System.IntPtr,System.Int32)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_SetHapticAutocenter(SDL_Haptic *haptic, int autocenter);

summary.para: Set the global autocenter of the device.
Autocenter should be between 0 and 100. Setting it to 0 will disable autocentering.
Device must support the feature.

member.param: the SDL_Haptic device to set autocentering on.
value to set autocenter to (0-100).
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

summary.para: Pause a haptic device.
Device must support the HAPTIC_PAUSE feature. Call to resume playback.
Do not modify the effects nor add new ones while the device is paused. That can cause all sorts of weird errors.

member.name: T:SDL3.SDL.GPUBlendFactor

summary.para: Specifies a blending factor to be used when pixels in a render target are blended with existing pixels in the texture.
The source color is the value written by the fragment shader. The destination color is the value currently existing in the texture.

member.since: This enum is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.GPUBlendFactor.SrcColor
member.summary: source color

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.GPUBlendFactor.OneMinusSrcColor
member.summary: 1 - source color

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.GPUBlendFactor.DstColor
member.summary: destination color

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.GPUBlendFactor.OneMinusDstColor
member.summary: 1 - destination color

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.GPUBlendFactor.SrcAlpha
member.summary: source alpha

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.GPUBlendFactor.OneMinusSrcAlpha
member.summary: 1 - source alpha

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.GPUBlendFactor.DstAlpha
member.summary: destination alpha

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.GPUBlendFactor.OneMinusDstAlpha
member.summary: 1 - destination alpha

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.GPUBlendFactor.ConstantColor
member.summary: blend constant

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.GPUBlendFactor.OneMinusConstantColor
member.summary: 1 - blend constant

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.GPUBlendFactor.SrcAlphaSaturate
member.summary: min(source alpha, 1 - destination alpha)

member.name: T:SDL3.SDL.GPUBlendOp

summary.para: Specifies the operator to be used when pixels in a render target are blended with existing pixels in the texture.
The source color is the value written by the fragment shader. The destination color is the value currently existing in the texture.

member.since: This enum is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.GPUBlendOp.Add
member.summary: (source * source_factor) + (destination * destination_factor)

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.GPUBlendOp.Subtract
member.summary: (source * source_factor) - (destination * destination_factor)

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.GPUBlendOp.ReverseSubtract
member.summary: (destination * destination_factor) - (source * source_factor)

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.GPUBlendOp.Min
member.summary: min(source, destination)

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.GPUBlendOp.Max
member.summary: max(source, destination)

member.name: T:SDL3.SDL.GPUBufferUsageFlags

summary.para: Specifies how a buffer is intended to be used by the client.
A buffer must have at least one usage flag. Note that some usage flag combinations are invalid.
Unlike textures, READ | WRITE can be used for simultaneous read-write usage. The same data synchronization concerns as textures apply.
If you use a STORAGE flag, the data in the buffer must respect std140 layout conventions. In practical terms this means you must ensure that vec3 and vec4 fields are 16-byte aligned.

member.since: This datatype is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.GPUBufferUsageFlags.Vertex
member.summary: Buffer is a vertex buffer.

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.GPUBufferUsageFlags.Index
member.summary: Buffer is an index buffer.

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.GPUBufferUsageFlags.Indirect
member.summary: Buffer is an indirect buffer.

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.GPUBufferUsageFlags.GraphicsStorageRead
member.summary: Buffer supports storage reads in graphics stages.

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.GPUBufferUsageFlags.ComputeStorageRead
member.summary: Buffer supports storage reads in the compute stage.

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.GPUBufferUsageFlags.ComputeStorageWrite
member.summary: Buffer supports storage writes in the compute stage.

member.name: T:SDL3.SDL.GPUColorTargetInfo

summary.para: A structure specifying the parameters of a color target used by a render pass.
The load_op field determines what is done with the texture at the beginning of the render pass.

list.item: LOAD: Loads the data currently in the texture. Not recommended for multisample textures as it requires significant memory bandwidth.
CLEAR: Clears the texture to a single color.
DONT_CARE: The driver will do whatever it wants with the texture memory. This is a good option if you know that every single pixel will be touched in the render pass.

summary.para: The store_op field determines what is done with the color results of the render pass.

list.item: STORE: Stores the results of the render pass in the texture. Not recommended for multisample textures as it requires significant memory bandwidth.
DONT_CARE: The driver will do whatever it wants with the texture memory. This is often a good option for depth/stencil textures.

RESOLVE: Resolves a multisample texture into resolve_texture, which must have a sample count of 1. Then the driver may discard the multisample texture memory. This is the most performant method of resolving a multisample target.

RESOLVE_AND_STORE: Resolves a multisample texture into the resolve_texture, which must have a sample count of 1. Then the driver stores the multisample texture's contents. Not recommended as it requires significant memory bandwidth.

member.since: This struct is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: T:SDL3.SDL.GPUDepthStencilTargetInfo

summary.para: A structure specifying the parameters of a depth-stencil target used by a render pass.
The load_op field determines what is done with the depth contents of the texture at the beginning of the render pass.

list.item: LOAD: Loads the depth values currently in the texture.
CLEAR: Clears the texture to a single depth.
DONT_CARE: The driver will do whatever it wants with the memory. This is a good option if you know that every single pixel will be touched in the render pass.

summary.para: The store_op field determines what is done with the depth results of the render pass.

list.item: STORE: Stores the depth results in the texture.
DONT_CARE: The driver will do whatever it wants with the depth results. This is often a good option for depth/stencil textures that don't need to be reused again.

summary.para: The stencil_load_op field determines what is done with the stencil contents of the texture at the beginning of the render pass.

list.item: LOAD: Loads the stencil values currently in the texture.
CLEAR: Clears the stencil values to a single value.
DONT_CARE: The driver will do whatever it wants with the memory. This is a good option if you know that every single pixel will be touched in the render pass.

summary.para: The stencil_store_op field determines what is done with the stencil results of the render pass.

list.item: STORE: Stores the stencil results in the texture.
DONT_CARE: The driver will do whatever it wants with the stencil results. This is often a good option for depth/stencil textures that don't need to be reused again.

summary.para: Note that depth/stencil targets do not support multisample resolves.
Due to ABI limitations, depth textures with more than 255 layers are not supported.

member.since: This struct is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: T:SDL3.SDL.GPUIndexedIndirectDrawCommand

summary.para: A structure specifying the parameters of an indexed indirect draw command.

Note that the FirstIndex and FirstInstance parameters are NOT compatible with built-in vertex/instance ID variables in shaders (for example, SV_VertexID); GPU APIs and shader languages do not define these built-in variables consistently, so if your shader depends on them, the only way to keep behavior consistent and portable is to always pass 0 for the correlating parameter in the draw calls.

member.name: T:SDL3.SDL.GPUPresentMode

summary.para: Specifies the timing that will be used to present swapchain textures to the OS.
mode will always be supported. and modes may not be supported on certain systems.
It is recommended to query after claiming the window if you wish to change the present mode to or .

list.item: : Waits for vblank before presenting. No tearing is possible. If there is a pending image to present, the new image is enqueued for presentation. Disallows tearing at the cost of visual latency.
: Immediately presents. Lowest latency option, but tearing may occur.

: Waits for vblank before presenting. No tearing is possible. If there is a pending image to present, the pending image is replaced by the new image. Similar to VSYNC, but with reduced visual latency.

member.since: This enum is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: T:SDL3.SDL.GPUPrimitiveType
member.summary:
Specifies the primitive topology of a graphics pipeline.
If you are using POINTLIST you must include a point size output in the vertex shader.
For HLSL compiling to SPIRV you must decorate a float output with [[vk::builtin("PointSize")]].
For GLSL you must set the gl_PointSize builtin. For MSL you must include a float output with the [[point_size]] decorator.
Note that sized point topology is totally unsupported on D3D12. Any size other than 1 will be ignored. In general, you should avoid using point topology for both compatibility and performance reasons. You WILL regret using it.
member.since: This enum is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.GPUPrimitiveType.TriangleList
member.summary: A series of separate triangles.

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.GPUPrimitiveType.TriangleStrip
member.summary: A series of connected triangles.

member.summary: A series of separate lines.

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.GPUPrimitiveType.LineStrip
member.summary: A series of connected lines.

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.GPUPrimitiveType.PointList
member.summary: A series of separate points.

member.name: T:SDL3.SDL.GPURasterizerState

summary.para: A structure specifying the parameters of the graphics pipeline rasterizer state.
Note that is not supported on many Android devices. For those devices, the fill mode will automatically fall back to FILL.

Also note that the D3D12 driver will enable depth clamping even if enable_depth_clip is true. If you need this clamp+clip behavior, consider enabling depth clip and then manually clamping depth in your fragment shaders on Metal and Vulkan.

member.since: This struct is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: P:SDL3.SDL.GPURasterizerState.EnableDepthClip
member.summary: true to enable depth clip, false to enable depth clamp.

member.name: T:SDL3.SDL.GPUSwapchainComposition

summary.para: Specifies the texture format and colorspace of the swapchain textures.
SDR will always be supported. Other compositions may not be supported on certain systems.
It is recommended to query after claiming the window if you wish to change the swapchain composition from SDR.

list.item: : B8G8R8A8 or R8G8B8A8 swapchain. Pixel values are in sRGB encoding.
: B8G8R8A8_SRGB or R8G8B8A8_SRGB swapchain. Pixel values are stored in memory in sRGB encoding but accessed in shaders in "linear sRGB" encoding which is sRGB but with a linear transfer function.
: swapchain. Pixel values are in extended linear sRGB encoding and permits values outside of the [0, 1] range.
: A2R10G10B10 or A2B10G10R10 swapchain. Pixel values are in BT.2020 ST2084 (PQ) encoding.

member.since: This enum is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: T:SDL3.SDL.GPUTextureTransferInfo
member.summary:
A structure specifying parameters related to transferring data to or from a texture.
If either of PixelsPerRow or RowsPerLayer is zero, then width and height of passed to or are used as default values respectively and data is considered to be tightly packed.
WARNING: On some older/integrated hardware, Direct3D 12 requires texture data row pitch to be 256 byte aligned, and offsets to be aligned to 512 bytes. If they are not, SDL will make a temporary copy of the data that is properly aligned, but this adds overhead to the transfer process. Apps can avoid this by aligning their data appropriately, or using a different GPU backend than Direct3D 12.
member.since: This struct is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: T:SDL3.SDL.GPUTextureUsageFlags

summary.para: Specifies how a texture is intended to be used by the client.
A texture must have at least one usage flag. Note that some usage flag combinations are invalid.

With regards to compute storage usage, READ | WRITE means that you can have shader A that only writes into the texture and shader B that only reads from the texture and bind the same texture to either shader respectively. SIMULTANEOUS means that you can do reads and writes within the same shader or compute pass. It also implies that atomic ops can be used, since those are read-modify-write operations. If you use SIMULTANEOUS, you are responsible for avoiding data races, as there is no data synchronization within a compute pass. Note that SIMULTANEOUS usage is only supported by a limited number of texture formats.

member.since: This datatype is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.summary: Texture supports sampling.

member.summary: Texture is a color render target.

member.summary: Texture is a depth stencil target.

member.summary: Texture supports storage reads in graphics stages.

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.GPUTextureUsageFlags.ComputeStorageRead
member.summary: Texture supports storage reads in the compute stage.

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.GPUTextureUsageFlags.ComputeStorageWrite
member.summary: Texture supports storage writes in the compute stage.

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.GPUTextureUsageFlags.ComputeStorageSimultaneousReadWrite
member.summary: Texture supports reads and writes in the same compute shader. This is NOT equivalent to READ | WRITE.

member.name: T:SDL3.SDL.GPUVertexBufferDescription

summary.para: A structure specifying the parameters of vertex buffers used in a graphics pipeline.

When you call , you specify the binding slots of the vertex buffers. For example if you called with a first_slot of 2 and num_bindings of 3, the binding slots 2, 3, 4 would be used by the vertex buffers you pass in.
Vertex attributes are linked to buffers via the buffer_slot field of . For example, if an attribute has a buffer_slot of 0, then that attribute belongs to the vertex buffer bound at slot 0.

member.name: T:SDL3.SDL.GPUVulkanOptions
member.summary:
A structure specifying additional options when using Vulkan.
When no such structure is provided, SDL will use Vulkan API version 1.0 and a minimal set of features. The requested API version influences how the feature_list is processed by SDL. When requesting API version 1.0, the feature_list is ignored. Only the vulkan_10_physical_device_features and the extension lists are used. When requesting API version 1.1, the feature_list is scanned for feature structures introduced in Vulkan 1.1. When requesting Vulkan 1.2 or higher, the feature_list is additionally scanned for compound feature structs such as VkPhysicalDeviceVulkan11Features. The device and instance extension lists, as well as vulkan_10_physical_device_features, are always processed.
member.since: This struct is available since SDL 3.4.0.

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.GPUVulkanOptions.VulkanApiVersion
member.summary: The Vulkan API version to request for the instance. Use Vulkan's VK_MAKE_VERSION or VK_MAKE_API_VERSION.

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.GPUVulkanOptions.FeatureList
member.summary: Pointer to the first element of a chain of Vulkan feature structs. (Requires API version 1.1 or higher.)

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.GPUVulkanOptions.Vulkan10PhysicalDeviceFeatures
member.summary: Pointer to a VkPhysicalDeviceFeatures struct to enable additional Vulkan 1.0 features.

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.GPUVulkanOptions.DeviceExtensionCount
member.summary: Number of additional device extensions to require.

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.GPUVulkanOptions.DeviceExtensionNames
member.summary: Pointer to a list of additional device extensions to require.

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.GPUVulkanOptions.InstanceExtensionCount
member.summary: Number of additional instance extensions to require.

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.CreateGPUDevice(SDL3.SDL.GPUShaderFormat,System.Boolean,System.String)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_GPUDevice *SDLCALL SDL_CreateGPUDevice(SDL_GPUShaderFormat format_flags, bool debug_mode, const char *name);
member.summary:
Creates a GPU context.
The GPU driver name can be one of the following:
vulkan: [Vulkan](CategoryGPU#vulkan)
direct3d12: [D3D12](CategoryGPU#d3d12)
metal: [Metal](CategoryGPU#metal)
NULL: let SDL pick the optimal driver
member.param: a bitflag indicating which shader formats the app is able to provide.
enable debug mode properties and validations.
the preferred GPU driver, or null to let SDL pick the optimal driver.
member.returns: a GPU context on success or null on failure; call for more information.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.CreateGPUDeviceWithProperties(System.UInt32)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_GPUDevice *SDLCALL SDL_CreateGPUDeviceWithProperties(SDL_PropertiesID props);

summary.para: Creates a GPU context.
These are the supported properties:

list.item: : enable debug mode properties and validations, defaults to true.
: enable to prefer energy efficiency over maximum GPU performance, defaults to false.
: enable to automatically log useful debug information on device creation, defaults to true.
: the name of the GPU driver to use, if a specific one is desired.

: Enable Vulkan device feature shaderClipDistance. If disabled, clip distances are not supported in shader code: gl_ClipDistance[] built-ins of GLSL, SV_ClipDistance0/1 semantics of HLSL and [[clip_distance]] attribute of Metal. Disabling optional features allows the application to run on some older Android devices. Defaults to true.

: Enable Vulkan device feature depthClamp. If disabled, there is no depth clamp support and enable_depth_clip in must always be set to true. Disabling optional features allows the application to run on some older Android devices. Defaults to true.

: Enable Vulkan device feature drawIndirectFirstInstance. If disabled, the argument first_instance of must be set to zero. Disabling optional features allows the application to run on some older Android devices. Defaults to true.

: Enable Vulkan device feature samplerAnisotropy. If disabled, enable_anisotropy of must be set to false. Disabling optional features allows the application to run on some older Android devices. Defaults to true.

summary.para: These are the current shader format properties:

list.item: : The app is able to provide shaders for an NDA platform.
: The app is able to provide SPIR-V shaders if applicable.
: The app is able to provide DXBC shaders if applicable : The app is able to provide DXIL shaders if applicable.
: The app is able to provide MSL shaders if applicable.
: The app is able to provide Metal shader libraries if applicable.

summary.para: With the D3D12 backend:

list.item: : the prefix to use for all vertex semantics, default is "TEXCOORD".

: By default, Resourcing Binding Tier 2 is required for D3D12 support. However, an application can set this property to true to enable Tier 1 support, if (and only if) the application uses 8 or fewer storage resources across all shader stages. As of writing, this property is useful for targeting Intel Haswell and Broadwell GPUs; other hardware either supports Tier 2 Resource Binding or does not support D3D12 in any capacity. Defaults to false.

: Certain feature checks are only possible on Windows 11 by default. By setting this alongside `SDL_PROP_GPU_DEVICE_CREATE_D3D12_AGILITY_SDK_PATH_STRING` and vendoring D3D12Core.dll from the D3D12 Agility SDK, you can make those feature checks possible on older platforms. The version you provide must match the one given in the DLL.

: Certain feature checks are only possible on Windows 11 by default. By setting this alongside and vendoring D3D12Core.dll from the D3D12 Agility SDK, you can make those feature checks possible on older platforms. The path you provide must be relative to the executable path of your app. Be sure not to put the DLL in the same directory as the exe; Microsoft strongly advises against this!

summary.para: With the Vulkan backend:

list.item: : By default, Vulkan device enumeration includes drivers of all types, including software renderers (for example, the Lavapipe Mesa driver). This can be useful if your application _requires_ SDL_GPU, but if you can provide your own fallback renderer (for example, an OpenGL renderer) this property can be set to true. Defaults to false.

: a pointer to an structure to be processed during device creation. This allows configuring a variety of Vulkan-specific options such as increasing the API version and opting into extensions aside from the minimal set SDL requires.

summary.para: With the Metal backend:

list.item: : By default, macOS support requires what Apple calls "MTLGPUFamilyMac2" hardware or newer. However, an application can set this property to true to enable support for "MTLGPUFamilyMac1" hardware, if (and only if) the application does not write to sRGB textures. (For history's sake: MacFamily1 also does not support indirect command buffers, MSAA depth resolve, and stencil resolve/feedback, but these are not exposed features in SDL_GPU.)

member.param: the properties to use.
member.returns: a GPU context on success or null on failure; call for more information.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.summary: Get the number of GPU drivers compiled into SDL.
member.returns: the number of built in GPU drivers.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.GetGPUDriver(System.Int32)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC const char * SDLCALL SDL_GetGPUDriver(int index);

summary.para: Get the name of a built in GPU driver.
The GPU drivers are presented in the order in which they are normally checked during initialization.
The names of drivers are all simple, low-ASCII identifiers, like "vulkan", "metal" or "direct3d12". These never have Unicode characters, and are not meant to be proper names.

member.param: the index of a GPU driver.
member.returns: the name of the GPU driver with the given index.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.param: a GPU context to query.
member.returns: the name of the device's driver, or null on error.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.GetGPUDeviceProperties(System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_PropertiesID SDLCALL SDL_GetGPUDeviceProperties(SDL_GPUDevice *device);

summary.para: Get the properties associated with a GPU device.
All properties are optional and may differ between GPU backends and SDL versions.
The following properties are provided by SDL:

: Contains the name of the underlying device as reported by the system driver. This string has no standardized format, is highly inconsistent between hardware devices and drivers, and is able to change at any time. Do not attempt to parse this string as it is bound to fail at some point in the future when system drivers are updated, new hardware devices are introduced, or when SDL adds new GPU backends or modifies existing ones.
Strings that have been found in the wild include:

list.item: GTX 970
GeForce GTX 970
NVIDIA GeForce GTX 970
Microsoft Direct3D12 (NVIDIA GeForce GTX 970)
NVIDIA Graphics Device
GeForce GPU
AMD 15D8:C9
AMD Custom GPU 0405
AMD Radeon (TM) Graphics
ASUS Radeon RX 470 Series
Intel(R) Arc(tm) A380 Graphics (DG2)
Virtio-GPU Venus (NVIDIA TITAN V)
SwiftShader Device (LLVM 16.0.0)
llvmpipe (LLVM 15.0.4, 256 bits)
Microsoft Basic Render Driver
unknown device

summary.para: The above list shows that the same device can have different formats, the vendor name may or may not appear in the string, the included vendor name may not be the vendor of the chipset on the device, some manufacturers include pseudo-legal marks while others don't, some devices may not use a marketing name in the string, the device string may be wrapped by the name of a translation interface, the device may be emulated in software, or the string may contain generic text that does not identify the device at all.
: Contains the self-reported name of the underlying system driver.
Strings that have been found in the wild include:

list.item: Intel Corporation
Intel open-source Mesa driver
Qualcomm Technologies Inc. Adreno Vulkan Driver
MoltenVK
Mali-G715
venus

summary.para: : Contains the self-reported version of the underlying system driver. This is a relatively short version string in an unspecified format. If is available then that property should be preferred over this one as it may contain additional information that is useful for identifying the exact driver version used.
Strings that have been found in the wild include:

: Contains the detailed version information of the underlying system driver as reported by the driver. This is an arbitrary string with no standardized format and it may contain newlines. This property should be preferred over if it is available as it usually contains the same information but in a format that is easier to read.
Strings that have been found in the wild include:

list.item: Mesa 22.2.0-devel (git-f226222 2022-04-14 impish-oibaf-ppa)

summary.para: This string has also been observed to be a multiline string (which has a trailing newline):
summary.code: Driver Build: 85da404, I46ff5fc46f, 1606794520 Date: 11/30/20 Compiler Version: EV031.31.04.01 Driver Branch: promo490_3_Google

member.param: a GPU context to query.
member.returns: a valid property ID on success or 0 on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.4.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.CreateGPUComputePipeline(System.IntPtr,SDL3.SDL.GPUComputePipelineCreateInfo@)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_GPUComputePipeline *SDLCALL SDL_CreateGPUComputePipeline(SDL_GPUDevice *device, const SDL_GPUComputePipelineCreateInfo *createinfo);

summary.para: Creates a pipeline object to be used in a compute workflow.
Shader resource bindings must be authored to follow a particular order depending on the shader format.
For SPIR-V shaders, use the following resource sets:

list.item: 0: Sampled textures, followed by read-only storage textures, followed by read-only storage buffers
1: Read-write storage textures, followed by read-write storage buffers
2: Uniform buffers

summary.para: For DXBC and DXIL shaders, use the following register order:

list.item: (t[n], space0): Sampled textures, followed by read-only storage textures, followed by read-only storage buffers
(u[n], space1): Read-write storage textures, followed by read-write storage buffers
(b[n], space2): Uniform buffers

summary.para: For MSL/metallib, use the following order:

list.item: [[buffer]]: Uniform buffers, followed by read-only storage buffers, followed by read-write storage buffers
[[texture]]: Sampled textures, followed by read-only storage textures, followed by read-write storage textures

member.param: a GPU Context.
a struct describing the state of the compute pipeline to create.
member.returns: a compute pipeline object on success, or null on failure; call for more information.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.CreateGPUShader(System.IntPtr,SDL3.SDL.GPUShaderCreateInfo@)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_GPUShader *SDLCALL SDL_CreateGPUShader(SDL_GPUDevice *device, const SDL_GPUShaderCreateInfo *createinfo);

summary.para: Creates a shader to be used when creating a graphics pipeline.
Shader resource bindings must be authored to follow a particular order depending on the shader format.
For SPIR-V shaders, use the following resource sets:
For vertex shaders:

list.item: 0: Sampled textures, followed by storage textures, followed by storage buffers
1: Uniform buffers

summary.para: For fragment shaders:

list.item: 2: Sampled textures, followed by storage textures, followed by storage buffers
3: Uniform buffers

summary.para: For DXBC and DXIL shaders, use the following register order:
For vertex shaders:

list.item: (t[n], space0): Sampled textures, followed by storage textures, followed by storage buffers
(s[n], space0): Samplers with indices corresponding to the sampled textures
(b[n], space1): Uniform buffers

summary.para: For pixel shaders:

list.item: (t[n], space2): Sampled textures, followed by storage textures, followed by storage buffers
(s[n], space2): Samplers with indices corresponding to the sampled textures
(b[n], space3): Uniform buffers

summary.para: For MSL/metallib, use the following order:

list.item: [[texture]]: Sampled textures, followed by storage textures
[[sampler]]: Samplers with indices corresponding to the sampled textures

[[buffer]]: Uniform buffers, followed by storage buffers. Vertex buffer 0 is bound at [[buffer(14)]], vertex buffer 1 at [[buffer(15)]], and so on. Rather than manually authoring vertex buffer indices, use the [[stage_in]] attribute which will automatically use the vertex input information from the SDL_GPUGraphicsPipeline.

summary.para: Shader semantics other than system-value semantics do not matter in D3D12 and for ease of use the SDL implementation assumes that non system-value semantics will all be TEXCOORD. If you are using HLSL as the shader source language, your vertex semantics should start at TEXCOORD0 and increment like so: TEXCOORD1, TEXCOORD2, etc. If you wish to change the semantic prefix to something other than TEXCOORD you can use with .
There are optional properties that can be provided through props. These are the supported properties:

list.item: : a name that can be displayed in debugging tools.

member.param: a GPU Context.
a struct describing the state of the shader to create.
member.returns: a shader object on success, or null on failure; call for more information.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.CreateGPUTexture(System.IntPtr,SDL3.SDL.GPUTextureCreateInfo@)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_GPUTexture *SDLCALL SDL_CreateGPUTexture(SDL_GPUDevice *device, const SDL_GPUTextureCreateInfo *createinfo);

summary.para: Creates a texture object to be used in graphics or compute workflows.
The contents of this texture are undefined until data is written to the texture, either via or by performing a render or compute pass with this texture as a target.
Note that certain combinations of usage flags are invalid. For example, a texture cannot have both the and flags.
If you request a sample count higher than the hardware supports, the implementation will automatically fall back to the highest available sample count.
There are optional properties that can be provided through 's props. These are the supported properties:

list.item: : (Direct3D 12 only) if the texture usage is , clear the texture to a color with this red intensity. Defaults to zero.
: (Direct3D 12 only) if the texture usage is , clear the texture to a color with this green intensity. Defaults to zero.
: (Direct3D 12 only) if the texture usage is , clear the texture to a color with this blue intensity. Defaults to zero.
: (Direct3D 12 only) if the texture usage is , clear the texture to a color with this alpha intensity. Defaults to zero.
: (Direct3D 12 only) if the texture usage is , clear the texture to a depth of this value. Defaults to zero.
: (Direct3D 12 only) if the texture usage is , clear the texture to a stencil of this value. Defaults to zero.
: (Direct3D 12 only) if the texture usage , clear the texture to a stencil of this Uint8 value. Defaults to zero.
: a name that can be displayed in debugging tools.

member.param: a GPU Context.
a struct describing the state of the texture to create.
member.returns: a texture object on success, or null on failure; call for more information.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.CreateGPUBuffer(System.IntPtr,SDL3.SDL.GPUBufferCreateInfo@)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_GPUBuffer *SDLCALL SDL_CreateGPUBuffer(SDL_GPUDevice *device, const SDL_GPUBufferCreateInfo *createinfo);

summary.para: Creates a buffer object to be used in graphics or compute workflows.
The contents of this buffer are undefined until data is written to the buffer.
Note that certain combinations of usage flags are invalid. For example, a buffer cannot have both the and flags.
If you use a STORAGE flag, the data in the buffer must respect std140 layout conventions. In practical terms this means you must ensure that vec3 and vec4 fields are 16-byte aligned.
For better understanding of underlying concepts and memory management with SDL GPU API, you may refer [this blog post](https://moonside.games/posts/sdl-gpu-concepts-cycling/).
There are optional properties that can be provided through props. These are the supported properties:

list.item: : a name that can be displayed in debugging tools.

member.param: a GPU Context.
a struct describing the state of the buffer to create.
member.returns: a buffer object on success, or null on failure; call for more information.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

summary.para: Creates a transfer buffer to be used when uploading to or downloading from graphics resources.
Download buffers can be particularly expensive to create, so it is good practice to reuse them if data will be downloaded regularly.

member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC void SDLCALL SDL_InsertGPUDebugLabel(SDL_GPUCommandBuffer *command_buffer, const char *text);

summary.para: Inserts an arbitrary string label into the command buffer callstream.
Useful for debugging.

On Direct3D 12, using requires WinPixEventRuntime.dll to be in your PATH or in the same directory as your executable. See [here](https://devblogs.microsoft.com/pix/winpixeventruntime/) for instructions on how to obtain it.

member.param: a command buffer.
a UTF-8 string constant to insert as the label.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.PushGPUDebugGroup(System.IntPtr,System.String)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC void SDLCALL SDL_PushGPUDebugGroup(SDL_GPUCommandBuffer *command_buffer, const char *name);

summary.para: Begins a debug group with an arbitrary name.
Used for denoting groups of calls when viewing the command buffer callstream in a graphics debugging tool.
Each call to must have a corresponding call to .

On Direct3D 12, using requires WinPixEventRuntime.dll to be in your PATH or in the same directory as your executable. See [here](https://devblogs.microsoft.com/pix/winpixeventruntime/) for instructions on how to obtain it.

On some backends (e.g. Metal), pushing a debug group during a render/blit/compute pass will create a group that is scoped to the native pass rather than the command buffer. For best results, if you push a debug group during a pass, always pop it in the same pass.

member.param: a command buffer.
a UTF-8 string constant that names the group.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC void SDLCALL SDL_PopGPUDebugGroup(SDL_GPUCommandBuffer *command_buffer);

Ends the most-recently pushed debug group.
On Direct3D 12, using requires WinPixEventRuntime.dll to be in your PATH or in the same directory as your executable. See [here](https://devblogs.microsoft.com/pix/winpixeventruntime/) for instructions on how to obtain it.
member.param: a command buffer.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.AcquireGPUCommandBuffer(System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_GPUCommandBuffer *SDLCALL SDL_AcquireGPUCommandBuffer(SDL_GPUDevice *device);

summary.para: Acquire a command buffer.

This command buffer is managed by the implementation and should not be freed by the user. The command buffer may only be used on the thread it was acquired on. The command buffer should be submitted on the thread it was acquired on.

It is valid to acquire multiple command buffers on the same thread at once. In fact a common design pattern is to acquire two command buffers per frame where one is dedicated to render and compute passes and the other is dedicated to copy passes and other preparatory work such as generating mipmaps. Interleaving commands between the two command buffers reduces the total amount of passes overall which improves rendering performance.

member.param: a GPU context.
member.returns: a command buffer, or null on failure; call for more information.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.PushGPUVertexUniformData(System.IntPtr,System.UInt32,System.IntPtr,System.UInt32)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC void SDLCALL SDL_PushGPUVertexUniformData(SDL_GPUCommandBuffer *command_buffer, Uint32 slot_index, const void *data, Uint32 length);

summary.para: Pushes data to a vertex uniform slot on the command buffer.
Subsequent draw calls will use this uniform data.
The data being pushed must respect std140 layout conventions. In practical terms this means you must ensure that vec3 and vec4 fields are 16-byte aligned.
For detailed information about accessing uniform data from a shader, please refer to .

member.param: a command buffer.
the vertex uniform slot to push data to.
client data to write.
the length of the data to write.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.BeginGPURenderPass(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr,System.UInt32,System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_GPURenderPass *SDLCALL SDL_BeginGPURenderPass(SDL_GPUCommandBuffer *command_buffer, const SDL_GPUColorTargetInfo *color_target_infos, Uint32 num_color_targets, const SDL_GPUDepthStencilTargetInfo *depth_stencil_target_info);

summary.para: Begins a render pass on a command buffer.

A render pass consists of a set of texture subresources (or depth slices in the 3D texture case) which will be rendered to during the render pass, along with corresponding clear values and load/store operations. All operations related to graphics pipelines must take place inside of a render pass. A default viewport and scissor state are automatically set when this is called. You cannot begin another render pass, or begin a compute pass or copy pass until you have ended the render pass.

member.param: a command buffer.
an array of texture subresources with corresponding clear values and load/store ops.
the number of color targets in the colorTargetInfos array.
a texture subresource with corresponding clear value and load/store ops, may be null.
member.returns: a render pass handle.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.BeginGPURenderPass(System.IntPtr,SDL3.SDL.GPUColorTargetInfo[]@,System.UInt32,System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_GPURenderPass *SDLCALL SDL_BeginGPURenderPass(SDL_GPUCommandBuffer *command_buffer, const SDL_GPUColorTargetInfo *color_target_infos, Uint32 num_color_targets, const SDL_GPUDepthStencilTargetInfo *depth_stencil_target_info);

summary.para: Begins a render pass on a command buffer.

A render pass consists of a set of texture subresources (or depth slices in the 3D texture case) which will be rendered to during the render pass, along with corresponding clear values and load/store operations. All operations related to graphics pipelines must take place inside of a render pass. A default viewport and scissor state are automatically set when this is called. You cannot begin another render pass, or begin a compute pass or copy pass until you have ended the render pass.

member.param: a command buffer.
an array of texture subresources with corresponding clear values and load/store ops.
the number of color targets in the colorTargetInfos array.
a texture subresource with corresponding clear value and load/store ops, may be null.
member.returns: a render pass handle.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.BeginGPURenderPass(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr,System.UInt32,SDL3.SDL.GPUDepthStencilTargetInfo@)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_GPURenderPass *SDLCALL SDL_BeginGPURenderPass(SDL_GPUCommandBuffer *command_buffer, const SDL_GPUColorTargetInfo *color_target_infos, Uint32 num_color_targets, const SDL_GPUDepthStencilTargetInfo *depth_stencil_target_info);

summary.para: Begins a render pass on a command buffer.

A render pass consists of a set of texture subresources (or depth slices in the 3D texture case) which will be rendered to during the render pass, along with corresponding clear values and load/store operations. All operations related to graphics pipelines must take place inside of a render pass. A default viewport and scissor state are automatically set when this is called. You cannot begin another render pass, or begin a compute pass or copy pass until you have ended the render pass.

member.param: a command buffer.
an array of texture subresources with corresponding clear values and load/store ops.
the number of color targets in the colorTargetInfos array.
a texture subresource with corresponding clear value and load/store ops, may be null.
member.returns: a render pass handle.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

summary.para: Draws data using bound graphics state with an index buffer and instancing enabled.
You must not call this function before binding a graphics pipeline.

Note that the firstIndex and firstInstance parameters are NOT compatible with built-in vertex/instance ID variables in shaders (for example, SV_VertexID); GPU APIs and shader languages do not define these built-in variables consistently, so if your shader depends on them, the only way to keep behavior consistent and portable is to always pass 0 for the correlating parameter in the draw calls.

member.param: a render pass handle.
the number of indices to draw per instance.
the number of instances to draw.
the starting index within the index buffer.
value added to vertex index before indexing into the vertex buffer.
the ID of the first instance to draw.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.BeginGPUComputePass(System.IntPtr,SDL3.SDL.GPUStorageTextureReadWriteBinding[],System.UInt32,SDL3.SDL.GPUStorageBufferReadWriteBinding[],System.UInt32)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_GPUComputePass *SDLCALL SDL_BeginGPUComputePass(SDL_GPUCommandBuffer *command_buffer, const SDL_GPUStorageTextureReadWriteBinding *storage_texture_bindings, Uint32 num_storage_texture_bindings, const SDL_GPUStorageBufferReadWriteBinding *storage_buffer_bindings, Uint32 num_storage_buffer_bindings);

summary.para: Begins a compute pass on a command buffer.

A compute pass is defined by a set of texture subresources and buffers that may be written to by compute pipelines. These textures and buffers must have been created with the bit or the bit. If you do not create a texture with , you must not read from the texture in the compute pass. All operations related to compute pipelines must take place inside of a compute pass. You must not begin another compute pass, or a render pass or copy pass before ending the compute pass.

A VERY IMPORTANT NOTE - Reads and writes in compute passes are NOT implicitly synchronized. This means you may cause data races by both reading and writing a resource region in a compute pass, or by writing multiple times to a resource region. If your compute work depends on reading the completed output from a previous dispatch, you MUST end the current compute pass and begin a new one before you can safely access the data. Otherwise you will receive unexpected results. Reading and writing a texture in the same compute pass is only supported by specific texture formats. Make sure you check the format support!

member.param: a command buffer.
an array of writeable storage texture binding structs.
the number of storage textures to bind from the array.
an array of writeable storage buffer binding structs.
the number of storage buffers to bind from the array.
member.returns: a compute pass handle.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

summary.para: Dispatches compute work.
You must not call this function before binding a compute pipeline.

A VERY IMPORTANT NOTE If you dispatch multiple times in a compute pass, and the dispatches write to the same resource region as each other, there is no guarantee of which order the writes will occur. If the write order matters, you MUST end the compute pass and begin another one.

number of local workgroups to dispatch in the X dimension.
number of local workgroups to dispatch in the Y dimension.
number of local workgroups to dispatch in the Z dimension.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.SetGPUAllowedFramesInFlight(System.IntPtr,System.UInt32)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_SetGPUAllowedFramesInFlight(SDL_GPUDevice *device, Uint32 allowed_frames_in_flight);

summary.para: Configures the maximum allowed number of frames in flight.

The default value when the device is created is 2. This means that after you have submitted 2 frames for presentation, if the GPU has not finished working on the first frame, will fill the swapchain texture pointer with null, and will block.
Higher values increase throughput at the expense of visual latency. Lower values decrease visual latency at the expense of throughput.
Note that calling this function will stall and flush the command queue to prevent synchronization issues.
The minimum value of allowed frames in flight is 1, and the maximum is 3.

member.param: a GPU context.
the maximum number of frames that can be pending on the GPU.
member.returns: true if successful, false on error; call for more information.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.1.8.

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.GetGPUSwapchainTextureFormat(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_GPUTextureFormat SDLCALL SDL_GetGPUSwapchainTextureFormat(SDL_GPUDevice *device, SDL_Window *window);

summary.para: Obtains the texture format of the swapchain for the given window.
Note that this format can change if the swapchain parameters change.

member.param: a GPU context.
an SDL_Window that has been claimed.
member.returns: the texture format of the swapchain.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.AcquireGPUSwapchainTexture(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr@,System.UInt32@,System.UInt32@)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_AcquireGPUSwapchainTexture(SDL_GPUCommandBuffer *command_buffer, SDL_Window *window, SDL_GPUTexture **swapchain_texture, Uint32 *swapchain_texture_width, Uint32 *swapchain_texture_height);

summary.para: Acquire a texture to use in presentation.

When a swapchain texture is acquired on a command buffer, it will automatically be submitted for presentation when the command buffer is submitted. The swapchain texture should only be referenced by the command buffer used to acquire it.

This function will fill the swapchain texture handle with NULL if too many frames are in flight. This is not an error. This NULL pointer should not be passed back into SDL. Instead, it should be considered as an indication to wait until the swapchain is available.

If you use this function, it is possible to create a situation where many command buffers are allocated while the rendering context waits for the GPU to catch up, which will cause memory usage to grow. You should use unless you know what you are doing with timing.
The swapchain texture is managed by the implementation and must not be freed by the user. You MUST NOT call this function from any thread other than the one that created the window.

member.param: a command buffer.
a window that has been claimed.
a pointer filled in with a swapchain texture handle.
a pointer filled in with the swapchain texture width, may be null.
a pointer filled in with the swapchain texture height, may be null.
member.returns: true on success, false on error; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called from the thread that created the window.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.WaitForGPUSwapchain(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_WaitForGPUSwapchain(SDL_GPUDevice *device, SDL_Window *window);

summary.para: Blocks the thread until a swapchain texture is available to be acquired.

member.param: a GPU context.
a window that has been claimed.
member.returns: true on success, false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called from the thread that created the window.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.WaitAndAcquireGPUSwapchainTexture(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr@,System.UInt32@,System.UInt32@)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_WaitAndAcquireGPUSwapchainTexture(SDL_GPUCommandBuffer *command_buffer, SDL_Window *window, SDL_GPUTexture **swapchain_texture, Uint32 *swapchain_texture_width, Uint32 *swapchain_texture_height);

summary.para: Blocks the thread until a swapchain texture is available to be acquired, and then acquires it.

When a swapchain texture is acquired on a command buffer, it will automatically be submitted for presentation when the command buffer is submitted. The swapchain texture should only be referenced by the command buffer used to acquire it. It is an error to call after a swapchain texture is acquired.

This function can fill the swapchain texture handle with null in certain cases, for example if the window is minimized. This is not an error. You should always make sure to check whether the pointer is null before actually using it.
The swapchain texture is managed by the implementation and must not be freed by the user. You MUST NOT call this function from any thread other than the one that created the window.
The swapchain texture is write-only and cannot be used as a sampler or for another reading operation.

member.param: a command buffer.
a window that has been claimed.
a pointer filled in with a swapchain texture handle.
a pointer filled in with the swapchain texture width, may be null.
a pointer filled in with the swapchain texture height, may be null.
member.returns: true on success, false on error; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called from the thread that created the window.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.SubmitGPUCommandBuffer(System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_SubmitGPUCommandBuffer(SDL_GPUCommandBuffer *command_buffer);

summary.para: Submits a command buffer so its commands can be processed on the GPU.
It is invalid to use the command buffer after this is called.
This must be called from the thread the command buffer was acquired on.
All commands in the submission are guaranteed to begin executing before any command in a subsequent submission begins executing.

member.param: a command buffer.
member.returns: true on success, false on failure; call for more information.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

summary.para: Submits a command buffer so its commands can be processed on the GPU, and acquires a fence associated with the command buffer.
You must release this fence when it is no longer needed or it will cause a leak. It is invalid to use the command buffer after this is called.
This must be called from the thread the command buffer was acquired on.
All commands in the submission are guaranteed to begin executing before any command in a subsequent submission begins executing.

member.param: a command buffer.
member.returns: a fence associated with the command buffer, or null on failure; call for more information.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.1.6.

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.OpenXRLoadLibrary
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_OpenXR_LoadLibrary(void);
member.summary:
Dynamically load the OpenXR loader.
SDL keeps a reference count of the OpenXR loader, calling this function multiple times will increment that count, rather than loading the library multiple times.
If not called, this will be implicitly called when creating a GPU device with OpenXR.
This function will use the platform default OpenXR loader name, unless the hint is set.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function is not thread safe.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.6.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.OpenXRUnloadLibrary
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC void SDLCALL SDL_OpenXR_UnloadLibrary(void);
member.summary:
Unload the OpenXR loader previously loaded by .
SDL keeps a reference count of the OpenXR loader, calling this function will decrement that count. Once the reference count reaches zero, the library is unloaded.
member.threadsafety: This function is not thread safe.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.6.0.

member.name: T:SDL3.SDL.AudioDeviceEvent
member.summary: Audio device event structure (event.adevice.*)

member.name: T:SDL3.SDL.EventFilter
member.code: typedef bool (SDLCALL *SDL_EventFilter)(void *userdata, SDL_Event *event);
member.summary: A function pointer used for callbacks that watch the event queue.
member.param: what was passed as userdata to or , etc.
the event that triggered the callback.
member.returns: true to permit event to be added to the queue, and false to disallow it. When used with , the return value is ignored.
member.threadsafety: SDL may call this callback at any time from any thread; the application is responsible for locking resources the callback touches that need to be protected.

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.Event.padding
member.summary:
This is necessary for ABI compatibility between Visual C++ and GCC. Visual C++ will respect the push pack pragma and use 52 bytes (size of SDL_TextEditingEvent, the largest structure for 32-bit and 64-bit architectures) for this union, and GCC will use the alignment of the largest datatype within the union, which is 8 bytes on 64-bit architectures.
So... we'll add padding to force the size to be the same for both.
On architectures where pointers are 16 bytes, this needs rounding up to the next multiple of 16, 64, and on architectures where pointers are even larger the size of SDL_UserEvent will dominate as being 3 pointers.

member.summary: The application is being terminated by the OS. This event must be handled in a callback set with . Called on iOS in applicationWillTerminate() Called on Android in onDestroy()

member.summary: The application is low on memory, free memory if possible. This event must be handled in a callback set with . Called on iOS in applicationDidReceiveMemoryWarning() Called on Android in onTrimMemory()

member.summary: The application is about to enter the background. This event must be handled in a callback set with . Called on iOS in applicationWillResignActive() Called on Android in onPause()

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.EventType.DidEnterBackground

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.EventType.DidEnterForeground
member.summary: The application is now interactive. This event must be handled in a callback set with . Called on iOS in applicationDidBecomeActive() Called on Android in onResume()

member.name: T:SDL3.SDL.GamepadDeviceEvent

summary.para: Gamepad device event structure (event.gdevice.*)
Joysticks that are supported gamepads receive both an and an .
SDL will send events for joysticks that are already plugged in during and are recognized as gamepads. It will also send events for joysticks that get gamepad mappings at runtime.

member.name: T:SDL3.SDL.PenProximityEvent

summary.para: Pressure-sensitive pen proximity event structure (event.pproximity.*)

When a pen becomes visible to the system (it is close enough to a tablet, etc), SDL will send an event with the new pen's ID. This ID is valid until the pen leaves proximity again (has been removed from the tablet's area, the tablet has been unplugged, etc). If the same pen reenters proximity again, it will be given a new ID.
Note that "proximity" means "close enough for the tablet to know the tool is there." The pen touching and lifting off from the tablet while not leaving the area are handled by and .
Not all platforms have a window associated with the pen during proximity events. Some wait until motion/button/etc events to offer this info.

member.since: This struct is available since SDL 3.2.0
