member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.CreateTextureWithProperties(System.IntPtr,System.UInt32)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_Texture * SDLCALL SDL_CreateTextureWithProperties(SDL_Renderer *renderer, SDL_PropertiesID props);
member.summary:
Create a texture for a rendering context with the specified properties.
These are the supported properties:
: an value describing the texture colorspace, defaults to for floating point textures, for 10-bit textures, for other RGB textures and for YUV textures.
: one of the enumerated values in , defaults to the best RGBA format for the renderer
: one of the enumerated values in , defaults to
: the width of the texture in pixels, required
: the height of the texture in pixels, required
: an SDL_Palette to use with palettized texture formats. This can be set later with
: for HDR10 and floating point textures, this defines the value of 100% diffuse white, with higher values being displayed in the High Dynamic Range headroom. This defaults to 100 for HDR10 textures and 1.0 for floating point textures.
: for HDR10 and floating point textures, this defines the maximum dynamic range used by the content, in terms of the SDR white point. This would be equivalent to maxCLL / for HDR10 content. If this is defined, any values outside the range supported by the display will be scaled into the available HDR headroom, otherwise they are clipped.
With the direct3d11 renderer:
: the ID3D11Texture2D associated with the texture, if you want to wrap an existing texture.
: the ID3D11Texture2D associated with the U plane of a YUV texture, if you want to wrap an existing texture.
: the ID3D11Texture2D associated with the V plane of a YUV texture, if you want to wrap an existing texture.
With the direct3d12 renderer:
: the ID3D12Resource associated with the texture, if you want to wrap an existing texture.
: the ID3D12Resource associated with the U plane of a YUV texture, if you want to wrap an existing texture.
: the ID3D12Resource associated with the V plane of a YUV texture, if you want to wrap an existing texture.
With the metal renderer:
: the CVPixelBufferRef associated with the texture, if you want to create a texture from an existing pixel buffer.
With the opengl renderer:
the GLuint texture associated with the texture, if you want to wrap an existing texture.
: the GLuint texture associated with the UV plane of an NV12 texture, if you want to wrap an existing texture.
: the GLuint texture associated with the U plane of a YUV texture, if you want to wrap an existing texture.
: the GLuint texture associated with the V plane of a YUV texture, if you want to wrap an existing texture.
With the opengles2 renderer:
: the GLuint texture associated with the texture, if you want to wrap an existing texture.
: the GLuint texture associated with the texture, if you want to wrap an existing texture.
: the GLuint texture associated with the UV plane of an NV12 texture, if you want to wrap an existing texture.
: the GLuint texture associated with the U plane of a YUV texture, if you want to wrap an existing texture.
: the GLuint texture associated with the V plane of a YUV texture, if you want to wrap an existing texture.
With the vulkan renderer:
: the VkImage with layout VK_IMAGE_LAYOUT_SHADER_READ_ONLY_OPTIMAL associated with the texture, if you want to wrap an existing texture.
With the GPU renderer:
: the SDL_GPUTexture associated with the texture, if you want to wrap an existing texture.
: the SDL_GPUTexture associated with the UV plane of an NV12 texture, if you want to wrap an existing texture.
: the SDL_GPUTexture associated with the U plane of a YUV texture, if you want to wrap an existing texture.
: the SDL_GPUTexture associated with the V plane of a YUV texture, if you want to wrap an existing texture.
member.param: the rendering context.
the properties to use.
member.returns: the created texture or null on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.GetTextureProperties(System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_PropertiesID SDLCALL SDL_GetTextureProperties(SDL_Texture *texture);

summary.para: Get the properties associated with a texture.
The following read-only properties are provided by SDL:

list.item: : an SDL_Colorspace value describing the texture colorspace.
: one of the enumerated values in .
: one of the enumerated values in .
: the width of the texture in pixels.
: the height of the texture in pixels.

: for HDR10 and floating point textures, this defines the value of 100% diffuse white, with higher values being displayed in the High Dynamic Range headroom. This defaults to 100 for HDR10 textures and 1.0 for other textures.

: for HDR10 and floating point textures, this defines the maximum dynamic range used by the content, in terms of the SDR white point. If this is defined, any values outside the range supported by the display will be scaled into the available HDR headroom, otherwise they are clipped. This defaults to 1.0 for SDR textures, 4.0 for HDR10 textures, and no default for floating point textures.

summary.para: With the direct3d11 renderer:

list.item: : the ID3D11Texture2D associated with the texture
: the ID3D11Texture2D associated with the U plane of a YUV texture
: the ID3D11Texture2D associated with the V plane of a YUV texture

summary.para: With the direct3d12 renderer:

list.item: : the ID3D12Resource associated with the texture
: the ID3D12Resource associated with the U plane of a YUV texture
: the ID3D12Resource associated with the V plane of a YUV texture

summary.para: With the vulkan renderer:

list.item: : the VkImage associated with the texture, if you want to wrap an existing texture.
: the VkImageLayout for the VkImage, defaults to VK_IMAGE_LAYOUT_SHADER_READ_ONLY_OPTIMAL.

summary.para: With the opengl renderer:

list.item: : the GLuint texture associated with the texture
: the GLuint texture associated with the UV plane of an NV12 texture
: the GLuint texture associated with the U plane of a YUV texture
: the GLuint texture associated with the V plane of a YUV texture
: the GLenum for the texture target (`GL_TEXTURE_2D`, `GL_TEXTURE_RECTANGLE_ARB`, etc)
: the texture coordinate width of the texture (0.0 - 1.0)
: the texture coordinate height of the texture (0.0 - 1.0)

summary.para: With the opengles2 renderer:

list.item: : the GLuint texture associated with the texture
: the GLuint texture associated with the UV plane of an NV12 texture
: the GLuint texture associated with the U plane of a YUV texture
: the GLuint texture associated with the V plane of a YUV texture
: the GLenum for the texture target (`GL_TEXTURE_2D`, `GL_TEXTURE_EXTERNAL_OES`, etc)

summary.para: With the gpu renderer:

list.item: : the SDL_GPUTexture associated with the texture

summary.para: With the gpu renderer:

list.item: : the SDL_GPUTexture associated with the texture
: the SDL_GPUTexture associated with the UV plane of an NV12 texture
: the SDL_GPUTexture associated with the U plane of a YUV texture
: the SDL_GPUTexture associated with the V plane of a YUV texture

member.param: the texture to query.
member.returns: a valid property ID on success or 0 on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

summary.para: Set an additional color value multiplied into render copy operations.
When this texture is rendered, during the copy operation each source color channel is modulated by the appropriate color value according to the following formula:
Color modulation is not always supported by the renderer; it will return false if color modulation is not supported.

the blue color value multiplied into copy operations.

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.UpdateTexture(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr,System.Int32)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_UpdateTexture(SDL_Texture *texture, const SDL_Rect *rect, const void *pixels, int pitch);

summary.para: Update the given texture rectangle with new pixel data.
The pixel data must be in the pixel format of the texture, which can be queried using the property.
This is a fairly slow function, intended for use with static textures that do not change often.

If the texture is intended to be updated often, it is preferred to create the texture as streaming and use the locking functions referenced below. While this function will work with streaming textures, for optimization reasons you may not get the pixels back if you lock the texture afterward.

member.param: the texture to update.
an structure representing the area to update, or null to update the entire texture.
the raw pixel data in the format of the texture.
the number of bytes in a row of pixel data, including padding between lines.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.LockTexture(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr@,System.Int32@)

summary.para: Lock a portion of the texture for write-only pixel access.

As an optimization, the pixels made available for editing don't necessarily contain the old texture data. This is a write-only operation, and if you need to keep a copy of the texture data you should do that at the application level.
You must use to unlock the pixels and apply any changes.

member.param: the texture to lock for access, which was created with .
an structure representing the area to lock for access; null to lock the entire texture.
this is filled in with a pointer to the locked pixels, appropriately offset by the locked area.
this is filled in with the pitch of the locked pixels; the pitch is the length of one row in bytes.
member.returns: true on success or false if the texture is not valid or was not created with ; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.LockTexture(System.IntPtr,SDL3.SDL.Rect@,System.IntPtr@,System.Int32@)

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.LockTextureToSurface(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr@)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_LockTextureToSurface(SDL_Texture *texture, const SDL_Rect *rect, SDL_Surface **surface);

summary.para: Lock a portion of the texture for write-only pixel access, and expose it as a SDL surface.
Besides providing an instead of raw pixel data, this function operates like .

As an optimization, the pixels made available for editing don't necessarily contain the old texture data. This is a write-only operation, and if you need to keep a copy of the texture data you should do that at the application level.
You must use to unlock the pixels and apply any changes.
The returned surface is freed internally after calling or . The caller should not free it.

member.param: the texture to lock for access, which must be created with .
a pointer to the rectangle to lock for access. If the rect is null, the entire texture will be locked.
a pointer to an SDL surface of size rect. Don't assume any specific pixel content.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.UnlockTexture(System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC void SDLCALL SDL_UnlockTexture(SDL_Texture *texture);

summary.para: Unlock a texture, uploading the changes to video memory, if needed.

Warning: Please note that is intended to be write-only; it will not guarantee the previous contents of the texture will be provided. You must fully initialize any area of a texture that you lock before unlocking it, as the pixels might otherwise be uninitialized memory.
Which is to say: locking and immediately unlocking a texture can result in corrupted textures, depending on the renderer in use.

member.param: a texture locked by .
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.SetRenderTarget(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_SetRenderTarget(SDL_Renderer *renderer, SDL_Texture *texture);

summary.para: Set a texture as the current rendering target.
The default render target is the window for which the renderer was created. To stop rendering to a texture and render to the window again, call this function with a null texture.

Viewport, cliprect, scale, and logical presentation are unique to each render target. Get and set functions for these states apply to the current render target set by this function, and those states persist on each target when the current render target changes.

member.param: the rendering context.
the targeted texture, which must be created with the flag, or null to render to the window instead of a texture.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_Texture * SDLCALL SDL_GetRenderTarget(SDL_Renderer *renderer);

summary.para: Get the current render target.
The default render target is the window for which the renderer was created, and is reported a null here.

member.param: the rendering context.
member.returns: the current render target or null for the default render target.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.SetRenderLogicalPresentation(System.IntPtr,System.Int32,System.Int32,SDL3.SDL.RendererLogicalPresentation)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_SetRenderLogicalPresentation(SDL_Renderer *renderer, int w, int h, SDL_RendererLogicalPresentation mode);

summary.para: Set a device-independent resolution and presentation mode for rendering.

This function sets the width and height of the logical rendering output. The renderer will act as if the current render target is always the requested dimensions, scaling to the actual resolution as necessary.
This can be useful for games that expect a fixed size, but would like to scale the output to whatever is available, regardless of how a user resizes a window, or if the display is high DPI.

Logical presentation can be used with both render target textures and the renderer's window; the state is unique to each render target, and this function sets the state for the current render target. It might be useful to draw to a texture that matches the window dimensions with logical presentation enabled, and then draw that texture across the entire window with logical presentation disabled. Be careful not to render both with logical presentation enabled, however, as this could produce double-letterboxing, etc.

You can disable logical coordinates by setting the mode to , and in that case you get the full pixel resolution of the render target; it is safe to toggle logical presentation during the rendering of a frame: perhaps most of the rendering is done to specific dimensions but to make fonts look sharp, the app turns off logical presentation while drawing text, for example.
You can convert coordinates in an event into rendering coordinates using .

member.param: the rendering context.
the width of the logical resolution.
the height of the logical resolution.
the presentation mode used.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.GetRenderLogicalPresentation(System.IntPtr,System.Int32@,System.Int32@,SDL3.SDL.RendererLogicalPresentation@)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_GetRenderLogicalPresentation(SDL_Renderer *renderer, int *w, int *h, SDL_RendererLogicalPresentation *mode);

summary.para: Get device independent resolution and presentation mode for rendering.
This function gets the width and height of the logical rendering output, or 0 if a logical resolution is not enabled.
Each render target has its own logical presentation state. This function gets the state for the current render target.

member.param: the rendering context.
an int filled with the logical presentation width.
an int filled with the logical presentation height.
a variable filled with the logical presentation mode being used.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.GetRenderLogicalPresentationRect(System.IntPtr,SDL3.SDL.FRect@)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_GetRenderLogicalPresentationRect(SDL_Renderer *renderer, SDL_FRect *rect);

summary.para: Get the final presentation rectangle for rendering.

This function returns the calculated rectangle used for logical presentation, based on the presentation mode and output size. If logical presentation is disabled, it will fill the rectangle with the output size, in pixels.
Each render target has its own logical presentation state. This function gets the rectangle for the current render target.

member.param: the rendering context.
a pointer filled in with the final presentation rectangle, may be null.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.ConvertEventToRenderCoordinates(System.IntPtr,SDL3.SDL.Event@)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_ConvertEventToRenderCoordinates(SDL_Renderer *renderer, SDL_Event *event);

summary.para: Convert the coordinates in an event to render coordinates.
This takes into account several states:

list.item: The window dimensions.
The logical presentation settings ()
The scale ()
The viewport ()

summary.para: Various event types are converted with this function: mouse, touch, pen, etc.
Touch coordinates are converted from normalized coordinates in the window to non-normalized rendering coordinates.

Relative mouse coordinates (xrel and yrel event fields) are _also_ converted. Applications that do not want these fields converted should use on the specific event fields instead of converting the entire event structure.
Once converted, coordinates may be outside the rendering area.

member.param: the rendering context.
the event to modify.
member.returns: true if the event is converted or doesn't need conversion, or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_RenderViewportSet(SDL_Renderer *renderer);

summary.para: Return whether an explicit rectangle was set as the viewport.
This is useful if you're saving and restoring the viewport and want to know whether you should restore a specific rectangle or NULL.
Each render target has its own viewport. This function checks the viewport for the current render target.

member.param: the rendering context.
member.returns: true if the viewport was set to a specific rectangle, or false if it was set to null (the entire target).
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.GetRenderSafeArea(System.IntPtr,SDL3.SDL.Rect@)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_GetRenderSafeArea(SDL_Renderer *renderer, SDL_Rect *rect);

summary.para: Get the safe area for rendering within the current viewport.

Some devices have portions of the screen which are partially obscured or not interactive, possibly due to on-screen controls, curved edges, camera notches, TV overscan, etc. This function provides the area of the current viewport which is safe to have interactible content. You should continue rendering into the rest of the render target, but it should not contain visually important or interactible content.

member.param: the rendering context.
a pointer filled in with the area that is safe for interactive content.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.SetRenderScale(System.IntPtr,System.Single,System.Single)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_SetRenderScale(SDL_Renderer *renderer, float scaleX, float scaleY);

summary.para: Set the drawing scale for rendering on the current target.
The drawing coordinates are scaled by the x/y scaling factors before they are used by the renderer. This allows resolution independent drawing with a single coordinate system.
If this results in scaling or subpixel drawing by the rendering backend, it will be handled using the appropriate quality hints. For best results use integer scaling factors.
Each render target has its own scale. This function sets the scale for the current render target.

member.param: the rendering context.
the horizontal scaling factor.
the vertical scaling factor.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

Get the drawing scale for the current target.
Each render target has its own scale. This function gets the scale for the current render target.
member.param: the rendering context.
a pointer filled in with the horizontal scaling factor.
a pointer filled in with the vertical scaling factor.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.SetRenderColorScale(System.IntPtr,System.Single)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_SetRenderColorScale(SDL_Renderer *renderer, float scale);

summary.para: Set the color scale used for render operations.

The color scale is an additional scale multiplied into the pixel color value while rendering. This can be used to adjust the brightness of colors during HDR rendering, or changing HDR video brightness when playing on an SDR display.
The color scale does not affect the alpha channel, only the color brightness.

member.param: the rendering context.
the color scale value.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_RenderClear(SDL_Renderer *renderer);

summary.para: Clear the current rendering target with the drawing color.

This function clears the entire rendering target, ignoring the viewport and the clip rectangle. Note, that clearing will also set/fill all pixels of the rendering target to current renderer draw color, so make sure to invoke when needed.

member.param: the rendering context.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.RenderTextureRotated(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr,System.Double,SDL3.SDL.FPoint@,SDL3.SDL.FlipMode)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_RenderTextureRotated(SDL_Renderer *renderer, SDL_Texture *texture, const SDL_FRect *srcrect, const SDL_FRect *dstrect, double angle, const SDL_FPoint *center, SDL_FlipMode flip);
member.summary: Copy a portion of the source texture to the current rendering target, with rotation and flipping, at subpixel precision.
member.param: the renderer which should copy parts of a texture.
the source texture.
a pointer to the source rectangle, or null for the entire texture.
a pointer to the destination rectangle, or null for the entire rendering target.
an angle in degrees that indicates the rotation that will be applied to dstrect, rotating it in a clockwise direction.
a pointer to a point indicating the point around which dstrect will be rotated (if null, rotation will be done around dstrect.w/2, dstrect.h/2).
an value stating which flipping actions should be performed on the texture.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.RenderTextureAffine(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_RenderTextureAffine(SDL_Renderer *renderer, SDL_Texture *texture, const SDL_FRect *srcrect, const SDL_FPoint *origin, const SDL_FPoint *right, const SDL_FPoint *down);

summary.para: Copy a portion of the source texture to the current rendering target, with affine transform, at subpixel precision.

member.param: the renderer which should copy parts of a texture.
the source texture.
a pointer to the source rectangle, or null for the entire texture.
a pointer to a point indicating where the top-left corner of srcrect should be mapped to, or null for the rendering target's origin.
a pointer to a point indicating where the top-right corner of srcrect should be mapped to, or null for the rendering target's top-right corner.
a pointer to a point indicating where the bottom-left corner of srcrect should be mapped to, or null for the rendering target's bottom-left corner.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: You may only call this function from the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.1.8.

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.RenderTextureTiled(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr,System.Single,System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_RenderTextureTiled(SDL_Renderer *renderer, SDL_Texture *texture, const SDL_FRect *srcrect, float scale, const SDL_FRect *dstrect);

summary.para: Tile a portion of the texture to the current rendering target at subpixel precision.
The pixels in srcrect will be repeated as many times as needed to completely fill dstrect.

member.param: the renderer which should copy parts of a texture.
the source texture.
a pointer to the source rectangle, or null for the entire texture.
the scale used to transform srcrect into the destination rectangle, e.g. a 32x32 texture with a scale of 2 would fill 64x64 tiles.
a pointer to the destination rectangle, or null for the entire rendering target.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.RenderTexture9Grid(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr,SDL3.SDL.FRect@,System.Single,System.Single,System.Single,System.Single,System.Single,System.IntPtr)

summary.para: erform a scaled copy using the 9-grid algorithm to the current rendering target at subpixel precision.

The pixels in the texture are split into a 3x3 grid, using the different corner sizes for each corner, and the sides and center making up the remaining pixels. The corners are then scaled using `scale` and fit into the corners of the destination rectangle. The sides and center are then stretched into place to cover the remaining destination rectangle.

member.param: the renderer which should copy parts of a texture.
the source texture.
the structure representing the rectangle to be used for the 9-grid, or null to use the entire texture.
the width, in pixels, of the left corners in srcrect.
the width, in pixels, of the right corners in srcrect.
the height, in pixels, of the top corners in srcrect.
the height, in pixels, of the bottom corners in srcrect.
the scale used to transform the corner of srcrect into the corner of dstrect, or 0.0f for an unscaled copy.
a pointer to the destination rectangle, or null for the entire rendering target.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.RenderReadPixels(System.IntPtr,System.Nullable{SDL3.SDL.Rect})
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_Surface * SDLCALL SDL_RenderReadPixels(SDL_Renderer *renderer, const SDL_Rect *rect);

summary.para: Read pixels from the current rendering target.
The returned surface contains pixels inside the desired area clipped to the current viewport, and should be freed with .
Note that this returns the actual pixels on the screen, so if you are using logical presentation you should use to get the area containing your content.
WARNING: This is a very slow operation, and should not be used frequently. If you're using this on the main rendering target, it should be called after rendering and before .

member.param: the rendering context.
an structure representing the area to read, which will be clipped to the current viewport, or null for the entire viewport.
member.returns: a new on success or null on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.RenderPresent(System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_RenderPresent(SDL_Renderer *renderer);

summary.para: Update the screen with any rendering performed since the previous call.

SDL's rendering functions operate on a backbuffer; that is, calling a rendering function such as does not directly put a line on the screen, but rather updates the backbuffer. As such, you compose your entire scene and present the composed backbuffer to the screen as a complete picture.
Therefore, when using SDL's rendering API, one does all drawing intended for the frame, and then calls this function once per frame to present the final drawing to the user.

The backbuffer should be considered invalidated after each present; do not assume that previous contents will exist between frames. You are strongly encouraged to call to initialize the backbuffer before starting each new frame's drawing, even if you plan to overwrite every pixel.

Please note, that in case of rendering to a texture - there is **no need** to call after drawing needed objects to a texture, and should not be done; you are only required to change back the rendering target to default via SetRenderTarget(renderer, null) afterwards, as textures by themselves do not have a concept of backbuffers. Calling while rendering to a texture will still update the screen with any current drawing that has been done _to the window itself_.

member.param: the rendering context.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.FlushRenderer(System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_FlushRenderer(SDL_Renderer *renderer);

summary.para: Force the rendering context to flush any pending commands and state.
You do not need to (and in fact, shouldn't) call this function unless you are planning to call into OpenGL/Direct3D/Metal/whatever directly, in addition to using an SDL_Renderer.

This is for a very-specific case: if you are using SDL's render API, and you plan to make OpenGL/D3D/whatever calls in addition to SDL render API calls. If this applies, you should call this function between calls to SDL's render API and the low-level API you're using in cooperation.
In all other cases, you can ignore this function.

This call makes SDL flush any pending rendering work it was queueing up to do later in a single batch, and marks any internal cached state as invalid, so it'll prepare all its state again later, from scratch.

This means you do not need to save state in your rendering code to protect the SDL renderer. However, there lots of arbitrary pieces of Direct3D and OpenGL state that can confuse things; you should use your best judgment and be prepared to make changes if specific state needs to be protected.

member.param: the rendering context.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.GetRenderMetalLayer(System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC void * SDLCALL SDL_GetRenderMetalLayer(SDL_Renderer *renderer);

summary.para: Get the CAMetalLayer associated with the given Metal renderer.
This function returns void *, so SDL doesn't have to include Metal's headers, but it can be safely cast to a CAMetalLayer *.

member.param: the renderer to query.
member.returns: a CAMetalLayer * on success, or null if the renderer isn't a Metal renderer.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.GetRenderMetalCommandEncoder(System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC void * SDLCALL SDL_GetRenderMetalCommandEncoder(SDL_Renderer *renderer);

summary.para: Get the Metal command encoder for the current frame.
This function returns void *, so SDL doesn't have to include Metal's headers, but it can be safely cast to an id<MTLRenderCommandEncoder>.

This will return null if Metal refuses to give SDL a drawable to render to, which might happen if the window is hidden/minimized/offscreen. This doesn't apply to command encoders for render targets, just the window's backbuffer. Check your return values!

member.param: the renderer to query.
member.returns: an id<MTLRenderCommandEncoder> on success, or null if the renderer isn't a Metal renderer or there was an error.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.AddVulkanRenderSemaphores(System.IntPtr,System.UInt32,System.Int64,System.Int64)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_AddVulkanRenderSemaphores(SDL_Renderer *renderer, Uint32 wait_stage_mask, Sint64 wait_semaphore, Sint64 signal_semaphore);

summary.para: Add a set of synchronization semaphores for the current frame.
The Vulkan renderer will wait for waitSemaphore before submitting rendering commands and signal signalSemaphore after rendering commands are complete for this frame.

This should be called each frame that you want semaphore synchronization. The Vulkan renderer may have multiple frames in flight on the GPU, so you should have multiple semaphores that are used for synchronization. Querying will give you the maximum number of semaphores you'll need.

member.param: the rendering context.
the VkPipelineStageFlags for the wait.
a VkSempahore to wait on before rendering the current frame, or 0 if not needed.
a VkSempahore that SDL will signal when rendering for the current frame is complete, or 0 if not needed.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: It is NOT safe to call this function from two threads at once.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.SetRenderVSync(System.IntPtr,System.Int32)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_SetRenderVSync(SDL_Renderer *renderer, int vsync);

summary.para: Toggle VSync of the given renderer.
When a renderer is created, vsync defaults to .

The `vsync` parameter can be 1 to synchronize present with every vertical refresh, 2 to synchronize present with every second vertical refresh, etc., for late swap tearing (adaptive vsync), or to disable. Not every value is supported by every driver, so you should check the return value to see whether the requested setting is supported.

member.param: the renderer to toggle.
the vertical refresh sync interval.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.RenderDebugText(System.IntPtr,System.Single,System.Single,System.String)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_RenderDebugText(SDL_Renderer *renderer, float x, float y, const char *str);

summary.para: Draw debug text to an SDL_Renderer.

This function will render a string of text to an SDL_Renderer. Note that this is a convenience function for debugging, with severe limitations, and not intended to be used for production apps and games.
Among these limitations:

list.item: It accepts UTF-8 strings, but will only renders ASCII characters.
It has a single, tiny size (8x8 pixels). You can use logical presentation or to adjust it.
It uses a simple, hardcoded bitmap font. It does not allow different font selections and it does not support truetype, for proper scaling.
It does no word-wrapping and does not treat newline characters as a line break. If the text goes out of the window, it's gone.

summary.para: For serious text rendering, there are several good options, such as SDL_ttf, stb_truetype, or other external libraries.
On first use, this will create an internal texture for rendering glyphs. This texture will live until the renderer is destroyed.
The text is drawn in the color specified by .

member.param: the renderer which should draw a line of text.
the x coordinate where the top-left corner of the text will draw.
the y coordinate where the top-left corner of the text will draw.
the string to render.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.1.6.

For the full list of limitations and other useful information, see .

the x coordinate where the top-left corner of the text will draw.
the y coordinate where the top-left corner of the text will draw.
the format string to draw.

member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_Surface * SDLCALL SDL_CreateSurfaceFrom(int width, int height, SDL_PixelFormat format, void *pixels, int pitch);

summary.para: Allocate a new surface with a specific pixel format and existing pixel data.
No copy is made of the pixel data. Pixel data is not managed automatically; you must free the surface before you free the pixel data.
Pitch is the offset in bytes from one row of pixels to the next, e.g. width*4 for .
You may pass null for pixels and 0 for pitch to create a surface that you will fill in with valid values later.

member.param: the width of the surface.
the height of the surface.
the for the new surface's pixel format.
a pointer to existing pixel data.
the number of bytes between each row, including padding.
member.returns: the new structure that is created or null on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.GetSurfaceProperties(System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_PropertiesID SDLCALL SDL_GetSurfaceProperties(SDL_Surface *surface);

summary.para: Get the properties associated with a surface.
The following properties are understood by SDL:

list.item: : for HDR10 and floating point surfaces, this defines the value of 100% diffuse white, with higher values being displayed in the High Dynamic Range headroom. This defaults to 203 for HDR10 surfaces and 1.0 for floating point surfaces.
: for HDR10 and floating point surfaces, this defines the maximum dynamic range used by the content, in terms of the SDR white point. This defaults to 0.0, which disables tone mapping.
: for HDR10 and floating point surfaces, this defines the maximum dynamic range used by the content, in terms of the SDR white point. This defaults to 0.0, which disables tone mapping.

: the tone mapping operator used when compressing from a surface with high dynamic range to another with lower dynamic range. Currently this supports "chrome", which uses the same tone mapping that Chrome uses for HDR content, the form "*=N", where N is a floating point scale factor applied in linear space, and "none", which disables tone mapping. This defaults to "chrome".
: the hotspot pixel offset from the left edge of the image, if this surface is being used as a cursor.
: the hotspot pixel offset from the top edge of the image, if this surface is being used as a cursor.

: the number of degrees a surface's data is meant to be rotated clockwise to make the image right-side up. Default 0. This is used by the camera API, if a mobile device is oriented differently than what its camera provides (i.e. - the camera always provides portrait images but the phone is being held in landscape orientation). Since SDL 3.4.0.

member.param: the structure to query.
member.returns: a valid property ID on success or 0 on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.CreateSurfacePalette(System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_Palette * SDLCALL SDL_CreateSurfacePalette(SDL_Surface *surface);

summary.para: Create a palette and associate it with a surface.

This function creates a palette compatible with the provided surface. The palette is then returned for you to modify, and the surface will automatically use the new palette in future operations. You do not need to destroy the returned palette, it will be freed when the reference count reaches 0, usually when the surface is destroyed.
Bitmap surfaces (with format or ) will have the palette initialized with 0 as white and 1 as black. Other surfaces will get a palette initialized with white in every entry.
If this function is called for a surface that already has a palette, a new palette will be created to replace it.

member.param: the structure to update.
member.returns: a new structure on success or null on failure (e.g. if the surface didn't have an index format); call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function can be called on different threads with different surfaces.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_SetSurfacePalette(SDL_Surface *surface, SDL_Palette *palette);

Setting the palette keeps an internal reference to the palette, which can be safely destroyed afterwards.
A single palette can be shared with many surfaces.

member.param: the structure to update.
the structure to use.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function can be called on different threads with different surfaces
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.AddSurfaceAlternateImage(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_AddSurfaceAlternateImage(SDL_Surface *surface, SDL_Surface *image);

summary.para: Add an alternate version of a surface.

This function adds an alternate version of this surface, usually used for content with high DPI representations like cursors or icons. The size, format, and content do not need to match the original surface, and these alternate versions will not be updated when the original surface changes.
This function adds a reference to the alternate version, so you should call on the image after this call.

member.param: the structure to update.
a pointer to an alternate to associate with this surface.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function can be called on different threads with different surfaces.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

summary.para: Get an array including all versions of a surface.

Freeing the array of surfaces does not affect the surfaces in the array. They are still referenced by the surface being queried and will be cleaned up normally.

a pointer filled in with the number of surface pointers returned, may be null.
member.returns: a null terminated array of pointers or null on failure; call for more information. This should be freed with when it is no longer needed.
member.threadsafety: This function can be called on different threads with different surfaces.

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.LockSurface(System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_LockSurface(SDL_Surface *surface);

summary.para: Set up a surface for directly accessing the pixels.
Between calls to / , you can write to and read from Surface.Pixels, using the pixel format stored in Surface.Format. Once you are done accessing the surface, you should use to release it.
Not all surfaces require locking. If MustLock(surface) evaluates to 0, then you can read and write to the surface at any time, and the pixel format of the surface will not change.

member.param: the structure to be locked.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function can be called on different threads with different surfaces. The locking referred to by this function is making the pixels available for direct access, not thread-safe locking.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.UnlockSurface(System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC void SDLCALL SDL_UnlockSurface(SDL_Surface *surface);
member.summary: Release a surface after directly accessing the pixels.
member.param: the structure to be unlocked.
member.threadsafety: This function is not thread safe. The locking referred to by this function is making the pixels available for direct access, not thread-safe locking.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.SaveBMPIO(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr,System.Boolean)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_SaveBMP_IO(SDL_Surface *surface, SDL_IOStream *dst, bool closeio);

summary.para: Save a surface to a seekable SDL data stream in BMP format.

Surfaces with a 24-bit, 32-bit and paletted 8-bit format get saved in the BMP directly. Other RGB formats with 8-bit or higher get converted to a 24-bit surface or, if they have an alpha mask or a colorkey, to a 32-bit surface before they are saved. YUV and paletted 1-bit and 4-bit formats are not supported.

member.param: the structure containing the image to be saved.
a data stream to save to.
if true, calls on dst before returning, even in the case of an error.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function can be called on different threads with different surfaces.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.SetSurfaceRLE(System.IntPtr,System.Boolean)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_SetSurfaceRLE(SDL_Surface *surface, bool enabled);

summary.para: Set the RLE acceleration hint for a surface.
If RLE is enabled, color key and alpha blending blits are much faster, but the surface must be locked before directly accessing the pixels.

member.param: the structure to optimize.
true to enable RLE acceleration, false to disable it.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function can be called on different threads with different surfaces.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.returns: true if the surface is RLE enabled, false otherwise.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.SetSurfaceColorKey(System.IntPtr,System.Boolean,System.UInt32)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_SetSurfaceColorKey(SDL_Surface *surface, bool enabled, Uint32 key);

summary.para: Set the color key (transparent pixel) in a surface.

The color key defines a pixel value that will be treated as transparent in a blit. For example, one can use this to specify that cyan pixels should be considered transparent, and therefore not rendered.
It is a pixel of the format used by the surface, as generated by .

member.param: the structure to update.
true to enable color key, false to disable color key.
the transparent pixel.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function can be called on different threads with different surfaces.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.SetSurfaceClipRect(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_SetSurfaceClipRect(SDL_Surface *surface, const SDL_Rect *rect);

summary.para: Set the clipping rectangle for a surface.
When surface is the destination of a blit, only the area within the clip rectangle is drawn into.
Note that blits are automatically clipped to the edges of the source and destination surfaces.

member.param: the structure to be clipped.
the structure representing the clipping rectangle, or null to disable clipping.
member.returns: true if the rectangle intersects the surface, otherwise false and blits will be completely clipped.
member.threadsafety: This function can be called on different threads with different surfaces.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.RotateSurface(System.IntPtr,System.Single)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_Surface * SDLCALL SDL_RotateSurface(SDL_Surface *surface, float angle);
member.summary:
Return a copy of a surface rotated clockwise a number of degrees.
The angle of rotation can be negative for counter-clockwise rotation.
When the rotation isn't a multiple of 90 degrees, the resulting surface is larger than the original, with the background filled in with the colorkey, if available, or RGBA 255/255/255/0 if not.
If surface has the property set on it, the new copy will have the adjusted value set: if the rotation property is 90 and angle was 30, the new surface will have a property value of 60 (that is: to be upright vs gravity, this surface needs to rotate 60 more degrees). However, note that further rotations on the new surface in this example will produce unexpected results, since the image will have resized and padded to accommodate the not-90 degree angle.
member.param: the surface to rotate.
the rotation angle, in degrees.
member.returns: a rotated copy of the surface or null on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function can be called on different threads with different surfaces.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.4.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.DuplicateSurface(System.IntPtr)

If the original surface has alternate images, the new surface will have a reference to them as well.
The returned surface should be freed with .

member.threadsafety: This function can be called on different threads with different surfaces.

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.ConvertSurface(System.IntPtr,SDL3.SDL.PixelFormat)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_Surface * SDLCALL SDL_ConvertSurface(SDL_Surface *surface, SDL_PixelFormat format);

summary.para: Copy an existing surface to a new surface of the specified format.

This function is used to optimize images for faster repeat blitting. This is accomplished by converting the original and storing the result as a new surface. The new, optimized surface can then be used as the source for future blits, making them faster.
If you are converting to an indexed surface and want to map colors to a palette, you can use instead.
If the original surface has alternate images, the new surface will have a reference to them as well.

member.param: the existing structure to convert.
the new pixel format.
member.returns: the new structure that is created or null on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function can be called on different threads with different surfaces.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.ConvertSurfaceAndColorspace(System.IntPtr,SDL3.SDL.PixelFormat,System.IntPtr,SDL3.SDL.Colorspace,System.UInt32)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_Surface * SDLCALL SDL_ConvertSurfaceAndColorspace(SDL_Surface *surface, SDL_PixelFormat format, SDL_Palette *palette, SDL_Colorspace colorspace, SDL_PropertiesID props);

summary.para: Copy an existing surface to a new surface of the specified format and colorspace.
This function converts an existing surface to a new format and colorspace and returns the new surface. This will perform any pixel format and colorspace conversion needed.
If the original surface has alternate images, the new surface will have a reference to them as well.

member.param: the existing structure to convert.
the new pixel format.
an optional palette to use for indexed formats, may be null.
the new colorspace.
an SDL_PropertiesID with additional color properties, or 0.
member.returns: the new structure that is created or null on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function can be called on different threads with different surfaces.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

an value of the src pixels format.
a pointer to the source pixels.
the pitch of the source pixels, in bytes.
an value of the dst pixels format.
a pointer to be filled in with premultiplied pixel data.
the pitch of the destination pixels, in bytes.
true to convert from sRGB to linear space for the alpha multiplication, false to do multiplication in sRGB space.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: The same destination pixels should not be used from two threads at once. It is safe to use the same source pixels from multiple threads.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.ClearSurface(System.IntPtr,System.Single,System.Single,System.Single,System.Single)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_ClearSurface(SDL_Surface *surface, float r, float g, float b, float a);

summary.para: Clear a surface with a specific color, with floating point precision.
This function handles all surface formats, and ignores any clip rectangle.
If the surface is YUV, the color is assumed to be in the sRGB colorspace, otherwise the color is assumed to be in the colorspace of the surface.

member.param: the to clear.
the red component of the pixel, normally in the range 0-1.
the green component of the pixel, normally in the range 0-1.
the blue component of the pixel, normally in the range 0-1.
the alpha component of the pixel, normally in the range 0-1.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function can be called on different threads with different surfaces.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_FillSurfaceRect(SDL_Surface *dst, const SDL_Rect *rect, Uint32 color);

summary.para: Perform a fast fill of a rectangle with a specific color.

color should be a pixel of the format used by the surface, and can be generated by or . If the color value contains an alpha component then the destination is simply filled with that alpha information, no blending takes place.

member.param: the structure that is the drawing target.
the structure representing the rectangle to fill, or null to fill the entire surface.
the color to fill with.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function can be called on different threads with different surfaces.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.BlitSurface(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_BlitSurface(SDL_Surface *src, const SDL_Rect *srcrect, SDL_Surface *dst, const SDL_Rect *dstrect);

summary.para: Performs a fast blit from the source surface to the destination surface with clipping.
If either srcrect or dstrect are null, the entire surface (src or dst) is copied while ensuring clipping to dst.clip_rect.
The blit function should not be called on a locked surface.
The blit semantics for surfaces with and without blending and colorkey are defined as follows:
summary.code:
RGBA->RGB: Source surface blend mode set to SDL_BLENDMODE_BLEND: alpha-blend (using the source alpha-channel and per-surface alpha) SDL_SRCCOLORKEY ignored. Source surface blend mode set to SDL_BLENDMODE_NONE: copy RGB. if SDL_SRCCOLORKEY set, only copy the pixels that do not match the RGB values of the source color key, ignoring alpha in the comparison.
RGB->RGBA: Source surface blend mode set to SDL_BLENDMODE_BLEND: alpha-blend (using the source per-surface alpha) Source surface blend mode set to SDL_BLENDMODE_NONE: copy RGB, set destination alpha to source per-surface alpha value. both: if SDL_SRCCOLORKEY set, only copy the pixels that do not match the source color key.
RGBA->RGBA: Source surface blend mode set to SDL_BLENDMODE_BLEND: alpha-blend (using the source alpha-channel and per-surface alpha) SDL_SRCCOLORKEY ignored. Source surface blend mode set to SDL_BLENDMODE_NONE: copy all of RGBA to the destination. if SDL_SRCCOLORKEY set, only copy the pixels that do not match the RGB values of the source color key, ignoring alpha in the comparison.
RGB->RGB: Source surface blend mode set to SDL_BLENDMODE_BLEND: alpha-blend (using the source per-surface alpha) Source surface blend mode set to SDL_BLENDMODE_NONE: copy RGB. both: if SDL_SRCCOLORKEY set, only copy the pixels that do not match the source color key.

member.param: the structure to be copied from.
the structure representing the rectangle to be copied, or nullto copy the entire surface.
the structure that is the blit target.

the structure representing the x and y position in the destination surface, or null for (0,0). The width and height are ignored, and are copied from srcrect. If you want a specific width and height, you should use .
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: Only one thread should be using the src and dst surfaces at any given time.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.BlitSurface(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr,SDL3.SDL.Rect@)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_BlitSurface(SDL_Surface *src, const SDL_Rect *srcrect, SDL_Surface *dst, const SDL_Rect *dstrect);

summary.para: Performs a fast blit from the source surface to the destination surface.

This assumes that the source and destination rectangles are the same size. If either srcrect or dstrect are null, the entire surface (src or dst) is copied. The final blit rectangles are saved in srcrect and dstrect after all clipping is performed.
The blit semantics for surfaces with and without blending and colorkey are defined as follows:
summary.code:
RGBA->RGB: Source surface blend mode set to SDL_BLENDMODE_BLEND: alpha-blend (using the source alpha-channel and per-surface alpha) SDL_SRCCOLORKEY ignored. Source surface blend mode set to SDL_BLENDMODE_NONE: copy RGB. if SDL_SRCCOLORKEY set, only copy the pixels that do not match the RGB values of the source color key, ignoring alpha in the comparison.
RGB->RGBA: Source surface blend mode set to SDL_BLENDMODE_BLEND: alpha-blend (using the source per-surface alpha) Source surface blend mode set to SDL_BLENDMODE_NONE: copy RGB, set destination alpha to source per-surface alpha value. both: if SDL_SRCCOLORKEY set, only copy the pixels that do not match the source color key.
RGBA->RGBA: Source surface blend mode set to SDL_BLENDMODE_BLEND: alpha-blend (using the source alpha-channel and per-surface alpha) SDL_SRCCOLORKEY ignored. Source surface blend mode set to SDL_BLENDMODE_NONE: copy all of RGBA to the destination. if SDL_SRCCOLORKEY set, only copy the pixels that do not match the RGB values of the source color key, ignoring alpha in the comparison.
RGB->RGB: Source surface blend mode set to SDL_BLENDMODE_BLEND: alpha-blend (using the source per-surface alpha) Source surface blend mode set to SDL_BLENDMODE_NONE: copy RGB. both: if SDL_SRCCOLORKEY set, only copy the pixels that do not match the source color key.

member.param: the structure to be copied from.
the structure representing the rectangle to be copied, or nullto copy the entire surface.
the structure that is the blit target.

the structure representing the x and y position in the destination surface, or null for (0,0). The width and height are ignored, and are copied from srcrect. If you want a specific width and height, you should use .
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: Only one thread should be using the src and dst surfaces at any given time.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_BlitSurfaceTiledWithScale(SDL_Surface *src, const SDL_Rect *srcrect, float scale, SDL_ScaleMode scaleMode, SDL_Surface *dst, const SDL_Rect *dstrect);

summary.para: Perform a scaled and tiled blit to a destination surface, which may be of a different format.
The pixels in srcrect will be scaled and repeated as many times as needed to completely fill dstrect.

member.param: the structure to be copied from.
the structure representing the rectangle to be copied, or null to copy the entire surface.
the scale used to transform srcrect into the destination rectangle, e.g. a 32x32 texture with a scale of 2 would fill 64x64 tiles.
scale algorithm to be used.
the structure that is the blit target.
the structure representing the target rectangle in the destination surface, or null to fill the entire surface.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: Only one thread should be using the src and dst surfaces at any given time.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.BlitSurface9Grid(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Single,SDL3.SDL.ScaleMode,System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_BlitSurface9Grid(SDL_Surface *src, const SDL_Rect *srcrect, int left_width, int right_width, int top_height, int bottom_height, float scale, SDL_ScaleMode scaleMode, SDL_Surface *dst, const SDL_Rect *dstrect);

summary.para: Perform a scaled blit using the 9-grid algorithm to a destination surface, which may be of a different format.

The pixels in the source surface are split into a 3x3 grid, using the different corner sizes for each corner, and the sides and center making up the remaining pixels. The corners are then scaled using scale and fit into the corners of the destination rectangle. The sides and center are then stretched into place to cover the remaining destination rectangle.

member.param: the structure to be copied from.
the structure representing the rectangle to be used for the 9-grid, or null to use the entire surface.
the width, in pixels, of the left corners in srcrect.
the width, in pixels, of the right corners in srcrect.
the height, in pixels, of the top corners in srcrect.
the height, in pixels, of the bottom corners in srcrect.
the scale used to transform the corner of srcrect into the corner of dstrect, or 0.0f for an unscaled blit.
scale algorithm to be used.
the structure that is the blit target.
the structure representing the target rectangle in the destination surface, or null to fill the entire surface.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: Only one thread should be using the src and dst surfaces at any given time.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.MapSurfaceRGB(System.IntPtr,System.Byte,System.Byte,System.Byte)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC Uint32 SDLCALL SDL_MapSurfaceRGB(SDL_Surface *surface, Uint8 r, Uint8 g, Uint8 b);

summary.para: Map an RGB triple to an opaque pixel value for a surface.
This function maps the RGB color value to the specified pixel format and returns the pixel value best approximating the given RGB color value for the given pixel format.
If the surface has a palette, the index of the closest matching color in the palette will be returned.
If the surface pixel format has an alpha component it will be returned as all 1 bits (fully opaque).

If the pixel format bpp (color depth) is less than 32-bpp then the unused upper bits of the return value can safely be ignored (e.g., with a 16-bpp format the return value can be assigned to a Uint16, and similarly a Uint8 for an 8-bpp format).

member.param: the surface to use for the pixel format and palette.
the red component of the pixel in the range 0-255.
the green component of the pixel in the range 0-255.
the blue component of the pixel in the range 0-255.
member.returns: a pixel value.
member.threadsafety: This function can be called on different threads with different surfaces.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.MapSurfaceRGBA(System.IntPtr,System.Byte,System.Byte,System.Byte,System.Byte)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC Uint32 SDLCALL SDL_MapSurfaceRGBA(SDL_Surface *surface, Uint8 r, Uint8 g, Uint8 b, Uint8 a);

summary.para: Map an RGBA quadruple to a pixel value for a surface.
This function maps the RGBA color value to the specified pixel format and returns the pixel value best approximating the given RGBA color value for the given pixel format.
If the surface pixel format has no alpha component the alpha value will be ignored (as it will be in formats with a palette).
If the surface has a palette, the index of the closest matching color in the palette will be returned.

If the pixel format bpp (color depth) is less than 32-bpp then the unused upper bits of the return value can safely be ignored (e.g., with a 16-bpp format the return value can be assigned to a Uint16, and similarly a Uint8 for an 8-bpp format).

member.param: the surface to use for the pixel format and palette.
the red component of the pixel in the range 0-255.
the green component of the pixel in the range 0-255.
the blue component of the pixel in the range 0-255.
the alpha component of the pixel in the range 0-255.
member.returns: a pixel value.
member.threadsafety: This function can be called on different threads with different surfaces.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

summary.para: Retrieves a single pixel from a surface.
This function prioritizes correctness over speed: it is suitable for unit tests, but is not intended for use in a game engine.
Like , this uses the entire 0..255 range when converting color components from pixel formats with less than 8 bits per RGB component.

member.param: the surface to read.
the horizontal coordinate, 0 <= x < width.
the vertical coordinate, 0 <= y < height.
a pointer filled in with the red channel, 0-255, or null to ignore this channel.
a pointer filled in with the green channel, 0-255, or null to ignore this channel.
a pointer filled in with the blue channel, 0-255, or null to ignore this channel.
a pointer filled in with the alpha channel, 0-255, or null to ignore this channel.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function can be called on different threads with different surfaces.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: T:SDL3.SDL.Surface

summary.para: A collection of pixels used in software blitting.
Pixels are arranged in memory in rows, with the top row first. Each row occupies an amount of memory given by the pitch (sometimes known as the row stride in non-SDL APIs).

Within each row, pixels are arranged from left to right until the width is reached. Each pixel occupies a number of bits appropriate for its format, with most formats representing each pixel as one or more whole bytes (in some indexed formats, instead multiple pixels are packed into each byte), and a byte order given by the format. After encoding all pixels, any remaining bytes to reach the pitch are used as padding to reach a desired alignment, and have undefined contents.

When a surface holds YUV format data, the planes are assumed to be contiguous without padding between them, e.g. a 32x32 surface in NV12 format with a pitch of 32 would consist of 32x32 bytes of Y plane followed by 32x16 bytes of UV plane.
When a surface holds MJPG format data, pixels points at the compressed JPEG image and pitch is the length of that data.

member.since: This struct is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: T:SDL3.SDL.EGLAttribArrayCallback
member.code: typedef SDL_EGLAttrib *(SDLCALL *SDL_EGLAttribArrayCallback)(void *userdata);

summary.para: EGL platform attribute initialization callback.
This is called when SDL is attempting to create an EGL context, to let the app add extra attributes to its eglGetPlatformDisplay() call.
The callback should return a pointer to an EGL attribute array terminated with EGL_NONE. If this function returns null, the process will fail gracefully.
The returned pointer should be allocated with SDL_malloc() and will be passed to .
The arrays returned by each callback will be appended to the existing attribute arrays defined by SDL.

member.param: an app-controlled pointer that is passed to the callback.
member.returns: a newly-allocated array of attributes, terminated with EGL_NONE.
member.since: This datatype is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: T:SDL3.SDL.EGLIntArrayCallback
member.code: typedef SDL_EGLint *(SDLCALL *SDL_EGLIntArrayCallback)(void *userdata, SDL_EGLDisplay display, SDL_EGLConfig config);

summary.para: EGL surface/context attribute initialization callback types.
This is called when SDL is attempting to create an EGL surface, to let the app add extra attributes to its eglCreateWindowSurface() or eglCreateContext calls.
For convenience, the EGLDisplay and EGLConfig to use are provided to the callback.
The callback should return a pointer to an EGL attribute array terminated with EGL_NONE. If this function returns null, the process will fail gracefully.
The returned pointer should be allocated with SDL_malloc() and will be passed to .
The arrays returned by each callback will be appended to the existing attribute arrays defined by SDL.

member.param: an app-controlled pointer that is passed to the callback.
the EGL display to be used.
the EGL config to be used.
member.returns: a newly-allocated array of attributes, terminated with EGL_NONE.
member.since: This datatype is available since SDL 3.2.0

summary.para: An enumeration of OpenGL configuration attributes.

While you can set most OpenGL attributes normally, the attributes listed above must be known before SDL creates the window that will be used with the OpenGL context. These attributes are set and read with and .

In some cases, these attributes are minimum requests; the GL does not promise to give you exactly what you asked for. It's possible to ask for a 16-bit depth buffer and get a 24-bit one instead, for example, or to ask for no stencil buffer and still have one available. Context creation should fail if the GL can't provide your requested attributes at a minimum, but you should check to see exactly what you got.

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.GLAttr.RedSize
member.summary: the minimum number of bits for the red channel of the color buffer; defaults to 8.

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.GLAttr.GreenSize
member.summary: the minimum number of bits for the green channel of the color buffer; defaults to 8.

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.GLAttr.BlueSize
member.summary: the minimum number of bits for the blue channel of the color buffer; defaults to 8.

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.GLAttr.AlphaSize
member.summary: the minimum number of bits for the alpha channel of the color buffer; defaults to 8

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.GLAttr.BufferSize
member.summary: the minimum number of bits for frame buffer size; defaults to 0.

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.GLAttr.DoubleBuffer
member.summary: whether the output is single or double buffered; defaults to double buffering on.

member.name: F:SDL3.SDL.GLAttr.DepthSize
member.summary: the minimum number of bits in the depth buffer; defaults to 16.

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.GetVideoDriver(System.Int32)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC const char * SDLCALL SDL_GetVideoDriver(int index);

summary.para: Get the name of a built in video driver.
The video drivers are presented in the order in which they are normally checked during initialization.
The names of drivers are all simple, low-ASCII identifiers, like "cocoa", "x11" or "windows". These never have Unicode characters, and are not meant to be proper names.

member.param: the index of a video driver.
member.returns: the name of the video driver with the given index, or null if index is out of bounds.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC const char * SDLCALL SDL_GetCurrentVideoDriver(void);

summary.para: Get the name of the currently initialized video driver.
The names of drivers are all simple, low-ASCII identifiers, like "cocoa", "x11" or "windows". These never have Unicode characters, and are not meant to be proper names.

member.returns: the name of the current video driver or null if no driver has been initialized.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.GetDisplayProperties(System.UInt32)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_PropertiesID SDLCALL SDL_GetDisplayProperties(SDL_DisplayID displayID);

summary.para: Get the properties associated with a display.
The following read-only properties are provided by SDL:

list.item: : true if the display has HDR headroom above the SDR white point. This is for informational and diagnostic purposes only, as not all platforms provide this information at the display level.

summary.para: On KMS/DRM:

list.item: : the "panel orientation" property for the display in degrees of clockwise rotation. Note that this is provided only as a hint, and the application is responsible for any coordinate transformations needed to conform to the requested display orientation.

summary.para: On Wayland:

list.item: : the wl_output associated with the display

summary.para: On Windows:

list.item: : the monitor handle (HMONITOR) associated with the display

member.param: displayID the instance ID of the display to query.
member.returns: a valid property ID on success or 0 on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

The primary display is often located at (0,0), but may be placed at a different location depending on monitor layout.

the structure filled in with the display bounds.
member.returns: success on success or false on failure; call for more information.

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.GetDisplayUsableBounds(System.UInt32,SDL3.SDL.Rect@)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_GetDisplayUsableBounds(SDL_DisplayID displayID, SDL_Rect *rect);

summary.para: Get the usable desktop area represented by a display, in screen coordinates.
This is the same area as reports, but with portions reserved by the system removed. For example, on Apple's macOS, this subtracts the area occupied by the menu bar and dock.
Setting a window to be fullscreen generally bypasses these unusable areas, so these are good guidelines for the maximum space available to a non-fullscreen window.

member.param: the instance ID of the display to query.
the structure filled in with the display bounds.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0
