member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.GetDisplayContentScale(System.UInt32)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC float SDLCALL SDL_GetDisplayContentScale(SDL_DisplayID displayID);

summary.para: Get the content scale of a display.

The content scale is the expected scale for content based on the DPI settings of the display. For example, a 4K display might have a 2.0 (200%) display scale, which means that the user expects UI elements to be twice as big on this display, to aid in readability.

After window creation, should be used to query the content scale factor for individual windows instead of querying the display for a window and calling this function, as the per-window content scale factor may differ from the base value of the display it is on, particularly on high-DPI and/or multi-monitor desktop configurations.

member.param: the instance ID of the display to query.
member.returns: the content scale of the display, or 0.0f on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.GetFullscreenDisplayModes(System.UInt32,System.Int32@)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_DisplayMode ** SDLCALL SDL_GetFullscreenDisplayModes(SDL_DisplayID displayID, int *count);

summary.para: Get a list of fullscreen display modes available on a display.
The display modes are sorted in this priority:

list.item: w -> largest to smallest
h -> largest to smallest
bits per pixel -> more colors to fewer colors
packed pixel layout -> largest to smallest
refresh rate -> highest to lowest
pixel density -> lowest to highest

member.param: the instance ID of the display to query.
a pointer filled in with the number of display modes returned, may be null.
member.returns: a null terminated array of display mode pointers or null on failure; call for more information. This is a single allocation that should be freed with when it is no longer needed.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.GetClosestFullscreenDisplayMode(System.UInt32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Single,System.Boolean,SDL3.SDL.DisplayMode@)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_GetClosestFullscreenDisplayMode(SDL_DisplayID displayID, int w, int h, float refresh_rate, bool include_high_density_modes, SDL_DisplayMode *closest);

summary.para: Get the closest match to the requested display mode.

The available display modes are scanned and closest is filled in with the closest mode matching the requested mode and returned. The mode format and refresh rate default to the desktop mode if they are set to 0. The modes are scanned with size being first priority, format being second priority, and finally checking the refresh rate. If all the available modes are too small, then false is returned.

member.param: the instance ID of the display to query.
the width in pixels of the desired display mode.
the height in pixels of the desired display mode.
the refresh rate of the desired display mode, or 0.0f for the desktop refresh rate.
boolean to include high density modes in the search.
a pointer filled in with the closest display mode equal to or larger than the desired mode.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.GetDesktopDisplayMode(System.UInt32)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC const SDL_DisplayMode * SDLCALL SDL_GetDesktopDisplayMode(SDL_DisplayID displayID);

summary.para: Get information about the desktop's display mode.

There's a difference between this function and when SDL runs fullscreen and has changed the resolution. In that case this function will return the previous native display mode, and not the current display mode.

member.param: the instance ID of the display to query.
member.returns: a pointer to the desktop display mode or null on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.GetWindowPixelDensity(System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC float SDLCALL SDL_GetWindowPixelDensity(SDL_Window *window);

summary.para: Get the pixel density of a window.
This is a ratio of pixel size to window size. For example, if the window is 1920x1080 and it has a high density back buffer of 3840x2160 pixels, it would have a pixel density of 2.0.

member.param: the window to query.
member.returns: the pixel density or 0.0f on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.GetWindowDisplayScale(System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC float SDLCALL SDL_GetWindowDisplayScale(SDL_Window *window);

summary.para: Get the content display scale relative to a window's pixel size.

This is a combination of the window pixel density and the display content scale, and is the expected scale for displaying content in this window. For example, if a 3840x2160 window had a display scale of 2.0, the user expects the content to take twice as many pixels and be the same physical size as if it were being displayed in a 1920x1080 window with a display scale of 1.0.
Conceptually this value corresponds to the scale display setting, and is updated when that setting is changed, or the window moves to a display with a different scale setting.

member.param: the window to query.
member.returns: the display scale, or 0.0f on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.SetWindowFullscreenMode(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_SetWindowFullscreenMode(SDL_Window *window, const SDL_DisplayMode *mode);

summary.para: Set the display mode to use when a window is visible and fullscreen.
This only affects the display mode used when the window is fullscreen. To change the window size when the window is not fullscreen, use .

If the window is currently in the fullscreen state, this request is asynchronous on some windowing systems and the new mode dimensions may not be applied immediately upon the return of this function. If an immediate change is required, call to block until the changes have taken effect.
When the new mode takes effect, an and/or an event will be emitted with the new mode dimensions.

member.param: the window to affect.
a pointer to the display mode to use, which can be null for borderless fullscreen desktop mode, or one of the fullscreen modes returned by to set an exclusive fullscreen mode.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

summary.para: Set the display mode to use when a window is visible and fullscreen.
This only affects the display mode used when the window is fullscreen. To change the window size when the window is not fullscreen, use .

If the window is currently in the fullscreen state, this request is asynchronous on some windowing systems and the new mode dimensions may not be applied immediately upon the return of this function. If an immediate change is required, call to block until the changes have taken effect.
When the new mode takes effect, an and/or an event will be emitted with the new mode dimensions.

member.param: the window to affect.
a pointer to the display mode to use, which can be null for borderless fullscreen desktop mode, or one of the fullscreen modes returned by to set an exclusive fullscreen mode.

member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.CreateWindow(System.String,System.Int32,System.Int32,SDL3.SDL.WindowFlags)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_Window * SDLCALL SDL_CreateWindow(const char *title, int w, int h, SDL_WindowFlags flags);

summary.para: Create a window with the specified dimensions and flags.
The window size is a request and may be different than expected based on the desktop layout and window manager policies. Your application should be prepared to handle a window of any size.
flags may be any of the following OR'd together:

list.item: : fullscreen window at desktop resolution
: window usable with an OpenGL context
: window is not visible
: no window decoration
: window can be resized
: window is minimized
: window is maximized
: window has grabbed mouse focus
: window has input focus
: window has mouse focus
: window not created by SDL
: window is modal
: window uses high pixel density back buffer if possible
: window has mouse captured (unrelated to )
: window should always be above others
: window should be treated as a utility window, not showing in the task bar and window list
: window should be treated as a tooltip and does not get mouse or keyboard focus, requires a parent window
: window should be treated as a popup menu, requires a parent window
: window has grabbed keyboard input
: window usable with a Vulkan instance
: window usable with a Metal instance
: window with transparent buffer
: window should not be focusable

summary.para: The SDL_Window will be shown if is not set. If hidden at creation time, can be used to show it later.
On Apple's macOS, you must set the NSHighResolutionCapable Info.plist property to YES, otherwise you will not receive a High-DPI OpenGL canvas.

The window pixel size may differ from its window coordinate size if the window is on a high pixel density display. Use to query the client area's size in window coordinates, and or to query the drawable size in pixels. Note that the drawable size can vary after the window is created and should be queried again if you get an event.
If the window is created with any of the or flags, then the corresponding LoadLibrary function ( or ) is called and the corresponding UnloadLibrary function is called by .
If is specified and there isn't a working Vulkan driver, will fail, because will fail.
If is specified on an OS that does not support Metal, will fail.
If you intend to use this window with an SDL_Renderer, you should use instead of this function, to avoid window flicker.
On non-Apple devices, SDL requires you to either not link to the Vulkan loader or link to a dynamic library version. This limitation may be removed in a future version of SDL.

member.param: the title of the window, in UTF-8 encoding.
the width of the window.
the height of the window.
0, or one or more OR'd together.
member.returns: the window that was created or null on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.CreatePopupWindow(System.IntPtr,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,SDL3.SDL.WindowFlags)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_Window * SDLCALL SDL_CreatePopupWindow(SDL_Window *parent, int offset_x, int offset_y, int w, int h, SDL_WindowFlags flags);

summary.para: Create a child popup window of the specified parent window.
The window size is a request and may be different than expected based on the desktop layout and window manager policies. Your application should be prepared to handle a window of any size.
The flags parameter **must** contain at least one of the following:

list.item: : The popup window is a tooltip and will not pass any input events.
: The popup window is a popup menu. The topmost popup menu will implicitly gain the keyboard focus.

summary.para: The following flags are not relevant to popup window creation and will be ignored:
The following flags are incompatible with popup window creation and will cause it to fail:
The parent parameter must be non-null and a valid window. The parent of a popup window can be either a regular, toplevel window, or another popup window.

Popup windows cannot be minimized, maximized, made fullscreen, raised, flash, be made a modal window, be the parent of a toplevel window, or grab the mouse and/or keyboard. Attempts to do so will fail.
Popup windows implicitly do not have a border/decorations and do not appear on the taskbar/dock or in lists of windows such as alt-tab menus.
By default, popup window positions will automatically be constrained to keep the entire window within display bounds. This can be overridden with the property.

By default, popup menus will automatically grab keyboard focus from the parent when shown. This behavior can be overridden by setting the flag, setting the property to false, or toggling it after creation via the function.

If a parent window is hidden or destroyed, any child popup windows will be recursively hidden or destroyed as well. Child popup windows not explicitly hidden will be restored when the parent is shown.

member.param: the parent of the window, must not be null.
the x position of the popup window relative to the origin of the parent.
the y position of the popup window relative to the origin of the parent window.
the width of the window.
the height of the window.
or , and zero or more additional OR'd together.
member.returns: the window that was created or null on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.CreateWindowWithProperties(System.UInt32)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_Window * SDLCALL SDL_CreateWindowWithProperties(SDL_PropertiesID props);

summary.para: Create a window with the specified properties.
These are the supported properties:

list.item: : true if the window should be always on top
: true if the window has no window decoration
: true if the window will be used with an externally managed graphics context.
: true if the window should accept keyboard input (defaults true)
: true if the window should start in fullscreen mode at desktop resolution
: the height of the window
: true if the window should start hidden
: true if the window uses a high pixel density buffer if possible
: true if the window should start maximized
: true if the window is a popup menu
: true if the window will be used with Metal rendering
: true if the window should start minimized
: true if the window is modal to its parent
: true if the window starts with grabbed mouse focus
: true if the window will be used with OpenGL rendering
: an SDL_Window that will be the parent of this window, required for windows with the "tooltip," "menu", and "modal" properties
: true if the window should be resizable
: the title of the window, in UTF-8 encoding
: true if the window show transparent in the areas with alpha of 0
: true if the window is a tooltip
: true if the window is a utility window, not showing in the task bar and window list
: true if the window will be used with Vulkan rendering
: the width of the window
: the x position of the window, or , defaults to . This is relative to the parent for windows with the "tooltip" or "menu" property set.
: the y position of the window, or , defaults to . This is relative to the parent for windows with the "tooltip" or "menu" property set.

summary.para: These are additional supported properties on macOS:

list.item: : the (__unsafe_unretained) NSWindow associated with the window, if you want to wrap an existing window.
: the (__unsafe_unretained) NSView associated with the window, defaults to [window contentView]

summary.para: These are additional supported properties on iOS, tvOS, and visionOS:

list.item: : the (__unsafe_unretained) UIWindowScene associated with the window, defaults to the active window scene.

summary.para: These are additional supported properties on Wayland:

list.item: - true if the application wants to use the Wayland surface for a custom role and does not want it attached to an XDG toplevel window. See [README-wayland](README-wayland) for more information on using custom surfaces.
- true if the application wants an associated wl_egl_window object to be created and attached to the window, even if the window does not have the OpenGL property or flag set.
- the wl_surface associated with the window, if you want to wrap an existing window. See [README-wayland](README-wayland) for more information.

summary.para: These are additional supported properties on Windows:

list.item: : the HWND associated with the window, if you want to wrap an existing window.
`: optional, another window to share pixel format with, useful for OpenGL windows

summary.para: These are additional supported properties with X11:

list.item: : the X11 Window associated with the window, if you want to wrap an existing window.

summary.para: The window is implicitly shown if the "hidden" property is not set.
These are additional supported properties with Emscripten:

list.item: : the id given to the canvas element. This should start with a # sign
: override the binding element for keyboard inputs for this canvas. The variable can be one of:
"#window": the javascript window object (default)
"#document": the javascript document object
"#screen": the javascript window.screen object
"#canvas": the WebGL canvas element
"#none": Don't bind anything at all

any other string without a leading # sign applies to the element on the page with that ID. Windows with the "tooltip" and "menu" properties are popup windows and have the behaviors and guidelines outlined in .

summary.para: Windows with the "tooltip" and "menu" properties are popup windows and have the behaviors and guidelines outlined in .

If this window is being created to be used with an SDL_Renderer, you should not add a graphics API specific property (, etc), as SDL will handle that internally when it chooses a renderer. However, SDL might need to recreate your window at that point, which may cause the window to appear briefly, and then flicker as it is recreated. The correct approach to this is to create the window with the property set to true, then create the renderer, then show the window with .

member.param: the properties to use.
member.returns: the window that was created or null on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.SetWindowIcon(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_SetWindowIcon(SDL_Window *window, SDL_Surface *icon);

summary.para: Set the icon for a window.

If this function is passed a surface with alternate representations added using , the surface will be interpreted as the content to be used for 100% display scale, and the alternate representations will be used for high DPI situations. For example, if the original surface is 32x32, then on a 2x macOS display or 200% display scale on Windows, a 64x64 version of the image will be used, if available. If a matching version of the image isn't available, the closest larger size image will be downscaled to the appropriate size and be used instead, if available. Otherwise, the closest smaller image will be upscaled and be used instead.

member.param: the window to change.
an structure containing the icon for the window.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.SetWindowPosition(System.IntPtr,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_SetWindowPosition(SDL_Window *window, int x, int y);

summary.para: Request that the window's position be set.
If the window is in an exclusive fullscreen or maximized state, this request has no effect.

This can be used to reposition fullscreen-desktop windows onto a different display, however, as exclusive fullscreen windows are locked to a specific display, they can only be repositioned programmatically via .

On some windowing systems this request is asynchronous and the new coordinates may not have have been applied immediately upon the return of this function. If an immediate change is required, call to block until the changes have taken effect.

When the window position changes, an event will be emitted with the window's new coordinates. Note that the new coordinates may not match the exact coordinates requested, as some windowing systems can restrict the position of the window in certain scenarios (e.g. constraining the position so the window is always within desktop bounds). Additionally, as this is just a request, it can be denied by the windowing system.

member.param: the window to reposition.
the x coordinate of the window, or or .
the y coordinate of the window, or or .
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_GetWindowPosition(SDL_Window *window, int *x, int *y);

This is the current position of the window as last reported by the windowing system.

a pointer filled in with the x position of the window, may be null.

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.SetWindowSize(System.IntPtr,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_SetWindowSize(SDL_Window *window, int w, int h);

summary.para: Request that the size of a window's client area be set.
If the window is in a fullscreen or maximized state, this request has no effect.
To change the exclusive fullscreen mode of a window, use

On some windowing systems, this request is asynchronous and the new window size may not have have been applied immediately upon the return of this function. If an immediate change is required, call to block until the changes have taken effect.

When the window size changes, an event will be emitted with the new window dimensions. Note that the new dimensions may not match the exact size requested, as some windowing systems can restrict the window size in certain scenarios (e.g. constraining the size of the content area to remain within the usable desktop bounds). Additionally, as this is just a request, it can be denied by the windowing system.

member.param: the window to change.
the width of the window, must be > 0.
the height of the window, must be > 0.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

The window pixel size may differ from its window coordinate size if the window is on a high pixel density display. Use or to get the real client area size in pixels.

a pointer filled in with the width of the window, may be null

member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_GetWindowSafeArea(SDL_Window *window, SDL_Rect *rect);

summary.para: Get the safe area for this window.

Some devices have portions of the screen which are partially obscured or not interactive, possibly due to on-screen controls, curved edges, camera notches, TV overscan, etc. This function provides the area of the window which is safe to have interactable content. You should continue rendering into the rest of the window, but it should not contain visually important or interactable content.

member.param: the window to query.
a pointer filled in with the client area that is safe for interactive content.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.SetWindowAspectRatio(System.IntPtr,System.Single,System.Single)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_SetWindowAspectRatio(SDL_Window *window, float min_aspect, float max_aspect);

summary.para: Request that the aspect ratio of a window's client area be set.
The aspect ratio is the ratio of width divided by height, e.g. 2560x1600 would be 1.6. Larger aspect ratios are wider and smaller aspect ratios are narrower.
If, at the time of this request, the window in a fixed-size state, such as maximized or fullscreen, the request will be deferred until the window exits this state and becomes resizable again.

On some windowing systems, this request is asynchronous and the new window aspect ratio may not have have been applied immediately upon the return of this function. If an immediate change is required, call to block until the changes have taken effect.

When the window size changes, an event will be emitted with the new window dimensions. Note that the new dimensions may not match the exact aspect ratio requested, as some windowing systems can restrict the window size in certain scenarios (e.g. constraining the size of the content area to remain within the usable desktop bounds). Additionally, as this is just a request, it can be denied by the windowing system.

member.param: the window to change.
the minimum aspect ratio of the window, or 0.0f for no limit.
the maximum aspect ratio of the window, or 0.0f for no limit.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

summary.para: Get the size of a window's borders (decorations) around the client area.
Note: If this function fails (returns false), the size values will be initialized to 0, 0, 0, 0 (if a non-NULL pointer is provided), as if the window in question was borderless.

Note: This function may fail on systems where the window has not yet been decorated by the display server (for example, immediately after calling ). It is recommended that you wait at least until the window has been presented and composited, so that the window system has a chance to decorate the window and provide the border dimensions to SDL.
This function also returns false if getting the information is not supported.

You can't change the resizable state of a fullscreen window.

true to allow resizing, false to disallow.

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.SetWindowFillDocument(System.IntPtr,System.Boolean)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_SetWindowFillDocument(SDL_Window *window, bool fill);
member.summary:
Set the window to fill the current document space (Emscripten only).
This will add or remove the window's flag.
Currently this flag only applies to the Emscripten target.
When enabled, the canvas element fills the entire document. Resize events will be generated as the browser window is resized, as that will adjust the canvas size as well. The canvas will cover anything else on the page, including any controls provided by Emscripten in its generated HTML file (in fact, any elements on the page that aren't the canvas will be moved into a hidden div element). ///
Often times this is desirable for a browser-based game, but it means several things that we expect of an SDL window on other platforms might not work as expected, such as minimum window sizes and aspect ratios.
member.param: the window of which to change the fill-document state.
true to set the window to fill the document, false to disable.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.4.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.RaiseWindow(System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_RaiseWindow(SDL_Window *window);

summary.para: Request that a window be raised above other windows and gain the input focus.

The result of this request is subject to desktop window manager policy, particularly if raising the requested window would result in stealing focus from another application. If the window is successfully raised and gains input focus, an event will be emitted, and the window will have the flag set.

member.param: the window to raise.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.MaximizeWindow(System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_MaximizeWindow(SDL_Window *window);

summary.para: Request that the window be made as large as possible.
Non-resizable windows can't be maximized. The window must have the flag set, or this will have no effect.

On some windowing systems this request is asynchronous and the new window state may not have have been applied immediately upon the return of this function. If an immediate change is required, call to block until the changes have taken effect.
When the window state changes, an event will be emitted. Note that, as this is just a request, the windowing system can deny the state change.

When maximizing a window, whether the constraints set via are honored depends on the policy of the window manager. Win32 and macOS enforce the constraints when maximizing, while X11 and Wayland window managers may vary.

member.param: the window to maximize.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.MinimizeWindow(System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_MinimizeWindow(SDL_Window *window);

summary.para: Request that the window be minimized to an iconic representation.
If the window is in a fullscreen state, this request has no direct effect. It may alter the state the window is returned to when leaving fullscreen.

On some windowing systems this request is asynchronous and the new window state may not have been applied immediately upon the return of this function. If an immediate change is required, call to block until the changes have taken effect.
When the window state changes, an event will be emitted. Note that, as this is just a request, the windowing system can deny the state change.

member.param: the window to minimize.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.RestoreWindow(System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_RestoreWindow(SDL_Window *window);

summary.para: Request that the size and position of a minimized or maximized window be restored.
If the window is in a fullscreen state, this request has no direct effect. It may alter the state the window is returned to when leaving fullscreen.

On some windowing systems this request is asynchronous and the new window state may not have have been applied immediately upon the return of this function. If an immediate change is required, call SDL_SyncWindow() to block until the changes have taken effect.
When the window state changes, an event will be emitted. Note that, as this is just a request, the windowing system can deny the state change.

member.param: the window to restore.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.SetWindowFullscreen(System.IntPtr,System.Boolean)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_SetWindowFullscreen(SDL_Window *window, bool fullscreen);

summary.para: Request that the window's fullscreen state be changed.
By default a window in fullscreen state uses borderless fullscreen desktop mode, but a specific exclusive display mode can be set using .

On some windowing systems this request is asynchronous and the new fullscreen state may not have have been applied immediately upon the return of this function. If an immediate change is required, call to block until the changes have taken effect.
When the window state changes, an or event will be emitted. Note that, as this is just a request, it can be denied by the windowing system.

member.param: the window to change.
true for fullscreen mode, false for windowed mode.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.SyncWindow(System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_SyncWindow(SDL_Window *window);

summary.para: Block until any pending window state is finalized.

On asynchronous windowing systems, this acts as a synchronization barrier for pending window state. It will attempt to wait until any pending window state has been applied and is guaranteed to return within finite time. Note that for how long it can potentially block depends on the underlying window system, as window state changes may involve somewhat lengthy animations that must complete before the window is in its final requested state.
On windowing systems where changes are immediate, this does nothing.

member.param: the window for which to wait for the pending state to be applied.
member.returns: true on success or false if the operation timed out before the window was in the requested state.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.GetWindowSurface(System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_Surface * SDLCALL SDL_GetWindowSurface(SDL_Window *window);

summary.para: Get the SDL surface associated with the window.
A new surface will be created with the optimal format for the window, if necessary. This surface will be freed when the window is destroyed. Do not free this surface.
This surface will be invalidated if the window is resized. After resizing a window this function must be called again to return a valid surface.
You may not combine this with 3D or the rendering API on this window.
This function is affected by .

member.param: the window to query.
member.returns: the surface associated with the window, or null on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.SetWindowSurfaceVSync(System.IntPtr,System.Int32)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_SetWindowSurfaceVSync(SDL_Window *window, int vsync);

summary.para: Toggle VSync for the window surface.
When a window surface is created, vsync defaults to

The vsync parameter can be 1 to synchronize present with every vertical refresh, 2 to synchronize present with every second vertical refresh, etc., for late swap tearing (adaptive vsync), or to disable. Not every value is supported by every driver, so you should check the return value to see whether the requested setting is supported.

member.param: the window.
the vertical refresh sync interval.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.SetWindowKeyboardGrab(System.IntPtr,System.Boolean)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_SetWindowKeyboardGrab(SDL_Window *window, bool grabbed);

summary.para: Set a window's keyboard grab mode.

Keyboard grab enables capture of system keyboard shortcuts like Alt+Tab or the Meta/Super key. Note that not all system keyboard shortcuts can be captured by applications (one example is Ctrl+Alt+Del on Windows).
This is primarily intended for specialized applications such as VNC clients or VM frontends. Normal games should not use keyboard grab.

When keyboard grab is enabled, SDL will continue to handle Alt+Tab when the window is full-screen to ensure the user is not trapped in your application. If you have a custom keyboard shortcut to exit fullscreen mode, you may suppress this behavior with .
If the caller enables a grab while another window is currently grabbed, the other window loses its grab in favor of the caller's window.

member.param: the window for which the keyboard grab mode should be set.
this is true to grab keyboard, and false to release.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.SetWindowParent(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_SetWindowParent(SDL_Window *window, SDL_Window *parent);

summary.para: Set the window as a child of a parent window.
If the window is already the child of an existing window, it will be reparented to the new owner. Setting the parent window to null unparents the window and removes child window status.

If a parent window is hidden or destroyed, the operation will be recursively applied to child windows. Child windows hidden with the parent that did not have their hidden status explicitly set will be restored when the parent is shown.
Attempting to set the parent of a window that is currently in the modal state will fail. Use to cancel the modal status before attempting to change the parent.
Popup windows cannot change parents and attempts to do so will fail.
Setting a parent window that is currently the sibling or descendent of the child window results in undefined behavior.

member.param: the window that should become the child of a parent.
the new parent window for the child window.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

To enable modal status on a window, the window must currently be the child window of a parent, or toggling modal status on will fail.

true to toggle modal status on, false to toggle it off.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.SetWindowHitTest(System.IntPtr,SDL3.SDL.HitTest,System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_SetWindowHitTest(SDL_Window *window, SDL_HitTest callback, void *callback_data);

summary.para: Provide a callback that decides if a window region has special properties.

Normally windows are dragged and resized by decorations provided by the system window manager (a title bar, borders, etc), but for some apps, it makes sense to drag them from somewhere else inside the window itself; for example, one might have a borderless window that wants to be draggable from any part, or simulate its own title bar, etc.

This function lets the app provide a callback that designates pieces of a given window as special. This callback is run during event processing if we need to tell the OS to treat a region of the window specially; the use of this callback is known as "hit testing."

Mouse input may not be delivered to your application if it is within a special area; the OS will often apply that input to moving the window or resizing the window and not deliver it to the application.
Specifying null for a callback disables hit-testing. Hit-testing is disabled by default.
Platforms that don't support this functionality will return false unconditionally, even if you're attempting to disable hit-testing.

Your callback may fire at any time, and its firing does not indicate any specific behavior (for example, on Windows, this certainly might fire when the OS is deciding whether to drag your window, but it fires for lots of other reasons, too, some unrelated to anything you probably care about _and when the mouse isn't actually at the location it is testing_). Since this can fire at any time, you should try to keep your callback efficient, devoid of allocations, etc.

member.param: the window to set hit-testing on.
the function to call when doing a hit-test.
an app-defined void pointer passed to callback.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for mor information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.SetWindowShape(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_SetWindowShape(SDL_Window *window, SDL_Surface *shape);

summary.para: Set the shape of a transparent window.

This sets the alpha channel of a transparent window and any fully transparent areas are also transparent to mouse clicks. If you are using something besides the SDL render API, then you are responsible for drawing the alpha channel of the window to match the shape alpha channel to get consistent cross-platform results.

The shape is copied inside this function, so you can free it afterwards. If your shape surface changes, you should call again to update the window. This is an expensive operation, so should be done sparingly.
The window must have been created with the flag.

member.param: the window.
the surface representing the shape of the window, or null to remove any current shape.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.GLLoadLibrary(System.String)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_GL_LoadLibrary(const char *path);

summary.para: Dynamically load an OpenGL library.

This should be done after initializing the video driver, but before creating any OpenGL windows. If no OpenGL library is loaded, the default library will be loaded upon creation of the first OpenGL window.
If you do this, you need to retrieve all of the GL functions used in your program from the dynamic library using .

member.param: the platform dependent OpenGL library name, or null to open the default OpenGL library.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.GLGetProcAddress(System.String)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_FunctionPointer SDLCALL SDL_GL_GetProcAddress(const char *proc);

summary.para: Get an OpenGL function by name.
If the GL library is loaded at runtime with , then all GL functions must be retrieved this way. Usually this is used to retrieve function pointers to OpenGL extensions.

list.item: On Windows, function pointers are specific to the current GL context; this means you need to have created a GL context and made it current before calling . If you recreate your context or create a second context, you should assume that any existing function pointers aren't valid to use with it. This is (currently) a Windows-specific limitation, and in practice lots of drivers don't suffer this limitation, but it is still the way the wgl API is documented to work and you should expect crashes if you don't respect it. Store a copy of the function pointers that comes and goes with context lifespan.

On X11, function pointers returned by this function are valid for any context, and can even be looked up before a context is created at all. This means that, for at least some common OpenGL implementations, if you look up a function that doesn't exist, you'll get a non-NULL result that is _NOT_ safe to call. You must always make sure the function is actually available for a given GL context before calling it, by checking for the existence of the appropriate extension with , or verifying that the version of OpenGL you're using offers the function as core functionality.

Some OpenGL drivers, on all platforms, will return nullif a function isn't supported, but you can't count on this behavior. Check for extensions you use, and if you get a null anyway, act as if that extension wasn't available. This is probably a bug in the driver, but you can code defensively for this scenario anyhow.
Just because you're on Linux/Unix, don't assume you'll be using X11. Next-gen display servers are waiting to replace it, and may or may not make the same promises about function pointers.

OpenGL function pointers must be declared APIENTRY as in the example code. This will ensure the proper calling convention is followed on platforms where this matters (Win32) thereby avoiding stack corruption.

member.param: the name of an OpenGL function.
member.returns: a pointer to the named OpenGL function. The returned pointer should be cast to the appropriate function signature.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.EGLGetProcAddress(System.String)

If an EGL library is loaded, this function allows applications to get entry points for EGL functions. This is useful to provide to an EGL API and extension loader.

member.returns: a pointer to the named EGL function. The returned pointer should be cast to the appropriate function signature.

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.GLExtensionSupported(System.String)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_GL_ExtensionSupported(const char *extension);

summary.para: Check if an OpenGL extension is supported for the current context.

This function operates on the current GL context; you must have created a context and it must be current before calling this function. Do not assume that all contexts you create will have the same set of extensions available, or that recreating an existing context will offer the same extensions again.

While it's probably not a massive overhead, this function is not an O(1) operation. Check the extensions you care about after creating the GL context and save that information somewhere instead of calling the function every time you need to know.

member.param: the name of the extension to check.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.returns: true if the extension is supported, false otherwise.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

summary.para: Create an OpenGL context for an OpenGL window, and make it current.
The OpenGL context will be created with the current states set through .
The SDL_Window specified must have been created with the flag, or context creation will fail.

Windows users new to OpenGL should note that, for historical reasons, GL functions added after OpenGL version 1.1 are not available by default. Those functions must be loaded at run-time, either with an OpenGL extension-handling library or with and its related functions.
SDL_GLContext is opaque to the application.

member.returns: the OpenGL context associated with window or null on failure; call for more information.

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.GLSetSwapInterval(System.Int32)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_GL_SetSwapInterval(int interval);

summary.para: Set the swap interval for the current OpenGL context.

Some systems allow specifying -1 for the interval, to enable adaptive vsync. Adaptive vsync works the same as vsync, but if you've already missed the vertical retrace for a given frame, it swaps buffers immediately, which might be less jarring for the user during occasional framerate drops. If an application requests adaptive vsync and the system does not support it, this function will fail and return false. In such a case, you should probably retry the call with 1 for the interval.
Adaptive vsync is implemented for some glX drivers with GLX_EXT_swap_control_tear, and for some Windows drivers with WGL_EXT_swap_control_tear.
Read more on the Khronos wiki: https://www.khronos.org/opengl/wiki/Swap_Interval#Adaptive_Vsync

member.param: 0 for immediate updates, 1 for updates synchronized with the vertical retrace, -1 for adaptive vsync.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.GLGetSwapInterval(System.Int32@)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_GL_GetSwapInterval(int *interval);

summary.para: Get the swap interval for the current OpenGL context.
If the system can't determine the swap interval, or there isn't a valid current context, this function will set *interval to 0 as a safe default.

member.param: output interval value. 0 if there is no vertical retrace synchronization, 1 if the buffer swap is synchronized with the vertical retrace, and -1 if late swaps happen immediately instead of waiting for the next retrace.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

summary.para: Update a window with OpenGL rendering.
This is used with double-buffered OpenGL contexts, which are the default.

On macOS, make sure you bind 0 to the draw framebuffer before swapping the window, otherwise nothing will happen. If you aren't using glBindFramebuffer(), this is the default and you won't have to do anything extra.

member.param: the window to change.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should only be called on the main thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: T:SDL3.SDL.WindowFlags

summary.para: The flags on a window.

These cover a lot of true/false, or on/off, window state. Some of it is immutable after being set through , some of it can be changed on existing windows by the app, and some of it might be altered by the user or system outside of the app's control.

When creating windows with , SDL will constrain resizable windows to the dimensions recommended by the compositor to fit it within the usable desktop space, although some compositors will do this automatically without intervention as well. Use after creation instead if you wish to create a window with a specific size.

member.since: This datatype is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.VulkanLoadLibrary(System.String)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL SDL_Vulkan_LoadLibrary(const char *path);

summary.para: Dynamically load the Vulkan loader library.

This should be called after initializing the video driver, but before creating any Vulkan windows. If no Vulkan loader library is loaded, the default library will be loaded upon creation of the first Vulkan window.

SDL keeps a counter of how many times this function has been successfully called, so it is safe to call this function multiple times, so long as it is eventually paired with an equivalent number of calls to . The path argument is ignored unless there is no library currently loaded, and and the library isn't actually unloaded until there have been an equivalent number of calls to .

It is fairly common for Vulkan applications to link with libvulkan instead of explicitly loading it at run time. This will work with SDL provided the application links to a dynamic library and both it and SDL use the same search path.

If you specify a non-NULL path, an application should retrieve all of the Vulkan functions it uses from the dynamic library using unless you can guarantee path points to the same vulkan loader library the application linked to.

On Apple devices, if path is NULL, SDL will attempt to find the vkGetInstanceProcAddr address within all the Mach-O images of the current process. This is because it is fairly common for Vulkan applications to link with libvulkan (and historically MoltenVK was provided as a static library). If it is not found, on macOS, SDL will attempt to load vulkan.framework/vulkan, libvulkan.1.dylib, MoltenVK.framework/MoltenVK, and libMoltenVK.dylib, in that order. On iOS, SDL will attempt to load libMoltenVK.dylib. Applications using a dynamic framework or .dylib must ensure it is included in its application bundle.
On non-Apple devices, application linking with a static libvulkan is not supported. Either do not link to the Vulkan loader or link to a dynamic library version.

member.param: the platform dependent Vulkan loader library name or null.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

The actual type of the returned function pointer is PFN_vkGetInstanceProcAddr, but that isn't available because the Vulkan headers are not included here. You should cast the return value of this function to that type, e.g.

member.returns: the function pointer for vkGetInstanceProcAddr or null on failure; call for more information.

member.name: M:SDL3.SDL.VulkanGetInstanceExtensions(System.UInt32@)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC char const * const * SDLCALL SDL_Vulkan_GetInstanceExtensions(Uint32 *count);

summary.para: Get the Vulkan instance extensions needed for vkCreateInstance.
his should be called after either calling or creating an SDL_Window with the flag.

On return, the variable pointed to by count will be set to the number of elements returned, suitable for using with VkInstanceCreateInfo::enabledExtensionCount, and the returned array can be used with VkInstanceCreateInfo::ppEnabledExtensionNames, for calling Vulkan's vkCreateInstance API.
You should not free the returned array; it is owned by SDL.

member.param: a pointer filled in with the number of extensions returned.
member.returns: an array of extension name strings on success, null on failure; call for more information.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

summary.para: Create a Vulkan rendering surface for a window.
The window must have been created with the flag and instance must have been created with extensions returned by enabled.
If allocator is null, Vulkan will use the system default allocator. This argument is passed directly to Vulkan and isn't used by SDL itself.

the Vulkan instance handle.
a VkAllocationCallbacks struct, which lets the app set the allocator that creates the surface. Can be null.
a pointer to a VkSurfaceKHR handle to output the newly created surface.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.since: This function is available since SDL 3.2.0

member.summary:
Compile to DXBC bytecode from HLSL code via a SPIRV-Cross round trip.
You must the returned buffer once you are done with it.
These are the optional properties that can be used:
: allows debug info to be emitted when relevant. Should only be used with debugging tools like Renderdoc.
: a UTF-8 name to be used with the shader. Relevant for use with debugging tools like Renderdoc.
: When true, indicates that the compiler should not cull unused shader resources. This behavior is disabled by default.
member.param: a struct describing the shader to transpile.
filled in with the bytecode buffer size.
member.returns: an SDL_malloc'd buffer containing DXBC bytecode.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.

member.name: T:SDL3.TTF.HintingFlags

summary.para: Hinting flags for TTF (TrueType Fonts)
This enum specifies the level of hinting to be applied to the font rendering. The hinting level determines how much the font's outlines are adjusted for better alignment on the pixel grid.

member.since: his enum is available since SDL_ttf 3.0.0.

member.name: F:SDL3.TTF.HintingFlags.Normal
member.summary: Normal hinting applies standard grid-fitting.

member.name: F:SDL3.TTF.HintingFlags.Light
member.summary: Light hinting applies subtle adjustments to improve rendering.

member.name: F:SDL3.TTF.HintingFlags.Mono
member.summary: Monochrome hinting adjusts the font for better rendering at lower resolutions.

member.name: F:SDL3.TTF.HintingFlags.None
member.summary: No hinting, the font is rendered without any grid-fitting.

member.name: F:SDL3.TTF.HintingFlags.LightSubpixel
member.summary: Light hinting with subpixel rendering for more precise font edges.

member.name: T:SDL3.TTF.HorizontalAlignment
member.summary: The horizontal alignment used when rendering wrapped text.
member.since: This enum is available since SDL_ttf 3.0.0.

member.name: T:SDL3.TTF.ImageType
member.summary: The type of data in a glyph image
member.since: This enum is available since SDL_ttf 3.0.0.

member.summary: The color channels are white

member.summary: The alpha channel has signed distance field

member.name: M:SDL3.TTF.Init
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL TTF_Init(void);

summary.para: Initialize SDL_ttf.
You must successfully call this function before it is safe to call any other function in this library.
It is safe to call this more than once, and each successful call should be paired with a matching call.

member.returns: >true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_ttf 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.TTF.OpenFont(System.String,System.Single)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC TTF_Font * SDLCALL TTF_OpenFont(const char *file, float ptsize);

summary.para: Create a font from a file, using a specified point size.
Some .fon fonts will have several sizes embedded in the file, so the point size becomes the index of choosing which size. If the value is too high, the last indexed size will be the default.
When done with the returned TTF_Font, use to dispose of it.

member.param: path to font file.
point size to use for the newly-opened font.
member.returns: a valid TTF_Font, or null on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_ttf 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.TTF.OpenFontIO(System.IntPtr,System.Boolean,System.Single)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC TTF_Font * SDLCALL TTF_OpenFontIO(SDL_IOStream *src, bool closeio, float ptsize);

summary.para: Create a font from an SDL_IOStream, using a specified point size.
Some .fon fonts will have several sizes embedded in the file, so the point size becomes the index of choosing which size. If the value is too high, the last indexed size will be the default.
If closeio is true, src will be automatically closed once the font is closed. Otherwise you should keep src open until the font is closed.
When done with the returned TTF_Font, use to dispose of it.

member.param: an SDL_IOStream to provide a font file's data.
true to close src when the font is closed, false to leave it open.
point size to use for the newly-opened font.
member.returns: a valid TTF_Font, or null on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_ttf 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.TTF.OpenFontWithProperties(System.UInt32)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC TTF_Font * SDLCALL TTF_OpenFontWithProperties(SDL_PropertiesID props);

summary.para: Create a font with the specified properties.
These are the supported properties:

list.item: : the font file to open, if an SDL_IOStream isn't being used. This is required if and aren't set.
: an SDL_IOStream containing the font to be opened. This should not be closed until the font is closed. This is required if and aren't set.
: the offset in the iostream for the beginning of the font, defaults to 0.
: true if closing the font should also close the associated SDL_IOStream.

: the point size of the font. Some .fon fonts will have several sizes embedded in the file, so the point size becomes the index of choosing which size. If the value is too high, the last indexed size will be the default.
: the face index of the font, if the font contains multiple font faces.
: the horizontal DPI to use for font rendering, defaults to if set, or 72 otherwise.
: the vertical DPI to use for font rendering, defaults to if set, or 72 otherwise.
: an optional TTF_Font that, if set, if set, will be used as the font data source and the initial size and style of the new font.

member.param: the properties to use.
member.returns: a valid TTF_Font, or null on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_ttf 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.TTF.CopyFont(System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC TTF_Font * SDLCALL TTF_CopyFont(TTF_Font *existing_font);

summary.para: Create a copy of an existing font.
The copy will be distinct from the original, but will share the font file and have the same size and style as the original.
When done with the returned TTF_Font, use to dispose of it.

member.param: the font to copy.
member.returns: a valid TTF_Font, or null on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should be called on the thread that created the original font.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_ttf 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.TTF.GetFontProperties(System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_PropertiesID SDLCALL TTF_GetFontProperties(TTF_Font *font);

summary.para: Get the properties associated with a font.
The following read-write properties are provided by SDL:

list.item: : The FT_Stroker_LineCap value used when setting the font outline, defaults to FT_STROKER_LINECAP_ROUND.
: The FT_Stroker_LineJoin value used when setting the font outline, defaults to FT_STROKER_LINEJOIN_ROUND.
: The FT_Fixed miter limit used when setting the font outline, defaults to 0.

member.param: the font to query.
member.returns: a valid property ID on success or 0 on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_ttf 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.TTF.GetFontGeneration(System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC Uint32 SDLCALL TTF_GetFontGeneration(TTF_Font *font);

The generation is incremented each time font properties change that require rebuilding glyphs, such as style, size, etc.

member.param: the font to query.
member.returns: the font generation or 0 on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should be called on the thread that created the font.

member.name: M:SDL3.TTF.AddFallbackFont(System.IntPtr,System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL TTF_AddFallbackFont(TTF_Font *font, TTF_Font *fallback);

summary.para: Add a fallback font.
Add a font that will be used for glyphs that are not in the current font. The fallback font should have the same size and style as the current font.
If there are multiple fallback fonts, they are used in the order added.
This updates any TTF_Text objects using this font.

member.param: the font to modify.
the font to add as a fallback.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should be called on the thread that created both fonts.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_ttf 3.0.0.

member.since: This function is available since SDL_ttf 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.TTF.SetFontSDF(System.IntPtr,System.Boolean)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool TTF_SetFontSDF(TTF_Font *font, bool enabled);

summary.para: Enable Signed Distance Field rendering for a font.
SDF is a technique that helps fonts look sharp even when scaling and rotating, and requires special shader support for display.
This works with Blended APIs, and generates the raw signed distance values in the alpha channel of the resulting texture.
This updates any objects using this font, and clears already-generated glyphs, if any, from the cache.

member.param: the font to set SDF support on.
true to enable SDF, false to disable.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should be called on the thread that created the font.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_ttf 3.0.0.

member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool TTF_GetFontSDF(const TTF_Font *font);

member.returns: true if enabled, false otherwise.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_ttf 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.TTF.SetFontKerning(System.IntPtr,System.Boolean)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC void SDLCALL TTF_SetFontKerning(TTF_Font *font, bool enabled);

summary.para: Set if kerning is enabled for a font.

Newly-opened fonts default to allowing kerning. This is generally a good policy unless you have a strong reason to disable it, as it tends to produce better rendering (with kerning disabled, some fonts might render the word kerning as something that looks like keming for example).
This updates any objects using this font.

member.param: the font to set kerning on.
true to enable kerning, false to disable.
member.threadsafety: This function should be called on the thread that created the font.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_ttf 3.0.0.

member.param: the font to query.
member.returns: true if kerning is enabled, false otherwise.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_ttf 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.TTF.FontIsFixedWidth(System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL TTF_FontIsFixedWidth(const TTF_Font *font);

summary.para: Query whether a font is fixed-width.

A "fixed-width" font means all glyphs are the same width across; a lowercase 'i' will be the same size across as a capital 'W', for example. This is common for terminals and text editors, and other apps that treat text as a grid. Most other things (WYSIWYG word processors, web pages, etc) are more likely to not be fixed-width in most cases.

member.param: the font to query.
member.returns: true if the font is fixed-width, false otherwise.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_ttf 3.0.0.

member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool TTF_FontIsScalable(const TTF_Font *font);

summary.para: Query whether a font is scalable or not.
Scalability lets us distinguish between outline and bitmap fonts.

member.param: the font to query.
member.returns: true if the font is scalable, false otherwise.
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_ttf 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.TTF.SetFontCharSpacing(System.IntPtr,System.Int32)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL TTF_SetFontCharSpacing(TTF_Font *font, int spacing);
member.summary:
Set additional space in pixels to be applied between any two rendered characters.
The spacing value is applied uniformly after each character, in addition to the normal glyph's advance.
Spacing may be a negative value, in which case it will reduce the distance instead.
This updates any TTF_Text objects using this font.
member.param: the font to specify a direction for.
the new additional glyph spacing for the font.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should be called on the thread that created the font.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_ttf 3.4.0.

member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC int SDLCALL TTF_GetFontCharSpacing(TTF_Font *font);

This defaults to 0 if it hasn't been set.
member.param: the font to query.
member.returns: the character spacing in pixels.
member.threadsafety: This function should be called on the thread that created the font.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_ttf 3.4.0.

summary.para: Calculate the dimensions of a rendered string of UTF-8 text.
This will report the width and height, in pixels, of the space that the specified string will take to fully render.
Text is wrapped to multiple lines on line endings and on word boundaries if it extends beyond wrapWidth in pixels.
If wrap_width is 0, this function will only wrap on newline characters.

member.param: the font to query.
text to calculate, in UTF-8 encoding.
the length of the text, in bytes, or 0 for null terminated text.
the maximum width or 0 to wrap on newline characters.
will be filled with width, in pixels, on return.
will be filled with height, in pixels, on return.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should be called on the thread that created the font.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_ttf 3.0.0.

member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL TTF_MeasureString(TTF_Font *font, const char *text, size_t length, int max_width, int *measured_width, size_t *measured_length);

summary.para: Calculate how much of a UTF-8 string will fit in a given width.
This reports the number of characters that can be rendered before reaching maxWidth.
This does not need to render the string to do this calculation.

member.param: the font to query.
text to calculate, in UTF-8 encoding.
the length of the text, in bytes, or 0 for null terminated text.
maximum width, in pixels, available for the string, or 0 for unbounded width.
a pointer filled in with the width, in pixels, of the string that will fit, may be null.
a pointer filled in with the length, in bytes, of the string that will fit, may be null.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should be called on the thread that created the font.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_ttf 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.TTF.RenderTextSolid(System.IntPtr,System.String,System.UIntPtr,SDL3.SDL.Color)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_Surface * SDLCALL TTF_RenderText_Solid(TTF_Font *font, const char *text, size_t length, SDL_Color fg);

summary.para: Render UTF-8 text at fast quality to a new 8-bit surface.
This function will allocate a new 8-bit, palettized surface. The surface's 0 pixel will be the colorkey, giving a transparent background. The 1 pixel will be set to the text color.
This will not word-wrap the string; you'll get a surface with a single line of text, as long as the string requires. You can use instead if you need to wrap the output to multiple lines.
This will not wrap on newline characters.
You can render at other quality levels with , TTF_RenderText_Blended, and .

member.param: the font to render with.
text to render, in UTF-8 encoding.
the length of the text, in bytes, or 0 for null terminated text.
the foreground color for the text.
member.returns: a new 8-bit, palettized surface, or null if there was an error.
member.threadsafety: This function should be called on the thread that created the font.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_ttf 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.TTF.RenderTextSolidWrapped(System.IntPtr,System.String,System.UIntPtr,SDL3.SDL.Color,System.Int32)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_Surface * SDLCALL TTF_RenderText_Solid_Wrapped(TTF_Font *font, const char *text, size_t length, SDL_Color fg, int wrapLength);

summary.para: Render word-wrapped UTF-8 text at fast quality to a new 8-bit surface.
This function will allocate a new 8-bit, palettized surface. The surface's 0 pixel will be the colorkey, giving a transparent background. The 1 pixel will be set to the text color.
Text is wrapped to multiple lines on line endings and on word boundaries if it extends beyond wrapLength in pixels.
If wrapLength is 0, this function will only wrap on newline characters.
You can render at other quality levels with , , and TTF_RenderText_LCD_Wrapped.

member.param: the font to render with.
text to render, in UTF-8 encoding.
the length of the text, in bytes, or 0 for null terminated text.
the foreground color for the text.
the maximum width of the text surface or 0 to wrap on newline characters.
member.returns: a new 8-bit, palettized surface, or null if there was an error.
member.threadsafety: This function should be called on the thread that created the font.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_ttf 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.TTF.RenderGlyphSolid(System.IntPtr,System.UInt32,SDL3.SDL.Color)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC SDL_Surface * SDLCALL TTF_RenderGlyph_Solid(TTF_Font *font, Uint32 ch, SDL_Color fg);

summary.para: Render a single 32-bit glyph at fast quality to a new 8-bit surface.
This function will allocate a new 8-bit, palettized surface. The surface's 0 pixel will be the colorkey, giving a transparent background. The 1 pixel will be set to the text color.
The glyph is rendered without any padding or centering in the X direction, and aligned normally in the Y direction.
You can render at other quality levels with , , and .

member.param: the font to render with.
the character to render.
the foreground color for the text.
member.returns: a new 8-bit, palettized surface, or null if there was an error.
member.threadsafety: This function should be called on the thread that created the font.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_ttf 3.0.0.

summary.para: Render UTF-8 text at high quality to a new 8-bit surface.

This function will allocate a new 8-bit, palettized surface. The surface's 0 pixel will be the specified background color, while other pixels have varying degrees of the foreground color. This function returns the new surface, or null if there was an error.
This will not word-wrap the string; you'll get a surface with a single line of text, as long as the string requires. You can use instead if you need to wrap the output to multiple lines.
This will not wrap on newline characters.
You can render at other quality levels with , , and .

text to render, in UTF-8 encoding.
the length of the text, in bytes, or 0 for null terminated text.
the foreground color for the text.
the background color for the text.

member.threadsafety: This function should be called on the thread that created the font.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_ttf 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.TTF.GetGPUTextDrawData(System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC TTF_GPUAtlasDrawSequence * SDLCALL TTF_GetGPUTextDrawData(TTF_Text *text);

summary.para: Get the geometry data needed for drawing the text.
text must have been created using a TTF_TextEngine from .

The positive X-axis is taken towards the right and the positive Y-axis is taken upwards for both the vertex and the texture coordinates, i.e, it follows the same convention used by the SDL_GPU API. If you want to use a different coordinate system you will need to transform the vertices yourself.
If the text looks blocky use linear filtering.

member.param: the text to draw.
member.returns: a null terminated linked list of objects or null if the passed text is empty or in case of failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should be called on the thread that created the text.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_ttf 3.0.0.

summary.para: Set the font used by a text object.

When a text object has a font, any changes to the font will automatically regenerate the text. If you set the font to null, the text will continue to render but changes to the font will no longer affect the text.
This function may cause the internal text representation to be rebuilt.

member.param: the to modify.
the font to use, may be null.

member.name: M:SDL3.TTF.SetTextWrapWhitespaceVisible(System.IntPtr,System.Boolean)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL TTF_SetTextWrapWhitespaceVisible(TTF_Text *text, bool visible);

summary.para: Set whether whitespace should be visible when wrapping a text object.

If the whitespace is visible, it will take up space for purposes of alignment and wrapping. This is good for editing, but looks better when centered or aligned if whitespace around line wrapping is hidden. This defaults false.
This function may cause the internal text representation to be rebuilt.

member.param: the to modify.
true to show whitespace when wrapping text, false to hide it.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should be called on the thread that created the text.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_ttf 3.0.0.

member.returns: true if whitespace is shown when wrapping text, or false otherwise.
member.threadsafety: This function should be called on the thread that created the text.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_ttf 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.TTF.GetTextSubString(System.IntPtr,System.Int32,SDL3.TTF.SubString@)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL TTF_GetTextSubString(TTF_Text *text, int offset, TTF_SubString *substring);

summary.para: Get the substring of a text object that surrounds a text offset.

If offset is less than 0, this will return a zero length substring at the beginning of the text with the flag set. If offset is greater than or equal to the length of the text string, this will return a zero length substring at the end of the text with the flag set.

member.param: the to query.
a byte offset into the text string.
a pointer filled in with the substring containing the offset.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should be called on the thread that created the text.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_ttf 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.TTF.GetTextSubStringForLine(System.IntPtr,System.Int32,SDL3.TTF.SubString@)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC bool SDLCALL TTF_GetTextSubStringForLine(TTF_Text *text, int line, TTF_SubString *substring);

summary.para: Get the substring of a text object that contains the given line.

If line is less than 0, this will return a zero length substring at the beginning of the text with the flag set. If `line` is greater than or equal to Text.NumLines this will return a zero length substring at the end of the text with the flag set.

member.param: the to query.
a zero-based line index, in the range [0 .. Text.NumLines-1].
a pointer filled in with the substring containing the offset.
member.returns: true on success or false on failure; call for more information.
member.threadsafety: This function should be called on the thread that created the text.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_ttf 3.0.0.

summary.para: Get the substrings of a text object that contain a range of text.

member.param: the to query.
a byte offset into the text string.
the length of the range being queried, in bytes, or -1 for the remainder of the string.
a pointer filled in with the number of substrings returned, may be null.
member.returns: a null terminated array of substring pointers or null on failure; call for more information. This is a single allocation that should be freed with when it is no longer needed.

member.name: M:SDL3.TTF.CloseFont(System.IntPtr)
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC void SDLCALL TTF_CloseFont(TTF_Font *font);

summary.para: Dispose of a previously-created font.
Call this when done with a font. This function will free any resources associated with it. It is safe to call this function on null, for example on the result of a failed call to .
The font is not valid after being passed to this function. String pointers from functions that return information on this font, such as and , are no longer valid after this call, as well.

member.param: the font to dispose of.
member.threadsafety: This function should not be called while any other thread is using the font.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_ttf 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.TTF.Quit
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC void SDLCALL TTF_Quit(void);

summary.para: Deinitialize SDL_ttf.
You must call this when done with the library, to free internal resources. It is safe to call this when the library isn't initialized, as it will just return immediately.
Once you have as many quit calls as you have had successful calls to , the library will actually deinitialize.

Please note that this does not automatically close any fonts that are still open at the time of deinitialization, and it is possibly not safe to close them afterwards, as parts of the library will no longer be initialized deal with it. A well-written program should call on any open fonts before calling this function!

member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_ttf 3.0.0.

member.name: M:SDL3.TTF.WasInit
member.code: extern SDL_DECLSPEC int SDLCALL TTF_WasInit(void);

summary.para: Check if SDL_ttf is initialized.
This reports the number of times the library has been initialized by a call to , without a paired deinitialization request from .
In short: if it's greater than zero, the library is currently initialized and ready to work. If zero, it is not initialized.
Despite the return value being a signed integer, this function should not return a negative number.

member.returns: the current number of initialization calls, that need to eventually be paired with this many calls to .
member.threadsafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
member.since: This function is available since SDL_ttf 3.0.0.
